A 24-year-old nulliparous woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic in the evening at 34 weeks gestation and complaining of decreased fetal movement. She says she had been at work all day and then came directly to the hospital when she noticed that the baby has not been moving as much as usual. There has been no leaking of amniotic fluid. There are no uterine contractions and also no vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A gravida 2 para 0 woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic reporting that she has not menstruated for the past 2 months. She is afraid of having another stillbirth and asks to be vaccinated against everything. Which of the following vaccines is most appropriate to administer?

A 30-year-old woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic with the inability to conceive despite trying for the past 18 months. Patient menstrual cycles were regular until 2 years ago, and since then she has had irregular vaginal bleeding. There is hair covering the upper lip and chin. A pelvic examination confirms bilaterally enlarged ovaries. DHEA and 17-OH progesterone values are normal. Body mass index 30 kg/m². Which of the following laboratory values is most likely finding?
option4: A Follicle Stimulating Hormone to Leutenizing Hormone ratio of 1:3
correct_option: A Follicle Stimulating Hormone to Leutenizing Hormone ratio of 1:3

question_text: A 20-year-old woman presented to the Maternal Health Clinic with severe facial acne and irregular menstrual bleeding. question_text: A pediatrician is called to attend a caesarean section delivery of 38 week''s gestation baby. On delivery, the neonate does not cry immediately, appears cyanotic and floppy. Heart rate 120 bpm, Breathing: Irregular, gasping, Reflex irritability: Coughs, and cries with stimulation, Activity: Arms and legs flexed, little movement, Appearance: Rosy with blue extremities. A 17-year-old woman comes at 39 weeks gestation to the Emergency Department with no previous antenatal care. She delivered a healthy baby boy normally with no complications reported. Then she was given an injection of RhoGAM. What is the indication of RhoGAM?
option1: Mother is Rh- and the baby is Rh+
option2: Father is RH+ and the baby is Rh-

question_text: A 37-year-old primigravida presented to the clinic with amenorrhea for 12 weeks, hyperemesis gravidarum, and raised human chorionic gonadotropin levels. A 28-year-old woman with mild intermittent asthma presents to the Health Clinic at 24 weeks gestation and says that her symptoms have worsened as the pregnancy progresses. However, she now complains of daily symptoms and once per week symptoms at night time. A forced expiratory volume in 1 second test shows 70% of normal. question_text: A 30-year-old woman is in the second stage of labour for 3 hours by now. She is not on epidural analgesia. question_text: A 22-year-old woman G2 P1001 at 37 weeks gestation presents after fainting and has severe abdominal pain. She is cold and diaphoretic. Fundal height is 39 cm; the cervical os is closed and there is no vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

option4: Give steroid and repeat biophysical profile within 24 hours

question_text: A 19-year-old woman 23-week pregnant primigravida presents with a complaint of irregular brownish patches of varying sizes on face and neck. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

option4: Work up and consultation of a gynecologic oncologist

question_text: A 30-year-old primigravida presents to the Emergency Room complaining of vaginal spotting for the last few days. question_text: A 29-year-old primigravida presented in labor and progressed well and had a mediolateral episiotomy. Which of the following is a theoretical benefit to performing a mediolateral episiotomy compared to a midline episiotomy?

question_text: Patient came for surgery, and during operation, doctor requested the nurse to administer a unit of blood. However, the nurse informed the doctor that the patient had already received a unit of blood as per a previous order. What is this situation called?

A child is admitted to the PICU with a subdural hematoma. The father reports the child fell from his bed. On physical examination, the child has ecchymosis on the buttocks and back and bilateral femoral fractures. What''s the most likely diagnosis?

A female who recently got married has been taking oral contraceptive pills (OCPs) even before her marriage. She wishes to continue taking OCPs, but her husband wants her to stop. What is the most appropriate approach?
option2: Both the patient and her husband should be consented
option3: The patient's consent should be taken after discussion with her husband
correct_option: Both the patient and her husband should be consented

question_text: A patient has recently stopped smoking and has started using nicotine replacement patches. The patient is motivated to quit smoking and is actively taking steps towards achieving that goal. Which stage of smoking cessation is the patient currently in?

A 27-year-old woman at 39 weeks of gestation experiences a complicated delivery due to shoulder dystocia. As a result, the infant develops brachial plexus palsy. What is the best approach to reduce complication rates for shoulder dystocia?
option1: Schedule simulation drills with the entire clinical team at regular intervals
option2: Conduct a debriefing for team members after each adverse event
option3: Perform a root cause analysis after every adverse event
option4: Use a checklist that categorizes shoulder dystocia risk in every patient chart
correct_option: Schedule simulation drills with the entire clinical team at regular intervals

question_text: A critically ill patient is admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU). The medical team is considering a Do Not Resuscitate (DNR) order for the patient. Which of the following statements is true about DNR orders?
option1: DNR orders should be taken by 3 worthy doctors
option2: DNR orders should be discussed with the patient
option3: DNR orders should be discussed with the patient's family
option4: DR orders should be discussed with hospital administrators
correct_option: DNR orders should be taken by 3 worthy doctors

question_text: An 8-year-old girl has sustained a painful open fracture-dislocation that needs immediate treatment following a car accident. Her parents are out of town, and the babysitter is in a different hospital. Which of the following is the best course of action?
option1: Reduce the fracture and complete a thorough evaluation
option2: Do not treat the patient until given verbal consent over the phone by her parents
option3: Obtain verbal consent from the babysitter over the phone
option4: Obtain a court order to treat the patient
correct_option: Reduce the fracture and complete a thorough evaluation

question_text: A patient is experiencing hemiplegia and weakness after a stroke. The patient is currently undergoing physiotherapy. What form of disease prevention is this?

A father of one of your patients, approaches you and asks you to prescribe an oral hypoglycaemic medication for his daughter''s treatment. However, the daughter is currently uninsured. He requests that you put the prescription in his name. What is correct?
option1: Write a prescription in the father's name but inform daughter's PCP physician about her treatment
option2: Write prescription in father's name to prevent complications and suggest daughter gets medical help
option3: Do not write a prescription to his insurance company for intention to commit fraud
option4: Write a prescription for the daughter for a different and less expensive medication
correct_option: Do not write a prescription to his insurance company for intention to commit fraud

question_text: A man with liver problems and a history of alcoholism visits the clinic. After he leaves, his wife approaches you and requests an alcohol lab test because she suspects that his drinking is causing his illness. What is the correct action to be taken?
option1: Do not perform a test for alcohol without the patient's permission
option2: Send the specimen for testing for alcohol but only inform the patient of the result
option3: Tell the patient about his wife's request and ask him about alcohol use
option4: Ask the wife for more information before deciding whether to send the specimen for alcohol testing
correct_option: Send the specimen for testing for alcohol but only inform the patient of the result

question_text: The doctor checks on the labor of his patient on Friday evening. he''s leaving for vacation in a few hours, but an on-call obstetrician will be available for deliveries. The physician recommends a CS. Which of the following principles is demonstrated?

A 57-year-old patient presents with hematemesis, mild varicosity of the distal esophagus, and a bleeding site that was recognized and cauterized. The patient has been a heavy drinker for the past 10 years and asks what caused this. How should you respond?
option1: It's probably a consequence of alcohol consumption causing oesophageal varices
option3: This happened because you are an alcoholic
option4: Let''s get you better first, and then we can discuss your disease in greater detail
correct_option: It's probably a consequence of alcohol consumption causing oesophageal varices

question_text: A woman you performed a tubal ligation on reports well and says shes ready to go home. You suspect that you left a surgical towel in the patient’s abdomen. Closer examination of post-surgery imaging confirms your suspicion. What is the appropriate action?
option1: Contact the hospital's legal team before taking further action
option2: Convince the patient to return to OR for another procedure and remove the towel after she is sedated
option3: Do not inform the patient and allow her to return home to avoid the risk of a second operation
option4: Inform the patient that you forgot to remove a towel and perform a second operation to remove it
correct_option: Inform the patient that you forgot to remove a towel and perform a second operation to remove it

question_text: A 92-year-old woman with likely osteomyelitis and sepsis, she was diagnosed with metastatic squamous cell lung cancer a month prior to admission. The patient will probably require intubation to survive, but she has a living will dated 3 weeks ago that states she does not want any extraordinary measure taken to prolong life. Her daughter just arrived and requests to have everything done for the patient. What is the most appropriate course of action?
option1: Meet with the patient’s daughter and any other family members to discuss their rationale for ignoring her wishes about terminal care
option2: Transfer the patient back to the nursing home
option3: Inform the patient’s daughter that her mother has a living will and that you will not intubate her to prolong her life
option4: Intubate the patient and transfer to ICU
correct_option: Inform the patient’s daughter that her mother has a living will and that you will not intubate her to prolong her life

question_text: A 27-year-old woman presented at full term at labor, she was having uncontrolled diabetes during this pregnancy. Which of the following neonatal complications will most likely increase?
A primipara presents 1 week after delivery. She is tearful, has spells of crying, and lacks appetite and sleep. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old Primigravida at 17 weeks of gestation is known to have epilepsy and is on valproic acid. She was concerned about the possible risks to her baby. Which of the following fetal abnormalities is markedly increased?
A 28-year-old Primigravida at 8 weeks of gestation is known to have chronic hypertension and presented to Antenatal Clinic. Which of the following antihypertensive medications is contraindicated?

A 28-year-old G4 P3 AO at 12 weeks of gestation with a prior history of preeclampsia and fetal growth restriction presented to the Antenatal Clinic. Which of the following will most likely reduce the risk of growth restriction and preeclampsia?

A 22-year-old G2 PO Al at 13 weeks of gestation presented to the Antenatal Clinic. In her first pregnancy, she had a painless fetal passage at 22 weeks. Which of the following interventions is most recommended for the current pregnancy?
option4: Regular antenatal visits with no specific intervention

question_text: A 30-year-old Primigravida is found to have hyperthyroidism during her pregnancy. Which of the following is the most contraindicated?

A 28-year-old Primigravida presented to the Antenatal Clinic for an Ultrasound scan. Ultrasound: Single viable fetus with significant lower levels of amniotic fluid (oligohydramnios). Which of the following is the most likely cause?

A 36-year-old presented to the Antenatal Clinic for an Ultrasound scan. Ultrasound: Single viable fetus with significant increased levels of amniotic fluid (polyhydramnios). Which of the following is the most likely cause?
A pregnant woman with prenatal diagnosis of Rhesus (Rh) alloimmunization had an ultrasound scan during follow-up. Which of the following is the most diagnostic sign?
option4: Low fetal middle cerebral artery (MCA) Doppler peak systolic velocity

question_text: A 23-year-old pregnant woman at 28 weeks of gestation. Ultrasound showed fetal hydrops with high peak systolic velocity more than 1.8 MOM in the middle cerebral artery Doppler. Which of the following is the most likely cause?

A 30-year-old G2 Pl AO Ll at 33 weeks of gestation presented with decreased fetal movement. Obstetric ultrasound was performed. Which of the following is the most reassuring biophysical profile score (BPS)?

During duty in the Labor and Delivery Room, the nurse called to evaluate the cardiotocography of PG 39 weeks in active labor. The nurse asked about the relationship between the cause and type of deceleration. Which of the following combinations is the most likely to occur?
option3: Fetal head compression causes late deceleration
option4: Fetal head compression causes variable deceleration

question_text: A 32-year-old PG at 25 weeks of gestation is complaining of severe left calf pain and swelling. The area of concern is slightly edematous, but no erythema is apparent. Examination demonstrates a positive Homan''s sign. Which of the following is the most appropriate primary mode of diagnosis?
A 28-year-old diabetic on insulin with no significant family history is concerned about pregnancy. Which of the following risks is the same for her as for the general population?
A 30-year-old PG at 30 weeks of gestation with severe preeclampsia complaining of right upper quadrant abdominal pain. Which of the following is the most likely cause?
A 27-year-old G3 P2 AO L2 at 34 weeks of gestation with severe preeclampsia. Which of the following is the rationale behind giving antihypertensive medications?
option4: Decrease risk of maternal complications such as stroke
correct_option: Decrease risk of maternal complications such as stroke

question_text: A 30-year-old G4 P3 AO L3 at 34 weeks of gestation with severe preeclampsia. Which of the following is most likely to be decreased significantly?

A pregnant woman with preeclampsia has just delivered and has a lax uterus with moderate postpartum hemorrhage. Physical examination reveals no laceration or retained product of conception. Which of the following medical therapies is most recommended?
option4: 20 units oxytocin in a 500 mL of D5W given intravenously
correct_option: 20 units oxytocin in a 500 mL of D5W given intravenously

question_text: A 30-year-old primigravida at 30 weeks of gestation newly diagnosed with preeclampsia. Which of the following antihypertensives is contraindicated?
A 22-year-old PG at 33 weeks of gestation newly diagnosed with gestational diabetes. Which of the following is the most appropriate first-line therapy?

A 21-year-old G2 Pl AO Ll at 18 weeks of gestation presented to the obstetric clinic for the first time. The obstetrician asked for an oral glucose tolerance test. Which of the following is the most likely indication for this early request?
option4: History of gestational diabetes in the previous pregnancy
correct_option: History of gestational diabetes in the previous pregnancy

question_text: A 23-year-old PG at 28 weeks of gestation known case of tricuspid regurgitation complaining of slight limitation of physical activity. What is her heart failure class based on the New York Heart Association classification?

A 22-year-old pregnant woman at 40 weeks of gestation was diagnosed with DVT and started on a therapeutic dose of heparin. The patient had a normal vaginal delivery. Shortly after a heparin injection, she sustained postpartum hemorrhage. The uterus was contracted and examination revealed no lacerations. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 32-year-old woman, primigravida, presented with shortness of breath during an antenatal visit. She was diagnosed with cardiac valve disease. Which of the following is associated with the worst outcome?
A 23-year-old primigravida admitted for induction of labor. The cervix is closed and 3.5 cm long. Which of the following is the most appropriate agent to be given to her for cervical ripening?

A 28-year-old G4 P3 AO at 40 weeks of gestation presented with irregular contractions. She was admitted to the Labor and Delivery Ward. After how many hours will the latent phase be considered prolonged?
A patient presents at 32 weeks of gestation with sudden vaginal bleeding of 700 cc. Which of the following is this type of bleeding?

A 22-year-old PG at 39 weeks of gestation admitted to the Labor Room with active labor. Initial cervical examination revealed 5 cm dilatation with 60% effacement and station was at -2. After 6 hours despite regular adequate contractions, abdominal examination revealed the presence of a retraction ring and vaginal examination showed cervical dilatation of 7 cm and station of -1. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old PG presents at 34 weeks of pregnancy with constant abdominal pain and moderate bleeding. Her symphysiofundal height is 37 cm, and her uterus is tender and firm. CTG: Unprovoked decelerations. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 29-year-old woman, G2, Pl, at 38 weeks of gestation comes to the Labor and Delivery Ward with frequent painful contractions. Her prenatal course was significant for a urine culture that showed 100,000 colony-forming units/milliliter of Group-B streptococci and bronchial asthma, for which she uses a Ventolin inhaler. Which of the following medications should this patient be treated with during labor and delivery?

A 30-year-old G4 P3 at 30 weeks of gestation presents with severe pain in the right flank radiating to her groin. She also complains of rigors and chills. Urine analysis: Numerous pus cells. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 23-year-old G2 P1 at 40 weeks of gestation presented to the Emergency Room complaining of painful uterine contractions. Vaginal examination revealed cervical dilatation of 3 cm and 60% effacement. Upon admission to the Labor Room, she had a gush of clear fluid. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 22-year-old PG at 30 weeks of gestation presented to the Emergency Room complaining of lower abdominal pain. Vaginal examination revealed cervical dilatation of 2 cm with 50% effacement. She was admitted to the Labor and Delivery Room. The consultant on duty decided to start her on oxytocin antagonist (atosiban) as a tocolytic. Which of the following is the rationale for using tocolytic therapy in this case?
option3: Prevent preterm premature rupture of membranes
option4: Delay labor to gain the maximum benefit from steroids
correct_option: Delay labor to gain the maximum benefit from steroids

question_text: A 39-year-old G4 P3 at 12 weeks of gestation came for an ultrasound study. Ultrasound: Single viable fetus with cystic hygroma. Which of the following is the most appropriate step in management to be offered at this gestational age?
A 32-year-old G2 Pi AO at 12 weeks of gestation. She is a known case of IDDM for 12 years and attended the Antenatal Clinic. Which of the following will most likely increase markedly in this patient?

A 28-year-old G2 P1 AO at 33 weeks of twin gestation presented to the Antenatal Clinic. Her investigations confirmed iron deficiency anemia. Which of the following is an indication for intravenous iron therapy?
A 25-year-old primigravida presents at 15 weeks of gestation to the antenatal visit where blood and urine investigations were requested. Which of the following is the most expected finding?

A 20-year-old nulliparous healthy married woman presents to the clinic requesting contraception. She has a history of menorrhagia and occasional dysmenorrhea. No family history of note. Which of the following is the most appropriate contraception method?

A 26-year-old average-size woman has delivered a baby boy within the last 24 hours. During the morning round in the post-partum ward, it was found that her blood group is B-, her husband is O+, and her son is A+. A 300-g dose of anti-D immunoglobulin was prescribed. Which of the following volume of fetal blood will be protected by the prescribed dose?

A 24-year-old primigravida at her 11th week of pregnancy presents with a history of lethargy, constipation, and intolerance to cold temperature. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 36-year-old woman G2 P1 AO L1 at 32 weeks gestation presents for routine prenatal care. Past medical history is unremarkable. Current pregnancy has been uncomplicated, with occasional Braxton Hicks contractions and increasing non-pruritic vaginal discharge like cervical mucus. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 26-year-old woman primigravida tested positive on a home pregnancy test. Her last menstrual period was 36 days ago, and she usually has a 30-day cycle. Three weeks ago, she received a rubella vaccine and was advised not to become pregnant for 2 months after the vaccine. Which of the following is the most likely outcome?
option3: Intrauterine fetal death in the third trimester
option4: Spontaneous abortion within the first trimester

question_text: A 38-year-old primigravida at 35 weeks gestation complains of a mild headache and facial edema. Her reflexes are brisk, and she is suspected of having preeclampsia. Which of the following is likely to show in the urinalysis?
A 40-year-old G4 P30Q3 at 32 weeks' gestation presented to the hospital with right upper quadrant pain. Which of the following is the most likely cause of her pain?

A 70-year-old married woman presents with a large prolapse. She has no other significant history except an abdominal hysterectomy at the age of 48 for heavy periods. Examination shows vaginal vault prolapse extending beyond the introitus and a very deficient perineum with a large vaginal opening. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 40-year-old G9 P6026 at 9 weeks' gestation presents with vaginal spotting and mild abdominal pain. Ultrasound shows a 5-week gestational size and no fetal heart detected. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 26-year-old woman presented with insomnia, fatigue, and sadness. She delivered her first child 5 days ago. Examination reveals a tearful and depressed appearance. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A 44-year-old P6006 woman had second-degree uterine descent and moderate cystorectocele. Which of the following is the most appropriate choice of management?
A 49-year-old morbidly obese woman presents with a history of pelvic organ prolapse. Her BMI is 48 kg/m². Which of the following is the most expected type of prolapse in her case?

A 38-year-old primigravida at 33 weeks' gestation is diagnosed with small for gestational age fetus and reversed end-diastolic flow on umbilical artery Doppler. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option3: Antenatal steroids and delivery within 1 week
option4: Repeat ultrasound growth assessment in 2 weeks
correct_option: Antenatal steroids and delivery within 1 week

question_text: A 23-year-old primigravida is admitted with threatened preterm labor at 30 weeks' gestation and has received antenatal corticosteroids. Which of the following is the rationale behind corticosteroid administration in this case?

A 30-year-old G3 P0020 at 17 weeks gestation is undergoing serial ultrasound assessments of cervical length due to a history of a previous second-trimester abortion. Which of the following features will indicate cervical cerclage before 24 weeks gestation?

A 26-year-old primigravida at 23 weeks' gestation presents for an ultrasound scan after a small amount of vaginal bleeding. The scan shows a viable fetus and a cervical length of 20 mm. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 27-year-old primigravida with a low-risk pregnancy is admitted at 32 weeks with antepartum hemorrhage and diagnosed with placental abruption. Bleeding settled with conservative management. Which of the following is the most appropriate antenatal care plan?
option2: Reassure and continue with 'low risk'" care"
option3: Reclassify as 'High Risk'" pregnancy care with serial ultrasound for fetal growth"
option4: Reassure and continue with 'low risk'" care and serial ultrasound for signs of bleeding"
correct_option: Reclassify as 'High Risk'" pregnancy care with serial ultrasound for fetal growth"

question_text: A 19-year-old primigravida is referred at 32 weeks' gestation due to raised blood pressure. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option3: Admit for 6-hourly blood pressure monitoring and 24-hour urine protein
option4: Discharge back to her family physician with advice for daily blood pressure and urine protein

question_text: A 20-year-old primigravida at 8 weeks'' gestation is known to have sickle cell disease, while her husband has normal hemoglobin. She has not taken any vitamin supplements so far. Which of the following is the most appropriate folic acid supplement to be prescribed?

A 22-year-old primigravida with Type 1 diabetes presents at 32 weeks' gestation with regular painful contractions and a closed cervix. Which of the following is the most appropriate antenatal management?
option4: Steroids, additional insulin, and tocolytic agent
correct_option: Steroids, additional insulin, and tocolytic agent

question_text: A 40-year-old nulliparous woman attends a preconception counseling session after being treated for breast carcinoma. She plans a pregnancy. How long after completion of treatment is it recommended to wait before conceiving?
A 30-year-old woman with known sickle cell trait attends an antenatal booking clinic at 8 weeks' gestation. Which of the following antenatal complications is significantly more common in her case?

A 36-year-old G4 P3003 is admitted in preterm labor at 36+5 weeks with polyhydramnios. Fetal bradycardia is noted after spontaneous rupture of membranes. Which of the following needs to be excluded by a prompt vaginal examination?

A 36-year-old G3 P2002 with 2 previous caesarean sections is diagnosed with major placenta previa and placenta accreta at 32 weeks' gestation. When is an elective caesarean section recommended for delivery?

A 40-year-old G4 P2012 at 41+5 weeks had undergone induction of labor by prostaglandin in the last 6 hours. She suddenly developed vaginal bleeding and shock. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 34-year-old healthy woman presents with recurrent symptoms, including headache, anxiety, and mastalgia, that start 8-10 days before menstruation and improve afterward. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 26-year-old woman primigravida at 16 weeks' gestation presents to the clinic complaining of nausea and vomiting. Which of the following is the most likely cause of this condition?

A 30-year-old G4 P3003 woman at 39 weeks'' gestation delivered a baby boy weighing 30.5 kg. Upon delivery of her placenta, an inverted uterus was noted and successfully reduced. Which placental site was the most likely predisposing factor?
A 25-year-old woman underwent a normal vaginal delivery at term. After 30 minutes, the placenta is still in place. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?
option4: Attempt manual extraction of the placenta
correct_option: Attempt manual extraction of the placenta

question_text: A 37-year-old woman G4 P3003 at 40 weeks' gestation presents in labor and progresses to vaginal delivery. Placenta delivery is complicated with inverted uterus and 1200 cc of vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is most likely the cause of her postpartum bleeding?
option3: Inadequate effect of the myometrial contractions
option4: Local coagulopathy of the endometrium and myometrium
correct_option: Inadequate effect of the myometrial contractions

question_text: A 25-year-old primigravida at 35 weeks' gestation presents with spontaneous rupture of membranes and undergoes augmentation of labor with parenteral oxytocin. Fetal monitoring shows distress. Which of the following maneuvers will most likely improve fetal oxygenation?
option2: Ceasing the oxytocin to improve placental perfusion
option3: Placing epidural analgesia to improve utero-placental perfusion
option4: Initiating morphine sulfate to improve pain and hence placental perfusion
correct_option: Ceasing the oxytocin to improve placental perfusion

question_text: A 27-year-old woman primigravida has severe uterine bleeding that exceeds 700 cc after a normal vaginal delivery which does not respond to medical treatment. The obstetrician in charge decided to go for surgical management. The patient desires future pregnancies. Which of the following structures is the most appropriate to ligate in her case in order to stop the bleeding?

A 30-year-old GS P3013 woman presented in labor at term. She had a vaginal delivery complicated by abruption placenta. She was then noted to have significant uterine bleeding. The team in charge noted she had bleeding from multiple venipuncture sites. Which of the following is the best next step in management?

A 25-year-old G2 POO1O at 33 weeks' gestation referred to the hospital with a BP of 160/110 mmHg on a routine appointment. Her BP at her first booking visit at 8 weeks was 124/70 mmHg with trace proteinuria. Further detailed history revealed that she was also complaining of headaches with no history of migraines. She was started on antihypertensives and magnesium sulfate along with steroid injections (see lab results). Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option1: Bed rest to term and spontaneous labor
option3: Bed rest to 37 weeks' gestation and induction of labor
option4: Immediate induction of labor for anticipated vaginal delivery
correct_option: Immediate induction of labor for anticipated vaginal delivery

question_text: A 35-year-old G9 P6026 at 8 weeks' gestation presents to the hospital with vaginal spotting associated with very mild lower abdominal pain. She has noticed some dark brown clots. Examination reveals she is comfortable in bed with no abdominal tenderness appreciated. Pelvic examination reveals minimal dark-red clots in the introitus and cervical os closed (see report). Transvaginal ultrasound: 5-week gestational size with no fetal heart. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step of management plan?

A 27-year-old G4 P2103 woman presents at 37 weeks'' gestation with moderately severe vaginal bleeding (see image and report). Ultrasound: 32 weeks'' gestation, with low-lying placenta covering part of the cervix. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option4: Digital examination to assess the cervix

question_text: A 23-year-old woman primigravida presents at 27 weeks' gestation with vaginal bleeding. She undergoes a transvaginal ultrasound showing a placenta previa. Which of the following is the most likely associated feature?
option4: Profuse first episode of vaginal bleeding

question_text: A 32-year-old nulligravid healthy woman presents for a routine visit. Physical examination is entirely normal. She is a heavy cigarette smoker. She undergoes colposcopy and cervical biopsies (see reports). Pap smear: High grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL). Cervical biopsies: Squamous cell carcinoma in situ (CIS). After counseling about her condition, she wants to preserve her fertility if her status allows. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 42-year-old P3003 healthy woman presents with heavy prolonged vaginal bleeding over the past 7 months. Her periods have become longer and progressively heavier, with the last one occurring 15 days ago. Pelvic examination reveals a small amount of blood in the vaginal vault, normal-sized uterus, and mobile ovaries. (See reports.) Pelvic ultrasound: Endometrium 13 mm and a 2.2 cm x 1 cm lesion in the uterine cavity. Endometrial biopsy: Normal histology. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option3: Magnetic resonance imaging of the uterus
option4: Evaluate hemoglobin level and coagulopathy screening

question_text: A 25-year-old primigravida at 34 weeks' gestation presents with generalized pruritus and yellowish discoloration of the sclera and skin. Which of the following is the most important investigation?
option2: Bile acids and liver function tests
correct_option: Bile acids and liver function tests

question_text: A 42-year-old P3003 healthy woman presents with a history of heavy and prolonged vaginal bleeding over the past 7 months. Her periods have become longer and progressively heavier. Her LMP was 15 days ago. Pelvic examination reveals a small amount of blood in the vaginal vault, a normal-sized uterus, and mobile ovaries. Pelvic ultrasound: 2.5 cm x 1.5 cm lesion in the uterine cavity. Which of the following is the most likely cause of bleeding?

A 58-year-old woman P2002 presents with a history of heaviness in the pelvis, discomfort, and abdominal distension over the last 6 months. Examination reveals a distended abdomen with significant discomfort on palpation and a large abdominopelvic mass. (See report.) Ultrasound and CT: A 12 cm left ovarian mass, ascites, and thickening of the peritoneum. Which of the following is the mainstay of treatment for the suspected diagnosis?

A 24-year-old woman primigravida undergoes an uneventful vaginal delivery at term. After placental delivery, she has 1000 cc of vaginal bleeding. She is alert, and her vital signs are stable, with the uterus noted to be firm. Which of the following is the most common cause?

A 39-year-old G8 P5126 at 38 weeks'' gestation presents for induction of labor due to decreased fetal movement. She is started on oxytocin and begins having contractions. After several hours of labor, her cervix dilates to 6 cm, but she then develops a fever and fetal tachycardia. A diagnosis of chorioamnionitis is entertained. She delivers a baby boy vaginally 4 hours later, but afterward, there is a large continuous vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most likely cause of her bleeding?
A 30-year-old woman had a spontaneous vaginal delivery for a term twin pregnancy 2 hours ago. Now she has active vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most likely cause of her bleeding?
A 38-year-old woman, known for low-lying placenta, delivered a normal vaginal delivery. One hour post-delivery, she had postpartum hemorrhage. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 24-year-old woman just delivered her first baby with a birth weight of 2.7 kg while she was at 38 weeks of gestation. She is not known to have any medical or surgical history. She has a heavy gush of bleeding postpartum. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 50-year-old woman (P5005) presents to the clinic complaining of urine leakage while coughing and sneezing. She denies any frequency, dysuria, or urgency. Which of the following is the most likely finding on physical examination?

A 64-year-old otherwise healthy woman presents with a history of constipation and difficulty with defecation. She reports using her fingers to push her vagina backward to pass bowel motions. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 58-year-old woman (P6006) presents with a 2-month history of pelvic heaviness and a sensation of lower pelvic pressure. Examination reveals vulvar atrophy, mucosal bulging through the introitus, and the rest of the pelvic examination is within normal limits. Which of the following is the most likely underlying etiology of this woman''s complaint?

A 44-year-old woman (P5005) presents to the clinic with an uncomfortable sensation in the vagina for the last year. She reports pelvic pressure associated with urinary frequency but no urinary leaks or irregular menstruation. Obstetric history includes 5 term spontaneous vaginal deliveries. She works in loading and unloading heavy boxes. Examination reveals a large bulging of the anterior vaginal wall. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 54-year-old menopausal woman presents with a history of involuntary urine leaks with urgency. The complaint occurs at any time during the day or night, without warning. There is no clear association between leaks and any specific activity, with occasional association with coughing and laughing. Physical examination is unremarkable. Urinalysis and culture are negative. Urodynamic study shows spontaneous bladder contractions even with small bladder filling. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 62-year-old postmenopausal woman (P4014), known diabetic and with congestive heart failure, is referred by a Cardiology Clinic. She has occasional incontinence with coughing and sneezing. During the interview, she also complains of lower abdominal heaviness and vaginal bulging. Pelvic examination reveals second-degree pelvic prolapse. Which of the following is the recommended treatment for this woman?

A 45-year-old healthy woman (P4004) presents with a complaint of urine leaks when she laughs, coughs, or sneezes. She denies any history of frequency, dysuria, nocturia, or hematuria. She does not report urge voiding when hearing running water. Her symptoms are embarrassing and inconvenient. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial therapy?

A 13-year-old healthy girl is brought by her mother with concerns about not yet starting menstruation. Examination reveals sparse hair covering the labia majora, not extending onto the mons pubis. She has developed breast buds and papilla and areolar enlargement, with no separation in contour between the breast and areola. Which Tanner stage would this girl be assigned to?

A 25-year-old pregnant woman presented with abdominal cramps and mild vaginal bleeding. Examination revealed an open cervix with mild bleeding. Ultrasound: A 7-week viable intrauterine pregnancy. Which of the following is the most likely type of abortion she had?

A 30-year-old woman is referred by a medical clinic with a complaint of vulvar pain. Examination reveals one ulcer on the left lower labia majora. The lesion has a ragged edge, necrotic base, and palpable adenopathy on the left inguinal region. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 36-year-old woman (G4 P2103) at 23 weeks' gestation presents with a primary attack of herpes simplex virus infection. Acyclovir is given orally for 10 days. Which of the following is the rationale for acyclovir treatment?
option3: Decrease the herpes simplex virus infection
option4: Decrease viral shedding and current infection duration

question_text: A 35-year-old woman underwent an uneventful suction evacuation procedure for a suspected hydatidiform mole. The human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) level prior to the procedure was taken (see lab result and report). Histopathology: Confirmed the diagnosis of hydatidiform mole. option1: Weekly beta-hCG until 3 negative readings are obtained
option2: Monthly beta-hCG until negative, then weekly for 6 months
option3: Weekly beta-hCG until negative, then monthly for 6 months
option4: Monthly beta-hCG until 3 negative readings are obtained
correct_option: Weekly beta-hCG until negative, then monthly for 6 months

question_text: A 35-year-old woman underwent suction curettage for a molar pregnancy, and her beta-hCG level was 70,000 mIU/mL prior to the procedure. She was followed up with weekly beta-hCG levels for 5 weeks: 1st week post-evacuation: 576 mIU/mL 2nd week post-evacuation: 350 mIU/mL 3rd week post-evacuation: 180 mIU/mL 4th week post-evacuation: 70 mIU/mL 5th week post-evacuation: 1 mIU/mL Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 35-year-old woman presents with unusual vaginal bleeding and a fleshy vaginal mass. She underwent a suction evacuation for hydatidiform mole. Her beta-hCG level was 870,000 mIU/mL prior to the procedure. She was followed with weekly beta-hCG levels for 5 weeks, with the following results: 1st week: 576 mIU/mL 2nd week: 350 mIU/mL 3rd week: 180 mIU/mL 4th week: 70 mIU/mL 5th week: 300 mIU/mL Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?
option3: Perform a biopsy to confirm the diagnosis
option4: Perform a metastatic workup without biopsy
correct_option: Perform a metastatic workup without biopsy

question_text: A 29-year-old woman presents to the clinic with 3-month postpartum abnormal vaginal bleeding. An examination reveals a fleshy vaginal mass protruding from the cervix that bleeds on touch (see report). Ultrasound: A mass protruding from the cervix, involving the uterine cavity. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?
A 34-year-old is inquiring about an effective reversible method of contraception. Which of the following is the most effective method of contraception?

A 20-year-old primigravida woman at 7 weeks' gestation is diagnosed with a 4-cm stable right tubal ectopic pregnancy. She has no clinical symptoms. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

question_text: A 33-year-old patient presents with vaginal spotting for the last 3 days. She is 7 weeks pregnant, and the transvaginal scan does not reveal an intrauterine or extrauterine gestational sac. Which of the following is the most appropriate next action?

question_text: A 17-year-old primigravida presents to the clinic for the first time after missing her period, with a positive home pregnancy test. Her last menstrual period was 5 weeks ago. A 17-year-old primigravida presents to the clinic for the first time after missing her period, LMP 9 weeks ago, and with a positive home pregnancy test. Pelvic examination reveals a closed cervical os with no bleeding (see report). Transvaginal Ultrasound: An intrauterine gestational sac with a crown-rump length of 9 weeks'' gestation with no embryonic heartbeat. Which of the following is the most likely type of abortion?

A 34-year-old woman presents at 10 weeks of gestation to the Emergency Department with fetal death, without expulsion of any fetal tissue for at least 8 weeks. Which of the following is the most likely type of abortion?

Transvaginal Ultrasound: An intrauterine gestational sac with a crown-rump length corresponding to 9 weeks' gestation with a positive heartbeat. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 17-year-old primigravida presents to the Emergency Department complaining of vaginal bleeding and abdominal cramping. She has a positive home pregnancy test, and her last menstrual period was 9 weeks ago. Pelvic examination reveals a closed cervix with bleeding noted through (see report). Transvaginal Ultrasound: Intrauterine gestational sac with a crown-rump length corresponding to 9 weeks'' gestation with a positive heartbeat. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment plan?
option3: Reassure and evaluate after 1 week's duration
option4: Admit, stabilize, and prepare for possible termination of pregnancy
correct_option: Admit, stabilize, and prepare for possible termination of pregnancy

question_text: A 17-year-old primigravida presents to the Emergency Department complaining of mild vaginal bleeding and abdominal cramping. Her last period was 9 weeks ago, and she has a positive home pregnancy test (see report). Transvaginal Ultrasonography: Intrauterine gestational sac with a crown-rump length corresponding to 9 weeks'' gestation with a positive heartbeat. Which of the following would be the most appropriate next step?

A 17-year-old primigravida presents to the Emergency Department complaining of mild vaginal bleeding and abdominal cramping. She missed her period along with a positive home pregnancy test, and her LMP was 6 weeks ago. Pelvic examination reveals an open cervical os with mild vaginal bleeding through the cervix. Which of the following would be the most appropriate next step?
option4: Reassure the patient and evaluate in 10 days

question_text: A 19-year-old primigravida presents to the Emergency Department complaining of abdominal cramps and mild vaginal bleeding. Her LMP was 6 weeks ago, with a positive home pregnancy test. Pelvic examination reveals a closed cervical os with abdominal pain on manipulation of the cervix. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?
option3: Perform a CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis

question_text: A 35-year-old woman presents to the Emergency Room with sudden severe abdominal pain for the last three hours, sharp and radiating to the right shoulder. A 24-year-old woman presents to the clinic with an incidental finding of a uterine fibroid. Her menstrual cycle is regular, and her examination is unremarkable. Ultrasound: An anterior subserosal fibroid of 3 x 2 cm, otherwise the other areas of the uterus, ovaries, and adnexa are unremarkable. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 32-year-old single woman is attending the clinic complaining of menorrhagia for the last few months. The physical examination is unremarkable. Pelvic Ultrasound: Presence of a serosal fibroid, 4 x 4 cm in size. Otherwise, the other parameters of the ultrasound were normal. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 50-year-old woman presents with menorrhagia for the last year. The physical examination is unremarkable. A trial of medical treatment failed to control the bleeding for the last few months. Ultrasound: Presence of a fibroid, 7 x 7 cm in size, which is distorting the endometrial lining. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 25-year-old woman has been seeking fertility for the last 3 years. She had 2 unsuccessful in vitro fertilization trials. Pelvic Ultrasound: Multiple uterine fibroids of variable sizes. Which of the following will most likely increase her chance of reaching a successful pregnancy?
option4: GnRH injection to reduce the size of the fibroids

question_text: A 60-year-old woman is attending the clinic complaining of post-menopausal bleeding for the last 3 months. She was found to have a uterine fibroid of 5 cm in size, noted in a recent ultrasound. Upon review of the previous medical records, the ultrasound performed 5 years ago showed the same size and location of the fibroid. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 25-year-old G2P1 woman at 39 weeks of gestation presents in the active phase of labor. Cardiotocography shows fetal tachycardia with a baseline of 180 beats per minute, with accelerations, early deceleration, and good variability. Which of the following has the highest alarming sign?
Which of the following interventions is the best to use with pregnant women who smoke?

A 30-year-old G3P2 woman presents with mild abdominal pain at 34 weeks of gestation. Cardiotocography shows a baseline of 140 beats per minute, good variability, accelerations were present with no decelerations. The examination is unremarkable, and the cervix was found to be 1 cm dilated and uneffaced. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 38-year-old G4P3 woman at 35 weeks of gestation is being observed in the labor and delivery room as she is complaining of decreased fetal movement. Cardiotocography shows a baseline of 130 beats per minute, accelerations were seen, no apparent deceleration with poor variability. After one hour of observation with intravenous hydration and continuous CTG monitoring, CTG improved with good variability. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option4: Discharge home with fetal kick chart
correct_option: Discharge home with fetal kick chart

question_text: A 23-year-old primigravida presents to the Emergency Room at 35 weeks of gestation complaining of decreased fetal movement for the last few hours. Examination revealed the cervix is 1 cm dilated and the head is at -2 station. Cardiotocography shows that the baseline is 160 beats per minute with good accelerations, good variability, and early decelerations. The uterine contractions are mild and regular. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 23-year-old primigravida presents with mild, infrequent abdominal pains at 36 weeks of gestation. Cardiotocography shows that the baseline is 140 beats per minute with good accelerations and variability and no decelerations. The uterine contractions are irregular and mild. The examination is unremarkable with no cervical dilation. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 27-year-old G4P3 woman is seen in labor at 39 weeks of gestation. Examination revealed the cervix is 5 cm dilated with clear amniotic fluid draining. Cardiotocography shows that the baseline is 140 beats per minute, no accelerations, good variability, and no decelerations. The uterine contractions are regular and strong, occurring at a rate of 3 to 4 in 10 minutes. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option4: Reassurance and reassessment in 30 to 60 minutes
correct_option: Reassurance and reassessment in 30 to 60 minutes

question_text: A 32-year-old G2P1 woman at 41 weeks of gestation presents in labor. Examination revealed the cervix is 7 cm dilated with meconium-stained amniotic fluid draining, and the head is at -2 station. Cardiotocography shows that the baseline is 100 beats per minute, with poor variability, no accelerations, and no decelerations. The uterine contractions are regular and strong, occurring at a rate of 3 to 4 in 10 minutes. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 19-year-old primigravida is diagnosed with intrauterine growth restriction in the antenatal follow-up. She came in labor at 38 weeks of gestation, the cervix is found to be 4 cm dilated with meconium-stained amniotic fluid. The cardiotocography shows that the baseline is 130 beats per minute, with no accelerations, poor variability, and recurrent late decelerations. The uterine contractions are regular and strong, occurring at a rate of 3 to 4 in 10 minutes. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 32-year-old woman G3P2 at 41 weeks of gestation is seen in the outpatient clinic. Abdominal palpation revealed there are no uterine contractions. Pelvic examination revealed that the cervix is thick and not dilated. The cardiotocography shows that the baseline is 130 beats per minute, with accelerations, good variability, and no decelerations. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 28-year-old woman G3P2 at 38 weeks of gestation is in active labor. Examination revealed the cervix is found to be 6 cm dilated. The cardiotocography is showing that the baseline is 120 beats per minute, absent accelerations and variability with recurrent late decelerations. Uterine contractions are strong, occurring at a rate of 3 to 4 every minute. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A 23-year-old patient at 38 weeks of gestation is in active labor and diagnosed with cord prolapse. Which of the following is the most expected finding on CTG?

A 30-year-old woman delivered a 4.2 kg baby without an episiotomy being required. The placenta was completely delivered by continuous cord traction. Post-delivery, the patient started to have a sudden gush of heavy vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most appropriate next action?
option4: Request blood for complete blood count

question_text: A 33-year-old G3P2 woman presents with mild uterine contractions for 12 hours. Examination revealed fundal height corresponds to gestational age, cephalic presentation, and mild contractions. Pelvic assessment shows the cervix is 4 cm dilated, and the head is at -1 station. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 21-year-old healthy primigravida is at 40 weeks in active labor. Pelvic assessment reveals that the cervix is 7 cm dilated, draining clear amniotic fluid, and the head is at +1 station. She is contracting regularly, 3 contractions in every 10 minutes, and the CTG is showing decelerations. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 30-year-old woman G3P2 is at 37 weeks of gestation. She is a case of Rh isoimmunization and the fetus is diagnosed with fetal anemia. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 35-year-old woman, G3P2 diabetic patient at 38 weeks of gestation, is planned to have induction of labor. She has no abdominal or pelvic pain, and no vaginal loss. Examination revealed the uterine size corresponds to gestational age, soft, and not tender. Pelvic examination shows the cervix is closed and uneffaced. CTG is done. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 33-year-old woman G5P4 with a history of 4 previous cesarean sections presents at 37 weeks of gestation to the Emergency Room complaining of contractions that started 8 hours ago. The pain progressed to become severe with moderate vaginal bleeding, and low blood pressure. She became restless and hyperventilated. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman is seeking fertility assistance and had undergone investigations that revealed that ovulation is regular and her husband's semen analysis is adequate. Which of the following is required to complete the assessment?

A 26-year-old woman presents with amenorrhea for the last 14 weeks. She started to have mild vaginal bleeding for the last 3 days. Examination revealed the uterine size is 18 weeks and is doughy on palpation. Ultrasound: An enlarged uterus with a snowstorm appearance. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 25-year-old woman is seen in the infertility clinic as a case of primary infertility for 2 years. Her history revealed that she has had amenorrhea for the last 6 months and only gets menstruation through using withdrawal bleeding pills. She had investigations repeated twice, performed 3 months apart, and they were all showing the same results. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 40-year-old woman presents with menorrhagia for the last 6 months. She underwent an endometrial biopsy. Endometrial biopsy: Presence of simple endometrial hyperplasia without atypia. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 50-year-old woman presented to the clinic with prolonged and heavy menstruation for the last 8 months. She underwent an endometrial biopsy. Endometrial biopsy: Presence of endometrial hyperplasia with atypia. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 70-year-old woman presented with post-menopausal bleeding for the last 7 months. Endometrial biopsy: Endometrial hyperplasia with atypia. She is unfit for surgery and anesthesia. Which of the following is the most appropriate alternative management?

A 24-year-old woman is admitted to the labor and delivery unit at 37 weeks of gestation in labor. She is pregnant with monochorionic monoamniotic twins, and the first twin is in breech presentation while the second is in cephalic presentation. Examination revealed the cervix is 4 cm dilated. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option4: External cephalic version followed by vaginal delivery

question_text: A 24-year-old woman has been trying to get pregnant for the last 18 months. She was recently diagnosed with polycystic ovarian syndrome and has irregular menstrual cycles. Her husband''s semen analysis was normal, and she has not received any medication. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option4: Oral contraceptives to regulate the menstrual cycle

question_text: A 30-year-old woman at 36 weeks of gestation presents with painless vaginal bleeding for one day. Which of the following is the most appropriate next action?
A 50-year-old woman had a recent miscarriage and is inquiring if her age contributed to the miscarriage. Which of the following is the most appropriate response?
option2: Age is not related to miscarriage
option4: 80% chance of miscarriage due to her age

question_text: A 23-year-old woman presents at 18 weeks of gestation with a sudden unexpected rupture of membranes followed by painless expulsion of products of conception. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman in her mid-trimester of pregnancy presents with a sudden unexpected rupture of membranes followed by painless expulsion of the products of conception. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old otherwise healthy woman presents to the clinic complaining of amenorrhea for the last 18 weeks. Her periods prior to this were all regular. Ultrasound: Presence of a snowstorm pattern within the uterine cavity. Which of the following is the most appropriate test to reach the diagnosis?

A 26-year-old woman was treated with methotrexate for an ectopic pregnancy. Her BhCG results are as follows: Baseline test was 3000 miu/ml on the day of the injection, Day 4: BhCG was 3100 miu/ml, Day 7: BhCG was 2900 miu/ml. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option3: Administer a multiple dose regimen of methotrexate
option4: Counsel for the possibility of administering a second methotrexate dose
correct_option: Counsel for the possibility of administering a second methotrexate dose

question_text: A 32-year-old known case of secondary infertility found to have a submucosal uterine fibroid measuring 6 cm on pelvic ultrasound. She desires to preserve her fertility. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 26-year-old woman was treated with methotrexate 8 days ago for ectopic pregnancy and presents to the Emergency Room with mild abdominal pain and vaginal spotting. Examination revealed a soft and non-tender abdomen. Her BhCG results show a drop from 1200 miu/ml on Day 4 to 500 miu/ml on Day 7. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 35-year-old woman presents with severe generalized abdominal pain, associated with nausea and vomiting for the last few hours. She is known to have an intrauterine contraceptive device inserted for the last 3 years. She did a home pregnancy test, which was positive. Ultrasound: Fluid collection of approximately 13 x 15 cm. No identified gestational sac. Both ovaries are normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman was diagnosed to have a complete abortion at 10 weeks of gestation. The patient is a heavy smoker and smokes more than 20 cigarettes a day. She was not taking any folic acid or dietary supplements. Which of the following is the most likely risk that caused the miscarriage?
option4: Smoking more than 20 cigarettes a day
correct_option: Smoking more than 20 cigarettes a day

question_text: A 50-year-old otherwise healthy woman presents to the Emergency Room with a history of amenorrhea for the last 17 weeks. Examination revealed normal vital signs. The uterine size corresponds to 20 weeks of gestation and feels doughy. Which of the following has the highest diagnostic value?
option4: CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis

question_text: A 17-year-old patient presents with a period of amenorrhea for the last 18 weeks. The uterus is about 22 weeks in size and feels doughy and compressible. Ultrasound: A snowstorm appearance with no clearly identified fetal pole. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 22-year-old, otherwise healthy woman, presents to the Emergency Room complaining of amenorrhea for the last 8 weeks and expelling blood and tissue through the vagina. Which of the following has the highest diagnostic value?
option4: Pelvic examination and CT scan of the pelvis

question_text: A 30-year-old, 12 weeks pregnant woman presented to the Emergency Room with vaginal bleeding. Examination revealed her fundal height corresponded with 19 weeks of gestation. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

An 18-year-old patient presents with amenorrhea for the last 15 weeks. She complains of vaginal bleeding and passage of tissue that she describes as vesicles. Ultrasound: A grape-like structure is filling the uterine cavity completely. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 44-year-old woman was just recently diagnosed with partial molar pregnancy, which was confirmed by histopathology. Which of the following is the most appropriate follow-up plan?

A 40-year-old man with a history of recurrent painful attacks affecting the right big toe, was diagnosed with gout and started on allopurinol. He is also a known case of psoriasis for several years. He presented to the clinic with recurrent joint pain affecting the right big toe, left ankle, and bilateral knees. He is using allopurinol regularly as prescribed but has no improvement in his joint pain. Examinations revealed he has active arthritis at the left ankle and knee joints bilaterally (see lab results). Which of the following is the most likely cause for his joint pain?

A 28-year-old woman with systemic lupus erythematosus for 2 years on hydroxychloroquine 200 mg/day, prednisone 5 mg/day, and azathioprine 100 mg/day presented to the Emergency Room with sudden onset of lower limb weakness and urinary incontinence. Examination revealed she has paraplegia and hyperreflexia (see lab results). Which of the following is the most appropriate test to be done immediately?
option4: Lumbar puncture, MRI, MRA, and MRV of the brain

question_text: A 50-year-old man is complaining of pain affecting the wrist joints bilaterally, second and third proximal interphalangeal joints, and right knee joint for the last 4 months. His examination revealed active arthritis at both wrists and second and third proximal interphalangeal joints (see lab results). Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 30-year-old woman with systemic lupus erythematosus is evaluated for right hip pain that started 3 weeks ago and occurs at night and with walking. Her current medications are azathioprine, hydroxychloroquine, prednisolone, and lisinopril for the last 5 years. Her examination was normal except for pain on internal rotation of the right hip. Laboratory tests including pelvic and left hip radiographs were normal. Which of the following is the most likely cause of hip pain?

A 60-year-old man complaining of right knee pain for several years that has increased over the last few months. He also has noticed frequent knee clicks. A diagnosis of osteoarthritis is suspected. Which of the following is the most appropriate to confirm the diagnosis?

A 52-year-old man with rheumatoid arthritis for 12 years. His current treatment consists of methotrexate 15 mg weekly and adalimumab 40 mg/2 weeks. He presented to the clinic complaining of fever and shortness of breath for 1 week. His chest examination revealed coarse crackles over the lower part of the right lung (see lab results). Which of the following is the most appropriate action to be taken on this visit?
option3: Hold his current treatment and start antibiotics
option4: Continue his current treatment and start antibiotics
correct_option: Hold his current treatment and start antibiotics

question_text: A 30-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department with an acute monoarthritis where joint aspiration was performed. Based on the presence of crystals in the synovial fluid, he was diagnosed with gout. Which of the following is the most likely type of the crystals seen?

A 30-year-old woman with a diagnosis of systemic lupus erythematosus who was maintained on mycophenolate mofetil 1 gm/day, hydroxychloroquine 200 mg/day, and prednisone 5 mg/day for the last 1 year. She has had inactive disease for more than 6 months. She is planning to get pregnant (see lab results). Which of the following is the most appropriate action at this time?
option3: Discontinue mycophenolate mofetil and start azathioprine
option4: Discontinue mycophenolate mofetil and start methotrexate
correct_option: Discontinue mycophenolate mofetil and start azathioprine

question_text: A 61-year-old woman is evaluated for a left pulmonary nodule that was discovered incidentally 2 months ago. She is currently asymptomatic and has not had shortness of breath, fever, chills, weight loss, or night sweats. Medical history is otherwise unremarkable, and she takes no medications. She never smoked. Examination revealed no cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy, and the lungs are clear to auscultation (see report). CT scan chest: Showed an 8 mm left lung nodule. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 63-year-old woman presented with exertional dyspnea for 8 months. She was diagnosed with COPD 3 years ago and has had 4 exacerbations in the last year. She completed pulmonary rehabilitation and quit smoking 2 years ago. Medications include long-acting B2 agonist, inhaled glucocorticoid, roflumilast, and albuterol as needed. Examination revealed no jugular venous distention; decreased breath sounds, a loud pulmonary component of S1 (see lab results and reports). Chest radiograph: Normal. Echocardiogram: Ejection fraction of 60%, no valvular or wall motion abnormalities, and estimated mean pulmonary artery pressure is 55 mmHg. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 58-year-old woman presented to the medical clinic complaining of daily wheezing and breathlessness during allergy season. These episodes of wheezing have limited her activities several times a week. She has a long history of seasonal allergies in the form of itchy eyes and runny nose. No other medical history. Medications include loratadine as needed, and antihistamine eye drops. Examination revealed watery eyes with conjunctival irritation, expiratory wheezing, and normal cardiac examination (see reports). Chest radiograph: Unremarkable. Spirometry: FEV1 of 74% of predicted, which improved by 18% with a bronchodilator. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?
option4: Add a low-dose inhaled glucocorticoid and long-acting B2 agonist
correct_option: Add a low-dose inhaled glucocorticoid and long-acting B2 agonist

question_text: A 71-year-old man is evaluated after a recent hospitalization 10 days ago for pneumonia and was discharged with a 10-day course of appropriate antibiotics. For the past 5 days, he has had a low-grade fever and increased shortness of breath. Pulmonary examination reveals decreased fremitus, dullness to percussion, and decreased breath sounds over the lower third of the right hemithorax (see lab results and report). Chest radiograph: Moderate right-sided pleural effusion. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 20-year-old man is evaluated for a 6-month history of chest and throat tightness, acute episodes of stridor, and a prolonged wheeze that occur during his college basketball practice. He has a history of moderate persistent asthma, which is controlled. He takes a short-acting B2 agonist inhaler as needed, but this does not relieve his symptoms. In addition to a low-dose inhaled glucocorticoid and a long-acting inhaled B2 agonist, he otherwise feels well. Medical history is otherwise unremarkable. Examination reveals normal vital signs and unremarkable systemic examination (see reports). Chest radiograph: Normal. Spirometry: No evidence of obstruction. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option4: Switch to a medium-dose inhaled glucocorticoid

question_text: A 33-year-old man presented with shortness of breath, fatigue, and nighttime cough. He has been working in a car repair shop for the past 1 year and notes that his symptoms began about 8 months ago. He believes his symptoms are associated with his workdays. He is being evaluated after several days off from work and currently has no symptoms or medical concerns. He has an unremarkable medical history and takes no medications. He has never smoked. Physical examination is unremarkable. A chest radiograph and spirometry results are normal. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 49-year-old woman is evaluated for a 4-month history of dry cough, which began 4 months ago when she noted a paroxysmal onset of persistent coughing spells that make it difficult to carry on a conversation. She was initially diagnosed with allergic rhinitis and was started on a glucocorticoid nasal inhaler with no improvement. Medical history is otherwise unremarkable except for a history of dust allergies. Examination revealed normal vital signs, slight nasal congestion, and dry cough are noted during the examination. Cardiovascular, pulmonary, and abdominal examination findings are unremarkable (see reports). Chest radiograph: Normal. Spirometry: Slightly reduced FEV/FVC ratio of 70% which increased after bronchodilator challenge by 20%. Which of the following is the most appropriate diagnostic test to perform next?

A 24-year-old primigravida is found during antenatal care at 22 weeks gestation to have a blood pressure of 140/95 mmHg in 2 different measurements documented 4 hours apart. Her urine test for protein was negative and she was asymptomatic. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
What is the latent period (incubation period) for lung cancer caused by cigarette smoking (in years)?

A 30-year-old woman G2 P1001 at 32 weeks' gestation is admitted to labor and delivery complaining of lower abdominal and back pain. She has a significant previous history of severe preeclampsia (see lab results). Blood pressure 140/90 mmHg Test Result Normal Values Platelets count 80 150-400 x 109/L Uric Acid 460 140-360 umol/L Which of the following findings would indicate severe preeclampsia?

A 38-year-old woman G3 P2002 at 34 weeks'' gestation is admitted to labor and delivery complaining of headache, epigastric pain and blurred vision. Non-stress test is reassuring. Blood pressure 170/120 mmHg Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option1: Inform anesthesia and deliver the patient immediately
option2: Stabilize general condition, administer magnesium sulfate and deliver the patient
option3: Administer magnesium sulfate, stabilize general condition and wait Until 37 weeks'' gestation
option4: Stabilize general condition, administer corticosteroids and deliver the patient
correct_option: Stabilize general condition, administer magnesium sulfate and deliver the patient

question_text: A 24-year-old woman presents at 7 weeks' gestation complaining of mild vaginal bleeding and lower abdominal pain colicky in nature. Upon examination; normal vital signs and speculum examination shows the cervix is closed and fresh bleed coming through the cervix. Which of the following is the most likely cause of the bleeding?

A 22-year-old primigravida at 7 weeks'' gestation is brought to Emergency Department complaining of moderate vaginal bleeding with passage of tissue like material and lower abdominal colicky pain. Speculum examination; tissue is in the vagina, uterine cervix is opened and admitting one finger. Product of conception is protruding through cervical canal. Bimanual examination reveals bulky uterus corresponding to seven-eight weeks gestation. Adnexa were free and there was no cervical excitation. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A woman presents at her 9th week of pregnancy with severe vaginal bleeding, lower abdominal pain and passage of the fetus through the vagina. On vaginal examination; cervix is open and tissue seen in the cervix. Which of the following the most appropriate management?
option1: Fluid replacement and evacuation and curettage
correct_option: Fluid replacement and evacuation and curettage

question_text: A 40-year-old woman has just delivered her 7th child at 38 weeks with 4 kg baby boy vaginally 10 minutes ago. The placenta is still in place and she starts to bleed profusely. The estimated amount of blood loss visually is around 800 ml. Which of the following best describes this case?

A 38-year-old G9 P8008 woman presented at 38 weeks gestation in labor and diagnosis of prolonged 2nd stage of labor was entertained and finally delivered 4.3 Kg baby by vacuum extraction. Anticipated management was taken because of risk of postpartum hemorrhage and hypovolemic shock. Which of the following is considered a best reliable indicator of above complications?

A 37-year-old woman G8 P7017 at 36 weeks' gestation has history of recurrent postpartum that required blood transfusion. She has no significant medical problem. Which of the following is the most appropriate preventive measure at delivery?
option1: Deliver the patient in the operating room
option2: Perform an elective cesarean section at 38 weeks gestation
option3: Perform active management of 3rd stage of labor
option4: Give the patient preload of crystalloid fluids while in labor to overcome the possible post-delivery hypovolemia
correct_option: Perform active management of 3rd stage of labor

question_text: A 14-year-old healthy girl has painless vaginal bleeding occurring at interval of 3 weeks to 2 months. The bleeding usually lasts 2 to 4 days, not preceded by premenstrual symptoms and not associated with clots. Review of systems is not significant. Physical examination shows a normal appearing pubertal girl along with normal development of secondary sex characteristics (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hemoglobin 120 112-165 g/L RBC 6 4.6-4.8 x 1012/L WBC 4.8 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Platelets count 220 150-400 x 109/L Pregnancy test Negative Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A 50-year-old woman presented with amenorrhea for the last 6 months and has unwanted hot flushes disturbing her night sleep. Which of the following serum levels would confirm the diagnosis?

A 22-year-old primigravida presented at 38 weeks'' gestation in labor. Vaginal examination showed 7cm dilated cervix, full effacement and vertex. An hour later the obstetrician noticed a loop of the umbilical cord below the fetal head. Fetal Heart Rate 120 beat/min. Which of the following is the most appropriate immediate management?

A 30-year-old woman G2 P1002 admitted at 40 weeks' gestation with onset of labor and ruptured membranes. Her cervix was 5 cm dilated and 100% effaced with 0 station vertex for 3 hours. A fetal monitoring strip showed 2 contractions in 10 minutes with a reassured non-stress test. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 33-year-old woman presents with a history of amenorrhea for a year following dilation and curettage because of abnormal uterine bleeding. She underwent to both progesterone and combined estrogen and progesterone withdrawal tests with no response. Her serum FSH level was normal. Which of the following syndromes is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman was brought to the Emergency Department 7 weeks after her last menstruation with positive pregnancy test. Abdominal and transvaginal ultrasound showed no intrauterine pregnancy sac. She has lower abdominal pain, mostly on the left side and mild vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most probable diagnosis?
After how many years the excess risk of myocardial infarction will decline significantly for individual who stop smoking?
A 29-year-old woman G7 P5015 is admitted at 30 weeks' gestation in labor room with leaking liquor for observation. Which of the following most likely confirm the diagnosis?

A 28-year-old woman G5 P3013 is seen in the Antenatal Clinic. Her previous pregnancies were term normal deliveries. Currently, she is 10 weeks'' gestation confirmed by a pregnancy test and ultrasound examination. Which of the following is the most important purpose of the first antenatal visit?

A 57-year-old man is evaluated for chronic cough, occasional wheezing, and exertional dyspnea while walking. Medical history includes an episode of bronchitis, for which he underwent outpatient treatment 8 months ago. He has a 20-pack-year smoking history. Examination revealed normal vital signs, mildly decreased breath sounds throughout both lungs, and scattered expiratory wheezes bilaterally. Spirometry shows an FEV1 of 75% of predicted and a post-bronchodilator FEV/FVC ratio of 68%. His modified Medical Research Council (mMRC) symptom score is 2. In addition to smoking cessation, which of the following is the most appropriate long-term management plan?
option1: Combination inhaled glucocorticoid and a long-acting bronchodilator
option2: Phosphodiesterase-4 inhibitor and combination inhaled glucocorticoid and long-acting bronchodilator
option3: Short-acting bronchodilator as needed, a long-acting bronchodilator, and pulmonary rehabilitation
option4: Short-acting bronchodilator as needed and an inhaled glucocorticoid
correct_option: Short-acting bronchodilator as needed, a long-acting bronchodilator, and pulmonary rehabilitation

question_text: A 42-year-old woman presented to the clinic for follow-up of a 7-month history of chronic, nonproductive cough. She was recently diagnosed with cough-variant asthma after a methacholine challenge test. She was started on inhaled fluticasone and as-needed albuterol 2 months ago. Her cough initially improved; however, her symptoms have not fully resolved and are worsened at times, most notably when she lies down. She also noted that mild hoarseness occasionally accompanies her cough, especially when she wakes up in the morning. Examination revealed a dry cough with frequent throat clearing. The chest was clear with no wheezing. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 17-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department with severe asthma exacerbation that began 12 hours ago. He is on an albuterol inhaler and a long-acting glucocorticoid at home without improvement. He was hospitalized and intubated 2 years ago. Examination revealed respiratory distress with diffuse expiratory wheezes. After 1 hour of systemic glucocorticoid and B2 agonist nebulizer, he still appeared in respiratory distress (see lab results and report). Chest X-ray: Hyperinflated lungs. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option3: Admit to the general medical floor
option4: Discharge home the next day with follow-up
