She reports that the symptoms began 2 days prior and had started with coughing up clear-colored phlegm. Blood pressure 124/72 mmHg Heart rate 120 /min Respiratory rate 24 /min Temperature 37.5° C oral Which of the following tests is initially most appropriate?

A 45-year-old woman presents to the Gynecological Clinic with history of genital warts that were treated by electrocautery 2 years back. The woman complained of recurrent occurrence of scanty fresh bleeding after intercourse. Which of the following is the likely source of her bleeding?

A 26-year-old multiparous woman in labor is requesting pain relief. While in the lithotomy position, the obstetrician performs a pudendal nerve block, and injects a local anesthetic into the sacrotuberous ligament close to the ischial spine. Which of the following structures will remain fully sensitive despite this pudendal nerve block?

A 37-year-old primigravida presented to the clinic with amenorrhea for 12 weeks, hyperemesis gravidarum, and raised human chorionic gonadotropin levels. Uterus size is equivalent to 16 weeks'' gestation (see report). Ultrasound: Fetus is small for dates. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 17-year-old woman comes at 39 weeks' gestation to the Emergency Department with no previous antenatal care. She delivered a healthy baby boy normally with no complications reported. Then she was given an injection of RhoGAM. What is the indication of RhoGAM?
option1: Mother is Rh- and the baby is Rh+
option2: Father is RH+ and the baby is Rh-
option3: Mother is Rh+ and the baby is Rh-
option4: Father is Rh- and the baby is Rh+
correct_option: Mother is Rh- and the baby is Rh+

question_text: A 24-year-old woman presents to the clinic with complaining of failure to conceive for more than 2 years of regular trials. Patient has history of irregular periods occurring once every 6 to 7 weeks and is usually scanty (see report). Body mass index 35 kg/m2 Transvaginal ultrasound: Revealed the presence of multiple small cysts; each is 3-5mm, lining the ovarian periphery. representing pearl necklace appearance. Which of the following is the most likely factor contributing to her infertility?

A 24-year-old married woman presented to the clinic with severe abdominal pain followed by vaginal bleeding, for the last 6 hours. Patient''s LMP was 8 weeks ago. On examination; she is drowsy and has a tense abdomen. Blood pressure 90/60 mmHg Heart rate 120 min Respiratory rate 20 /min Temperature 36.8 ° C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 23-year-old nulligravida patient complains of inability to become pregnant after 3 months of trying. Patient periods are regular and normal. She had no prior reproductive problems. She currently is not on any contraception. examination is normal. Her husband is 25-year-old healthy and has one autistic cousin and one with trisomy 21. Which of the following is the best action?

A 15-year-old unmarried girl presents with history of pain during menstruation. Her menarche was at age of 13 and menses are regular. Physical and pelvic examinations are normal for her age. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A patient is seen at 8 weeks' gestation in an Antenatal Clinic with a diagnosis of cervical incompetence. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option1: Insertion cervical suture the same week
option2: Confirm the diagnosis by Hegar dilator

question_text: A 25-year-old nulliparous woman at 37 weeks' gestation presents to the hospital with regular contractions and spontaneous rupture of membranes. A pelvic examination shows cervical dilation of 6 cm and 90% effacement. Fetal heart rate 130-140 bpm with good variability. Ultrasound examination shows the fetus in a breech position and the patient is admitted for a caesarean section. When would be the optimal time to administer intravenous antibiotics?
option2: Upon closure of the skin wound

question_text: A nulliparous woman is admitted to the hospital at 42 weeks'' gestation for induction of labour with cervical prostaglandin gel. On admission, a pelvic examination shows the cervix is closed, thick and posterior facing. A non-stress test shows the fetal heart rate is 130-140 beats per minute with good variability. There are 3 accelerations and 2 fetal movements during the examination. 1 hour after applying the prostaglandin gel, the fetal heart tones are 80 beats per minute and the contractions last for two minutes. Which of the following intervention is the most important?
option1: Administer oxygen at 10 L/min by facemask
option2: Prepare for an urgent caesarean section

question_text: A 24-year-old primigravida is found during antenatal care at 22 weeks gestation to have a blood pressure of 140/95 mmHg in 2 different measurements documented 4 hours apart. Her urine test for protein was Negative and she was asymptomatic. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old woman gravida 4, para 3 woman presents to Antenatal Clinic at 32 weeks'' gestation with headache and swelling of the feet. At the initial antenatal visit, there was +2 pitting edema of the lower extremities and the face appears swollen. Dipsick of the urine shows proteinuria. The fundal height is 28 cm and fetal heart rate was 140-150 beats per minute with good variability. She reports regular fetal movements. Blood pressure 150/100 mmHg Heart rate 90 /min Respiratory rate 26 /min Temperature 37.1° C Which additional clinical finding would be associated with intrauterine growth restriction?

A 32-year-old multiparous woman is admitted for induction of labour at 41 weeks and 2 days gestation. She had prior 2 uneventful vaginal deliveries. A pelvic examination shows the cervix is dilated at 2 cm and is 50% effaced with a medium consistency and mid-anterior position. The presenting part is at -2 station. Which of the following methods would be most appropriate for labour induction?

A 30-year-old woman presents to the Women's Health Clinic after a spontaneous rupture of membranes occurred at home 1 hour before. Patient medical records indicate a positive history of recurrent vaginal herpes simplex. Which of the following is the next best step in management?

A gravida 3, para 2 is 16 weeks'' pregnant presents to the obstetrics Clinic concerned about outbreak of illness in her child''s school and the potential effects on the fetus. Which of the following vaccines is recommended at this time?

A 24-year-old multiparous woman is in the second stage of labour. She has a history of asthma and she uses an albuterol inhaler as needed. She has a prior herpes simplex virus infection and reports outbreaks about once per year. As a child, she contracted rheumatic fever and takes prophylactic antibiotics when needed for mitral valve stenosis. Which significant medical history indicates beneficial use of forceps?

A 28-year-old gravida 3, para 2 presents to Obstetric Triage Unit at 37 weeks'' gestation following spontaneous rupture of the amniotic membranes confirmed by physical examination. On admission to labour and delivery room she was complaining of abdominal pain. Digital examination shows a cervical dilation of 4 cm and 80% effacement with the presenting part at 0 station. CTG is reactive with baseline fetal heart of 170 bpm. Blood pressure 138/78 mmHg Heart rate 111 /min Respiratory rate 24 /min Temperature 38.4° C oral Which of the following is the next step in management?

A 30-year-old woman presents to the Gynecology Clinic with diffuse lower right-sided abdominal pain and low back pain that has been progressively worsening for the past month. The pain intensity worsens after sexual intercourse. Patient menstrual periods occur irregularly and with heavy bleeding. Palpation reveals a tender mass in the lower right quadrant. Ultrasound pelvis scan shows a 9X9 centimetre heterogeneous mass filled with calcifications. Which of the following is most appropriate method of treatment?

A 32-year-old woman 16 weeks pregnant presents to the clinic with vaginal bleeding, abdominal enlargement and vomiting for the past 4 days. On examination, the uterine size is larger than expected for the gestational age (see lab result and report). Test Result Normal Value Human chorionic gonadotropin 80,000 < 5 mlU/mL Ultrasound: 'Snowstorm-like'" appearance. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"

A hemodynamically stable woman has a tubal, ectopic pregnancy measuring 2.5 x 3 cm by ultrasound (see lab result). Test Result Normal Value Human chorionic gonadotropin N 5000 < 5 mlU/mL Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 25-year-old woman in her first trimester of pregnancy has come for a routine prenatal visit. She used to smoke one pack of cigarettes per day, and is now being advised to quit smoking. Which of the following is the greatest risk to her unborn child?

A 48-year-old obese woman complains of nausea. Patient LMP was 2 months ago. She is using barrier method (condoms) as a birth control method. Examination is normal except for a slight dusky discoloration of her cervix. Which of the following is the next step in management?
A 30-year-old woman with a history of infertility for 6 years complains of severe dysmenorrhea unrelieved by naproxen. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 40-year-old woman complains of Post-coital bleeding. Speculum examination reveals cervical mass. Which of the following is the most appropriate next investigation?

A young primigravida presents with significant edema of the lower extremities and hands. The patient was admitted for evaluation and management. Blood pressure 150/95 mmHg Heart rate 66 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6° C Which of the following would be the most appropriate management?

A 30-year-old woman P1+0 presented to Obstetrics and Gynecology Clinic complains of inability to conceive for the last 3 years. During that time, she was not on contraceptive and her menstrual periods every 45 days but no period for the last 6 months (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone 9.2 U/L 0.4 - 5.0 jU/L Follicular phase 5-20 IU/. Mid cycle peak 15-35 IU/L Follicle-stimulating hormone 5.3 qU/L Luteal phase 5-15 lU/L Postmenopausal women 50-100 1U/L Men 5-20 IU/L Men 3-15 U/L Luteinizing hormone 7.2 U/L Follicular phase 5-22 U/L Luteal phase 5-22 U/L Ovulatory phase 30-250 U/L Postmenopausal Women > 30 U/L Prolactin 140 ng/ml < 25 ng/ml Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 42-year-old obese woman presented with a 5-year history of irregular vaginal bleeding. Thyroid studies are normal. She noticed that her irregular in menstruation has started to put on weight (see report). Body mass index 35 kg/m2 Endometrial Biopsy: Adenomatous hyperplasia. Which of the following is the most likely cause?
option3: Local genetic mutation of the endometrium
option4: Peripheral conversion of precursors to estrogen
correct_option: Peripheral conversion of precursors to estrogen

question_text: A 42 year-old obese nulligravid patient with polycystic ovary syndrome has had irregular vaginal bleeding since menarche. She has used no medication to control her periods (see lab results and report). Test Result Normal Values Cortisol 8 a.m. 335 138-635 mol/L Estrogen 630 90-550 pmol/L. Follicle-stimulating hormone 38 5-20 IU/L Testosterone 3.1 0.7-2.8 mol/L Prolactin 590 < 870 pmol/L T3 (Total serum) 1.2 0.9-2.8 mol/L Thyroxine (T4 Total serum) 82 64- 155 mol/L. Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone 4.5 0.4 - 5.0 U/L Endometrial Biopsy: Adenomatous hyperplasia. Which of the following is the most likely cause of the adenomatous hyperplasia?

A 31-year-old woman P2 presented to the Women''s Health Clinic for a routine physical examination. She has presented to routine health examinations annually for the last 5 years. Records indicate that in the previous year, she has negative tests for both the human papilloma virus and the papanicolaou cervical smear. This year both tests are with no abnormal findings. When should the tests be repeated?

A paediatrician is called to attend a caesarean section delivery of 38 week''s gestation baby. On delivery, the neonate does not cry immediately, appears cyanotic and floppy. After drying, suctioning and stimulating the baby under the radiant warmer, the following evidence is recorded at 5 minutes (see table). Heart rate 120 beats per minute Breathing Irregular, gasping Reflex irritability Coughs, and cries with stimulation Activity Arms and legs flexed, little movement Appearance Rosy with blue extremities What is the most accurate Apgar score?

A 20-year-old woman presented to the Maternal Health Clinic with severe facial acne and irregular menstrual bleeding. examination shows fine hair growth on the chin, upper back, breasts and lower abdomen. A pregnancy test is returned negative. She returns to the clinic on the 5th day of her menstrual cycle and has a serum sample drawn for analysis (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Follicle-stimulating hormone 10 5-20 IU/L Luteinizing hormone 28 5-22 U/L Prolactin 890 < 870 pmol/L Testosterone 3.2 0.7-2.8 mol/L Which additional laboratory values should be obtained?
option1: Thyroid stimulating hormone and free T3 and T4

question_text: A 30-year-old woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic with the inability to conceive despite trying for the past 18 months. Patient menstrual cycles were regular until 2 years ago, and since then she has had irregular vaginal bleeding. There is hair covering the upper lip and chin. A pelvic examination confirms bilaterally enlarged ovaries. DHEA and 17-OH progesterone values are normal. Body mass index 30 kg/m2 Which of the following laboratory values is most likely finding?
option4: A Follicle Stimulating Hormone to Luteinizing Hormone ratio of 1:3
correct_option: A Follicle Stimulating Hormone to Luteinizing Hormone ratio of 1:3

question_text: A gravida 2 para 0 woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic reporting that she has not menstruated for the past 2 months. The previous pregnancy had resulted in a stillbirth at 34 weeks' gestation. A human chorionic gonadotropin test is now positive. She has been trying to conceive for the past 6 months. She is afraid of having another stillbirth and asks to be vaccinated against everything. Which of the following vaccines is most appropriate to administer?

A 24-year-old nulliparous woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic in the evening at 34 weeks' gestation and complaining of decreased fetal movement. She says she had been at work all day and then came directly to the hospital when she noticed that the baby has not been moving as much as usual. There has been no leaking of amniotic fluid. There are no uterine contractions and also no vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 40-year-old woman underwent a laparoscopic tubal ligation during the postpartum period following the delivery of her 4th child 4 years ago. She presents to the Maternal Health Clinic complaining of bright red vaginal spotting. Patient last menstrual cycle was 6 weeks ago. A pelvic examination shows fullness over the right adnexa. There is no pain or tenderness. Her last pregnancy resulted in an emergency caesarean section with a mid-line incision. Which of the following is the best next step in management?

A 22-year-old woman attends the Women''s Health Clinic for a postnatal examination. She delivered a full-term baby boy 6 weeks earlier and the infant is exclusively breastfeeding and shows a good latch and weight gain. She would like to have more children, but not within the next 2 years. She does not smoke cigarettes and not at risk for heart disease. She inquired about birth control methods. Which of the following methods is most appropriate?

A 30-year-old para 1 woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic at 20 days postpartum. She has delivered a full-term girl vaginally by forceps-assisted delivery. The weight at birth is 3.54 kg. She presents with a yellow-white colored vaginal discharge. The discharge is odorless and there is no itching or discomfort. An examination shows a pink cervix and absence of uterine tenderness. A microscopic analysis of the vaginal fluid is positive for leukocytes and epithelial cells. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 35-year-old nulliparous woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic at 24 weeks'' gestation. The significant medical history includes insulin-dependent diabetes class F with nephritis. Additionally, she has chronic hypertension which is controlled with medication. The pregnancy has been uncomplicated so far. The uterine fundus measured 25 cm above the symphysis and fetal heart tones show a baseline of 160 beats per minute. Which of the following complications is most likely to occur?

A multiparous woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic at 37 weeks' gestation with regular contractions occurring every 2 minutes lasting for 45-60 seconds with moderate intensity. Last pregnancy ended up with low transverse incision caesarean section. She is now pregnant with twins. Twin A is breech and twin B is cephalic. There has been small amount of clear fluid leaking from the vagina for the past hour. A nitrazine paper test turns blue-green in color. Pelvic examination shows a cervical dilation of 4 cm and 80% effacement. The obstetrician informs her that a caesarean section is necessary. Which of the following is the most likely reason for a caesarean section?

A 28-year-old primigravida 8 weeks gestation woman attended her first antenatal appointment. During the history assessment, she reports that she never had chickenpox. A serum blood titre is drawn and is negative for antibodies. Which of the following is the next best step in management?

A 34-year-old gravida 2 para 1 woman 10 weeks gestation known to have a hypertension taking labetalol 100 milligrams twice per day. In her first pregnancy had no complications and resulted in a healthy infant. During this pregnancy which of the following risks is increased?

A 26-year-old primigravida woman presented to the clinic at 37 weeks' gestation for a routine antenatal care. She reports feeling ill and has a frontal headache. A urine dipstick analysis is performed and shows proteinuria 2+. She is admitted to the hospital High Risk Unit and started on an intravenous infusion of magnesium sulfate. Blood pressure 152/102 mmHg Heart rate 72 /min Respiratory rate 16 /min Temperature 37.2° C oral Which of the following is the primary reason for administering the intravenous medication?

A 34-year-old woman presented to the hospital 7 days before with right-sided lower abdominal pain. In addition, she had slight bright red vaginal bleeding. A blood serum sample of B-human chorionic gonadotropin was 2900 I/Liter. She was administered intramuscular methotrexate. A week later, she reported to the hospital with severe right-sided lower abdominal pain. A blood serum sample of B-human chorionic gonadotropin was drawn and shown to be 6,000 IU/Liter. Which of the following would be most appropriate?
A 34-year-old multiparous woman presents at 37 weeks' twins gestation with regular contractions. Which of the following fetal presentations is favourable for vaginal delivery?

A 30-year-old woman presented to the Emergency Department at 03:00 o''clock with right-sided abdominal pain in the lower quadrant. She rates the pain at a level 9 on the 1-10 pain scale. The pain was sudden, associated with nausea and vomiting. A human chorionic gonadotropin urine test was negative. On palpation, there is tenderness and a mass is noted. An ultrasound confirms a mass of 6 cm diameter. Blood pressure 120/70 mmHg Heart rate 110 /min Respiratory rate 20 /min Temperature 37.0° C oral Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 34-week pregnant woman presents in labor with contractions occurring every 4 minutes, she also has vaginal bleeding which is heavier than normal menstrual flow. The fetus is in a back-down position, transverse lie, and the cervix is dilated 3 cm with bulging membranes. The fetal heart rate is 170 bpm. The sonography showed a posterior fundal placenta with a retroplacental sonolucent area. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial management?

A 40-year-old sexually active patient presents for cervical cancer screening. Physical examination is unremarkable, menstruation is regular with a 28-day cycle and last menstrual period was 5 days ago. Which of the following is the best advice about Pap smear?
option4: Three negative results and no further screening

question_text: A 32-year-old primigravida at 34 weeks' gestation presents for urgent care after falling down a flight of stairs. The patient complains of uterine tenderness and reduced fetal movement in the 45 minutes since the fall. There has been a small amount of dark red vaginal bleeding. The fetal heart monitoring revealed a heart rate of 150 bpm with irregular uterine contractions every 3 to 4 minutes. Her pregnancy has been uncomplicated. She has positive history of cigarettes smoking. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 22-year-old married woman has had severe, recurrent depression associated with multiple suicide attempts. She has been stabilized for the past 3 months on paroxetine and is now pregnant. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option1: Discontinue paroxetine due to avoid prematurity
option2: Continue paroxetine and monitor for depression
option3: Discontinue paroxetine to avoid fetal damage
correct_option: Continue paroxetine and monitor for depression

question_text: A 23-year-old woman presented with breast tenderness, abdominal bloating, and food cravings. She has a 6-month history of tiredness, anxiety, emotional lability, difficulty concentrating and insomnia. There are no menstrual irregularities or prodromal life stressors. The symptoms recurred on a regular basis during the week leading up to the menstrual period but completely resolve within the first 3 days of menses. The patient felt totally incapacitated when symptomatic, and the symptoms adversely affected personal and professional life. Which of the following is the best-evidenced treatment?

A 14-year-old girl has a 6-month history of lower mid-abdominal pain colicky in nature, radiating to the back and upper thighs. It often begins with onset of menses which lasts for 2-4 days. She has missed several days of school during the last 2 months because the severity of pain. Physical examination is normal for abdomen and pelvis. Normal secondary sexual development is also noted. Which of the following is the first step in management?

A 48-year-old woman nulliparous presented to the Emergency Department with heavy vaginal bleeding associated with slight dizziness. No other significant past medical history except heavy menstruation in the past few months. The examination was normal apart from the presence of a large amount of blood in the vaginal vault (see lab results). Supine Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 90 /min Standing Blood pressure 104/66 mmHg Heart rate 100 /min Temperature 36.6° C Test Result Normal Values Hb 10 120-160 g/L Pregnancy test -ve Which of the following is the first step in management?

A 27-year-old nulliparous is concerned about not having conceived after a year of regular, unprotected intercourse. The patient denied any major medical illnesses and takes no medications. The patient''s spouse is reported to be healthy but has never fathered a child. What is the most appropriate initial step in evaluation after history and examination?

A 25-year-old nulliparous presented with an 18-month history of cyclic and debilitating pelvic pain related to menses. Menses is regular and heavy, requiring 10-15 thick pads on the days of heaviest flow. The patient has been engaging in regular intercourse without contraception for 1 year in an attempt to conceive. Pelvic examination revealed a normal-sized, immobile, retroverted uterus with nodularity and tenderness on palpation of the uterosacral ligaments. Which of the following would most likely help in the diagnosis?

A 27-year-old nulliparous presents for a routine examination. Her gynecological history indicates a 5-year history of oligomenorrhea with only about 2-3 menses a year. She denies inter-cycle spotting or premenstrual symptoms. The last menses was 3 months ago. The physical examination confirmed a moderate amount of facial hair and inflammatory acne while the pelvic examination is normal. Weight 60 kg Blood pressure 120/75 mmHg Heart rate 72 /min Respiratory rate 17 /min Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 24-year-old married nulliparous has an 18-month history of cyclic, debilitating pelvic pain related to menses which are regular and heavy requiring 10-15 thick pads on the days of heaviest flow. For the past year, the patient has been engaging in regular intercourse without contraception in an attempt to conceive. Pelvic examination showed normal-size, immobile, retroverted uterus with nodularity and tenderness on palpation of the uterosacral ligaments. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A young couple has been trying to conceive a baby for 6 months with no success. The wife has regular cycles every 23 days and 4-day periods. Which of the following is most appropriate investigation?

A 19-year-old woman presented with crampy, lower abdominal pain beginning with the onset of menses every month, and lasting for 2 to 3 days. There was no pain between menses, but reported radiation of pain to the lower back and upper thighs, along with nausea, fatigue, and headache which has been present for several years and progressively getting worse. Physical examination normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 14-year-old child presented to the clinic after being sexually abused 6 hours earlier. Genital examination confirmed anogenital erythema and mild bleeding vaginally, and labia majora was swollen and bruised. Blood pressure 120/70 mmHg Heart rate 90 /min Respiratory rate 20 /min Temperature 36.6° C What is the most likely site of hymeneal tear that is suggestive of penile penetration?

A 16-year-old presented to the clinic for evaluation of primary amenorrhea. Examination showed normal adult-size breasts. But coarse pubic and axillary hair (see lab result). Test Result Normal Value Testosterone 13.86 0.7-2.8 mol/L Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 20-year-old pregnant woman presented with loss of fetal movements, followed by decreased urinary output and difficulty breathing (see lab results and report). Test Result Normal Values Hb 100 120-160 g/L DLCO 70 75-140% Platelets count 75 150-400 x 109/L Creatinine 105 44-115 mol/L Prothrombin Time 24 10-13 sec APTT 60 30-40 sec Fibrinogen 1.6 2-4 g/L Chest X-ray: Diffuse ground glass opacities. Which of the following is the most appropriate diagnosis?

A 26-year-old woman presented to the clinic with secondary amenorrhea of 6 months duration. She gave history of breast tenderness. On examination; bilateral breast whitish discharge from nipples. Her urine pregnancy test was Negative and elevated serum prolactin levels. Which of the following radiological assessments is most appropriate?

A 25-year-old woman G2 P1001 was admitted at 39 weeks'' gestation in Labor Suite with labour pains. On Abdominal examination, 3 moderate contractions every 10 minutes. The cervix was 6 cm dilated, 80% effaced. Mouth, lips, nose and orbital hollows were palpable. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 32-year-old woman G3 P2002 presented at 38 weeks'' gestation to Antenatal Clinic. She has had history of 2 previous normal deliveries of 3 Kg babies. Current pregnancy, fetus found to be breech in presentation. The patient was counselled and consented for external cephalic version. On the scheduled day, an estimated fetal weight is 2.6 Kg by ultrasound, flexed fetal head, and uterus is bicornuate with average liquor measured. Which of the above findings is contraindicated external cephalic version?

A 33-year-old woman was referred to the Gynecology Clinic because of secondary infertility. Detailed clinical history revealed that she has history of severe dysmenorrhea. Which of the following will typically be the cause of her dysmenorrhea?

A primigravida at 37 weeks gestation known to have bicornuate uterus complains of subcostal discomfort and fetal kicking is appreciated in the lower abdomen mainly. Leopold maneuver; shows global soft presenting part with sluggish ballottement by first and second pelvic grips. Fetal heart detected at the level of maternal umbilicus. Which of the following is the most likely fetal presentation?

A primigravida at 37 weeks gestation known to have bicornuate uterus complains of subcostal discomfort and fetal kicking is appreciated in the lower abdomen mainly. Leopold maneuver, shows global soft presenting part with sluggish ballottement by first and second pelvic grips. Fetal heart detected at the level of maternal umbilicus. What is the recommended method of delivery if this fetal presentation persisted?

A 23-year-old woman G3 P02002 at 20 weeks'' gestation with history of previous 2 preterm deliveries attends Antenatal Clinic. Current pregnancy is uneventful. An ultrasound shows size correspond to date, anterior placenta and cervical length is 30 mm. Which of the following is the most appropriate intervention?

A 26-year-old woman G2 P1001 admitted to the hospital in active labor and ruptured membranes at 38 weeks of gestation. Upon vaginal examination, the cervix was 6 cm dilated and 1 cm long. The orbital ridge and orbits, saddle of the nose, mouth, and chin were palpable. What is the scientific term for this fetal presentation?

A 30-year-old woman G2 P2002 at 32 weeks'' gestation presents to Emergency Department complaining of lower abdominal and back pain which has increased in frequency and intensity over the last few hours. Abdominal examination shows fundal height equals to 32 cm, longitudinal lie fetus and cephalic presentation. Fetal heart was positive and cardiotocography is reactive with 2-3 uterine contractions/10 minutes. Vaginal examination shows 1 cm dilated cervix, 70% effacement, -3 station and cephalic. Which of the following is the next step to confirm the diagnosis?
option3: Repeat vaginal assessment in two hour-time
option4: Cervical length assessment by ultrasound scanning
correct_option: Repeat vaginal assessment in two hour-time

question_text: A 30-year-old woman G2 P2002 at 32 weeks'' gestation presents to Emergency Department complaining of lower abdominal and back pain, which has increased in frequency and intensity over the last few hours. Abdominal examination shows fundal height equals to 32 cm, longitudinal lie fetus and cephalic presentation. Fetal heart was positive and cardiotocography is reactive with 2-3 uterine contractions per 10 minutes. Vaginal examination shows 1 cm dilated cervix, 70% effacement, -3 station and cephalic. Which of the following is the most appropriate step in management?
option1: Inform neonatologist, administer corticosteroids and strict bed rest
option2: Inform neonatologist, administer corticosteroids and hydrate the patient
option3: Inform neonatologist, administer tocolytics and start intravenous antibiotics
option4: Inform neonatologist, administer intravenous antibiotics and strict bed rest
correct_option: Inform neonatologist, administer corticosteroids and hydrate the patient

question_text: A 47-year-old woman, poorly controlled diabetic, complains of polymenorrhagia every couple of weeks. Body mass index 37 kg/m2 Which of the following is most appropriate investigation to establish the diagnosis?
option4: Magnetic resonance imaging of the pelvis

question_text: A 55-year-old woman comes to the Gynecology Clinic complaining of a mass protruding through her vagina when standing for quite some times especially during cough and straining. She has 7 children that were delivered vaginally. The last delivery was complicated by prolonged second stage and ended up with vacuum extraction delivery of a 4.2 Kg baby. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 25-year-old woman complains of velvety, hyperpigmented plaques on her neck and axilla. She fails to conceive over the last 2 years despite regular intercourse. Her menses are irregular and occurring once every 6 to 8 weeks. She starts recently to experience increased facial hairs. What is the appropriate term of her skin hyperpigmentation?

A 26-year-old married woman presents to Endocrinology-Gynecology clinic for a follow-up of her investigation results. Accordingly, a Polycystic ovarian syndrome is made. She is counselled about plan of management and metformin use in her condition. What does metformin do in the above condition?
option4: Lowering blood sugar by increasing consumption

question_text: A 45-year-old woman comes to the clinic complaining of loss of appetite, bloating and abdominal pain. Clinical assessment and investigations confirmed the presence of moderate ascites, pleural effusion and bilateral ovarian masses. Cancer antigen 125 (CA 125) was 100 unit/ml. Which investigation is the most helpful in making a diagnosis?
option4: Magnetic resonance imaging of the pelvic soft tissue

question_text: A 25-year-old woman complains of velvety, hyperpigmented plaques on her neck and under arm. She fails to conceive over the last 2 years despite regular intercourse. Her menses are infrequent and occurring once every 6 to 8 weeks. She notices recently increased facial hair. Her husband is healthy with a normal seminal analysis. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old woman comes to the clinic complaining of loss of appetite, bloating and abdominal pain. Clinical assessment and investigations confirmed the presence of moderate ascites, pleural effusion and bilateral ovarian masses. Cancer antigen 125 (CA 125) was 95 unit/ml. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 37-years-old P4 with a history of 3 previous caesarean sections is undergoing an elective caesarean section at 37 weeks of gestation. The placenta was found to be attached deeply into the uterine wall. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 24-year-old woman is complaining of failure to conceive over the last 16 months despite regular intercourse. She has infrequent menstruation occurring once every 6 to 8 weeks along with increased facial hair and acne. On ultrasound, her ovaries had multiple small follicles each is 2-5 mm diameter on the surface. She indicated that her main aim is getting pregnant. Body mass index 37 kg/m2 Which of the following is the initial step in management?
option2: Weight reduction, metformin and clomiphene citrate
option3: Weight reduction, in vitro fertilization and salicylic acid
option4: Weight reduction, laparoscopic ovarian wedge resection and metformin
correct_option: Weight reduction, metformin and clomiphene citrate

question_text: A 24-year-old woman is complaining of failure to conceive over the last 16 months despite regular intercourse. She has infrequent menstruation occurring once every 6 to 8 weeks, with increased facial hair and acne. On ultrasound, her ovaries had multiple small follicles each is 2-5 mm in diameter on the surface. Body mass index 37 kg/m2 Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 16-year-old girl is complaining of infrequent cycle occurring once every 6 to 8 weeks. Which of the following is most likely the cause?
option3: Physiological variation in menstrual period timing
option4: Uterine benign masses in particular uterine fibroids
correct_option: Physiological variation in menstrual period timing

question_text: A 33-year-old woman presented to the Emergency Department with severe lower abdominal pain. On examination, abdomen was diffuse tenderness, more in the lower quadrants. Bimanual pelvic exam showed bilateral adnexal masses and palpable. A diagnosis of bilateral tubo-ovarian abscesses was entertained. Blood pressure 130/70 mmHg Heart rate 100 /min Respiratory rate 22 /min Temperature 39.9° C Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 39-year-old primigravida attended her routine prenatal visit at 39 weeks'' gestation. She denied any headache, visual changes, nausea, vomiting or abdominal pain. Urinalysis Was Negative for protein (see lab results). Vital signs at this visit: Blood pressure 160/90 mmHg Heart rate 84 /min Vitals signs at all other visits: Blood pressure 120/70 mmHg Heart rate 68 /min Test Result Normal Values HCT 0.34 0.37-0.48 Platelets count 165 150-400 x 109/L Alanine aminotransferase 12 5-40 IU/L Aspartate aminotransferase 22 12-40 IU/L Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old woman who has had last normal pregnancies 15 years ago presents with amenorrhea for 7 months duration. Pregnancy test is negative. She expresses her desire to become pregnant again. Which of the following is most appropriate investigation?

A 25-year-old woman G5 P2022 presented to the clinic at 10 weeks'' gestation with painless vaginal bleeding. During her hospital admission, she developed heavy vaginal bleeding and lower abdominal pain. She has noticed passage of tissue. On examination, mild pain, vital signs normal. Pelvic examination showed trickling blood through cervix and os is open. Which of the following is most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old healthy woman presents to her gynecologist with history of inability to conceive after 2 years of trying. Menarche at 13-year-old, regular cycles every 36-40 days and 5-day periods. Not on any medications. Semenanalysis of husband showed normospermia. Cervical component examination is required. The basal body temperature showed a monophasic pattern. Which of the following is the most probable cause of the infertility?
A 29-year-old woman G1 P0015 is admitted at 30 weeks' gestation in labor room with leaking liquor for observation. Which of the following most likely confirm the diagnosis?

A 28-year-old woman G5 P3013 is seen in the Antenatal Clinic. Her previous pregnancies were term normal deliveries. Currently, she is 10 weeks'' gestation confirmed by a pregnancy test and ultrasound examination. Which of the following is the most important purpose of the first antenatal visit?

A 33-year-old woman presents with a history of amenorrhea for a year following dilation and curettage because of abnormal uterine bleeding. She underwent to both progesterone and combined estrogen and progesterone withdrawal tests with no response. Her serum FSH level was normal. Which of the following syndromes is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman was brought to the Emergency Department 7 weeks after her last menstruation with positive pregnancy test. Abdominal and transvaginal ultrasound showed no intrauterine pregnancy sac. She has lower abdominal pain, mostly on the left side and mild vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most probable diagnosis?

A 30-year-old woman G2 P1002 admitted at 40 weeks' gestation with onset of labor and ruptured membranes. Her cervix was 5 cm dilated and 100% effaced with 0 station vertex for 3 hours. A fetal monitoring strip showed 2 contractions in 10 minutes with a reassured non-stress test. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 22-year-old primigravida presented at 38 weeks'' gestation in labor. Vaginal examination showed 7cm dilated cervix, full effacement and vertex. An hour later the obstetrician noticed a loop of the umbilical cord below the fetal head. Fetal Heart Rate 120 beat/min Which of the following is the most appropriate immediate management?

A child''s developmental assessment conforms the following (see table). Motor Walks alone, crawls up stairs Adaptive Makes a tower of three cubes, makes a line with crayons Language Jargon, follows simple commands Social Indicates desire by pointing Which of the following is the most likely age in months?

A 14-year-old healthy girl has painless vaginal bleeding occurring at interval of 3 weeks to 2 months. The bleeding usually lasts 2 to 4 days, not preceded by premenstrual symptoms and not associated with clots. Review of systems is not significant. Physical examination shows a normal appearing pubertal girl along with normal development of secondary sex characteristics (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hemoglobin 120 112-165 g/L RBC 6 4.6-4.8 x 1012/L WBC 4.8 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Platelets count 220 150-400 x 109/L Pregnancy test Negative Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 37-year-old woman G8 P7017 at 36 weeks' gestation has history of recurrent postpartum that required blood transfusion. She has no significant medical problem. Which of the following is the most appropriate preventive measure at delivery?
option1: Deliver the patient in the operating room
option2: Perform an elective cesarean section at 38 weeks gestation
option3: Perform active management of 3rd stage of labor
option4: Give the patient preload of crystalloid fluids while in labor to overcome the possible post-delivery hypovolemia
correct_option: Perform active management of 3rd stage of labor

question_text: A 40-year-old woman has just delivered her 7th child at 38 weeks with 4 kg baby boy vaginally 10 minutes ago. The placenta is still in place and she starts to bleed profusely. The estimated amount of blood loss visually is around 800 ml. Which of the following best describes this case?

A 38-year-old G9 P8008 woman presented at 38 weeks gestation in labor and diagnosis of prolonged 2nd stage of labor was entertained and finally delivered 4.3 Kg baby by vacuum extraction. Anticipated management was taken because of risk of postpartum hemorrhage and hypovolemic shock. Which of the following is considered a best reliable indicator of above complications?

A woman presents at her 9th week of pregnancy with severe vaginal bleeding, lower abdominal pain and passage of the fetus through the vagina. On vaginal examination; cervix is open and tissue seen in the cervix. Which of the following the most appropriate management?
option1: Fluid replacement and evacuation and curettage
correct_option: Fluid replacement and evacuation and curettage

question_text: A 24-year-old woman presents at 7 weeks' gestation complaining of mild vaginal bleeding and lower abdominal pain colicky in nature. Upon examination; normal vital signs and speculum examination shows the cervix is closed and fresh bleed coming through the cervix. Which of the following is the most likely cause of the bleeding?

A 22-year-old primigravida at 10 weeks'' gestation is brought to Emergency Department complaining of moderate vaginal bleeding with passage of tissue like material and lower abdominal colicky pain. Speculum examination; tissue is in the vagina, uterine cervix is opened and admitting one finger. Product of conception is protruding through cervical canal. Bimanual examination reveals bulky uterus corresponding to seven-eight weeks gestation. Adnexa were free and there was no cervical excitation. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 38-year-old woman G3 P2002 at 34 weeks gestation is admitted to labor and delivery complaining of headache, epigastric pain and blurred vision. Non-stress test is reassuring. Blood pressure 170/120 mmHg Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option1: Inform anesthesia and deliver the patient immediately
option2: Stabilize general condition, administer magnesium sulfate and deliver the patient
option3: Administer magnesium sulfate, stabilize general condition and wait till 37 weeks'' gestation
option4: Stabilize general condition, administer corticosteroids and deliver the patient
correct_option: Stabilize general condition, administer magnesium sulfate and deliver the patient

question_text: A 30-year-old woman G2 P1001 at 32 weeks' gestation is admitted to labor and delivery complaining of lower abdominal and back pain. She has a significant previous history of severe preeclampsia (see lab results). Blood pressure 140/90 mmHg Test Result Normal Values Platelets count 80 150-400 x 109/L Uric Acid 460 140-360 mol/L Which of the following findings would indicate severe preeclampsia?

A 34-year-old primigravida presented to the obstetrician for an initial prenatal visit after self- confirming pregnancy with a home pregnancy test. She complained of breast tenderness and occasional nausea. There were no complaints of urinary frequency or urgency. Urine dipstick analysis was performed and a mid-stream urine sample was obtained (see lab results and report). Test Result Normal Values Nitrites ++ Negative Leukocyte esterase positive Negative Culture Report: Escherichia coli count is 50,000 CFU/ml. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old woman presented with pain, fever, spasm of lower abdominal muscles and profuse purulent vaginal discharge for the past 2 days. On examination, there was suprapubic tenderness. Vaginal examination revealed tenderness In the fornices. Blood pressure 108/56 mmHg Heart rate 110 /min Respiratory rate 14 /min Temperature 38.4 ° C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 23-year-old woman presented with vaginal discharge with an unpleasant smell. Examination confirmed a homogenous grayish-white vaginal discharge that has a high PH. A saline wet-preparation examination of the discharge showed 'clue cells'" and with application of potassium hydroxide a fishy smell was generated. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"

A woman in her last month of pregnancy is diagnosed with right lower lobe pneumonia, Immune response to this infection leads to the formation of specific IgG antibodies. The fetus would be immunologically protected when born owing to which of the following mechanisms?
Which of the following is a developmental screening tool that relies only on information from the parents?
option4: Safety word inventory and literacy screener

question_text: A 21-year-old woman presented with an abnormal vaginal discharge, burning on micturition, and blood spotting for the past 2 months. On genital examination, there was an eroded and friable (strawberry appearance) cervix, with a yellow-green mucopurulent discharge (see report). Lab Testing: >10 PMNs per oil immersion field. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 20-year-old woman has a history of 3 first trimester abortions. She was diagnosed last year with pulmonary embolism (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Prothrombin Time 11 10-13 sec APTT 69 30-40 sec Platelets count 320 150-400 × 109/L Lupus anticoagulant positive Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 20-year-old nulliparous woman at 20 weeks' gestation has been on iron supplement therapy throughout her pregnancy to treat anemia. She complained of weakness and becoming easily tired (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 78 120-160 g/L MCV 60 80-95 fL RBC 5.4 4.2-5.4 x 1012/L Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 34-year-old woman G5 P4004 presented with amenorrhea, tiredness, nausea and vomiting during the morning hours for 3 months. Human chorionic gonadotropin test was positive. A physical examination showed no swelling in the lower extremities. The thyroid was enlarged on palpation. The tongue, conjunctiva and nail beds appeared pale. The uterine fundus was palpable 3 cm above the pubic symphysis. A peripheral blood smear showed macrocytic red blood cells and hypersegmented neutrophils (see lab results). Blood pressure 100/60 mmHg Heart rate 120 /min Respiratory rate 26 /min Temperature 37.0* C oral Test Result Normal Values Hb 85 120-160 g/L HCT 0.32 0.37-0.48 MCHC 332 320-360 g/L MCV 100 80-95 fL Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone 0.44 0.4 - 5.0 pU/L Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 48-year-old woman has had 2 inflammatory pap smears 3 months apart and a normal pelvic examination. A colposcopically directed biopsy of a normal appearing cervix revealed invasive carcinoma. Which of the following is the best next step in management?

A 22-year-old diabetic woman presented with vaginal discharge and pruritus. The discharge is thick, grey and odorless. On physical examination, her vulva was erythematous and swollen. The vagina has a white-grey discharge that is adherent to the mucosa. Microscopic examination of the discharge confirmed the presence of spores. Which of the following is the most likely pathogen?

A 27-year-old woman presented with vaginal itching and an unpleasant smelling discharge. Itching gets worse at night and after intercourse. Physical examination confirmed a frothy and yellow-green vaginal discharge. Her vulva is erythematous and edematous and the cervix is inflamed and friable (see report). Wet Preparation: Flagellated protozoa. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman had severe pelvic inflammatory disease 2 years ago that was treated by intravenous antibiotics. The isolated organism was chlamydia trachomatis. Since then the woman was trying for pregnancy but failed to conceive. She had normal regular period and her husband had a normal seminal analysis. Hysterosalpingogram revealed normal uterine cavity and no free spillage of the injected dye bilaterally (see lab results). Body mass index 35 kg/m2 Test Result Normal Values Follicle-stimulating hormone 4.5 5-20 IU/L Luteinizing hormone 3.9 5-22 U/L Which of the following is the most appropriate method of management?
option2: Induction of ovulation followed by normal intercourse
option3: Induction of ovulation followed by intrauterine insemination
option4: Weight reduction to reach body mass index of 23 kg/m2

question_text: A 25-year-old woman had severe pelvic inflammatory disease 2 years ago that was treated by intravenous antibiotics. The isolated organism was chlamydia trachomatis. Since then the patient was trying for pregnancy but failed to do so. She had normal regular period and normal seminal analysis of her husband. Which of the following investigations is recommended?
option2: Magnetic resonance image of the pelvis

question_text: A 38-year-old woman presented with dysuria and increased frequency of urination. History reveals, she is allergic to sulfa, penicillin, and shellfish (see report). Blood Pressure 120/80 mmHg Heart Rate 80 /min Respiratory Rate 16 /min Temperature 38.0° C Urine Dipstick: positive for leukocyte esterase. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A patient comes for a follow-up 2 weeks after a vaginal hysterectomy with anterior and posterior repair; the patient had leakage of urine from the vagina during micturition. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 56-year-old nulliparous and menopausal for 4 years, presented to the clinic with intermittent vaginal bleeding for the last 4 weeks. The bleeding was scanty and bright in color. Pelvic examination shows healthy vagina and cervix with a small amount of blood in the cervical os. Which of the following is the next step in management?

A 30-year-old para 3 woman presented with a significant history of smoking 2 packs of per week for the past 7 years. She was using a progestin-only pills. She has had annual Papanicolaou examinations for the past 9 years and all results have been normal. Bimanual pelvic examination showed normal findings. Papanicolaou smear repeated with results of low-grade epithelial lesions. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

When a mother placed her infant on the examining table, the infant laughs out loudly. As the doctor approaches him, he squeals and reaches for his mother. What is the most likely developmental month for this child?

A 68-year-old woman underwent a total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. 3 days following the surgery, urinary catheter was removed and she was able to independently void 1 hour with an output of 35 ml/hr. She has been receiving fluids by continuous intravenous drip. She described the incisional pain as uncomfortable and rates it at a level 5 on the pain assessment scale. For pain relief, she was taking ibuprofen 800 milligrams every 6 hours. The doctor considered her ready for discharge (see lab results). Blood pressure 118/68 mmHg Heart rate 70 /min Respiratory rate 20 /min Temperature 37.4° C oral Test Result Normal Values HCT 0.37 0.37-0.48 Hb 122 120-160 g/L Which of the following would be a reason for this patient to remain in hospital?

A 48-year-old asymptomatic woman attended the Gynecology Clinic for her Pap smear screening. During pelvic examination, pelvic mass was felt, further assessment by transvaginal ultrasonography indicated the presence of solitary uterine fibroid on the uterine fundus measuring 5 x 6 cm. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option3: Regular follow up with yearly pelvic ultrasound
option4: Regular follow up with pelvic ultrasound and complete blood count every two months
correct_option: Regular follow up with yearly pelvic ultrasound

question_text: A 35-year-old woman delivered her fifth baby by vacuum extraction due to prolonged second stage of labor 3 months ago. The woman was complaining of leakage of urine when she coughed, sneezed or laughed. The symptoms have started after delivery and persisted since then. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A post-menopausal woman undergoing tamoxifen treatment for breast cancer, she had her last gynecological assessment by ultrasonography during her premenopausal period 5 years ago. At that time, she had intramural uterine fibroid measuring 2 × 3 cm. The patient complained of on and off moderate amount of vaginal bleeding and her ultrasound confirmed intramural uterine fibroid measuring 5 x 8 cm and homogenous endometrium 5 mm in thickness. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 39-year-old woman, who is a mother of 3 children and has completed her family, she is a known case of endometrioma in the right ovary that has been removed 2 years ago. She came to the clinic complaining of mild to moderate dysmenorrhea and deep pain during intercourse along with her chronic lower abdomen pain. Pelvic ultrasonography revealed the presence of left ovarian cyst measuring 6X7 cm that appeared to be an endometriotic cyst. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?
option1: Removing the cyst when the size becomes more than 10 cm
option2: Aspiration of the content of cyst under ultrasound guidance
option3: Total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy
option4: Removing the cyst laparoscopically with ablation of any endometriotic spot
correct_option: Removing the cyst laparoscopically with ablation of any endometriotic spot

question_text: A 38-year-old woman attended Outpatient Clinic complaining of fresh vaginal bleeding occurring after each intercourse. This problem has been disturbing her over the last 3 months. Upon examination, her speculum examination revealed normal cervix. The report of Pap smear is showing squamous cell carcinoma. Which of the following is the most appropriate next immediate action?

25-year-old woman complained of recurrent behavioral symptoms that started few days before her menstrual cycles and usually disappear around the second day of the cycle. These symptoms have led to reduced work productivity, absenteeism from work, and increased visits to ambulatory healthcare. Which of the following is the most reliable method to establish the diagnosis?

A 35-year-old healthy woman attended the Gynecological Clinic for her regular Pap smear screening. Upon speculum pelvic examination, the uterine cervix appeared raised irregular mass measuring 10 X 12 mm localized on the cervix left lateral aspect. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?
option4: Reassurance until the pap smear result

question_text: A primigravida woman at 32 weeks'' gestation brought to the Emergency Room with vaginal bleeding and abdominal pain. On examination: Fundal height equals to 30 weeks and abdomen is tense. Cardiotocography revealed fetal tachycardia, reduced variability and late decelerations. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman complained of recurrent behavioral symptoms that started few days before her menstrual cycles and usually disappeared around the second day of the cycle. These symptoms have led to reduced work productivity, increased work absenteeism, and increased visits to ambulatory healthcare. What is the most common affective or behavioral symptom for her diagnosis?
A 53-year-old woman has missed her period over the last 12 months. Which of the following hormones is likely to be altered as a result of the condition?

A 45-year-old woman has undergone a total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo- oophorectomy for a benign reason. Immediately post operation, she started to have acute vasomotor symptoms. Accordingly, she was counselled for hormone replacement therapy and agreed. Which of the following is the most appropriate hormone replacement therapy regimens?

A 19-year-old girl complained of recurrent painful menstruation that starts on day one of her menses and subsides between day 3 to 4. The pain is severe enough to affect her daily activities and school attendance. All investigations were Negative. She was started on non-steroid anti-inflammatory drugs, her pain severity became less and she was able to resume normal function during menses. The patient is still not satisfied and insisting to receive a stronger pain killer. Which of the following is an appropriate management?

A 25-year-old woman complained of crampy, lower abdominal pain that occurs during the first day of her menses and usually improves by the third day. Her periods is regular and all clinical assessment and pelvic ultrasound within normal. Which of the following most likely explains her clinical presentation?

A 3-year-old girl presents to the Emergency Department with vaginal bleeding and pain. Her mother reports that has been passing stools in her pants for 2 weeks despite being toilet trained. On examination, there is bruises on with blood staining labia majora (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 120 112-165 g/L WBC 9.4 4-10.5 x 109/L Platelet count 344 150-400 × 109/L Prothrombin time 13 12-14 sec APTT 36 30-40 sec Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 32-year-old woman presents with smelly vaginal discharge associated with intense itching. Her husband has history of slight urethral discharge. A pelvic examination confirmed strawberry spots on the cervix. Which one of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 25-year-old woman presented to the clinic with an unpleasant-smelling discharge. A speculum examination showed vaginal discharge, grey-green in color. The cervix was covered with papillae with light-red color. A specimen of vaginal secretions was obtained for a wet-prep microscopy which showed unicellular organisms with flagella. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 28-year-old woman at 24 weeks'' gestation presented to the clinic for a routine examination. A pelvic examination showed a watery grey-colored discharge, fish-like smell. There were no signs of inflammation of the vagina or cervix. She reported some itching and moderate discomfort. The vaginal pH 5.1. A sample of the vaginal discharge was collected for analysis. Which of the following is the most likely finding?

A 26-year-old nulliparous patient presented to the clinic with vaginal itching and increased vaginal discharge. She had been diagnosed with a positive pregnancy test 3 months ago. A pelvic examination showed a yellow-white discharge at the vaginal introitus. The labia were inflamed and covered with scratch marks. The vaginal discharge was fishy smell ph 4.8. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 35-year-old woman G4 P2012 at 26 weeks'' gestation, uneventful pregnancy presented for a 50-gram oral glucose tolerance test. 1 hour later by finger stick sample glucose was 8.1 mmol/L. Which vaginal infection is she at the highest risk of contracting?

A 58-year-old multiparous woman is complaining of urine leakage at laugh or cough. She is not able to hold her urine along with feeling of 'dragging'" or '"balloon-like'" sensation in the genital area for the past 4 months. Physical examination is normal except for bulging of the lower anterior vaginal wall. Which of the following is the most likely cause of those symptoms?"

A 30-year-old married woman has had an abnormal Papanicolaou test result. Repeated smears were suggestive of high-grade intraepithelial lesions. Pelvic and rectal examinations were normal. Which of the following is the next step in management?

A 24-year-old married woman presented with severe abdominal pain followed by vaginal bleeding for the last 6 hours. She reported having amenorrhea for the past 8 weeks as well. The patient looked drowsy with a tense abdomen. Blood pressure 90/60mmHg Heart rate 120 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6° C Which of the following is the most likely source of bleeding?

A 23-year-old woman presented with a yellowish green malodorous vaginal discharge. Speculum exam showed the cervix was slightly red and inflamed. A probe for chlamydia was collected and wet prep confirmed Trichomonas vaginalis. Which of the following is the first step in management?

A 28-year-old woman presented with a history of a copious white vaginal discharge associated with intense vulvar irritation and itching for four days. Microscopic examination revealed evidence of pseudohyphae. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A woman comes to the clinic for having a history of Increasing pelvic pain, which is cyclic in nature and increasing in intensity just before menses. Which of the following is an acceptable method for diagnosing endometriosis?

A 67-year-old woman presented with vaginal itching and burning. Currently, she is only on calcium supplements. Physical examination revealed thinning and pallor of the vaginal mucosa and no discharge. Wet prep from the scant material present was Negative for any organisms. Which of the following is most appropriate initial treatment?
A young adult was being treated with Ceftriaxone for acute salpingitis and pelvic inflammatory disease without benefit. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 34-year-old woman non-smoker with 2 previous normal deliveries presented for a routine pap smear and her last pap was normal 3 years ago. The patient has had 1 episode of venereal warts at 18 year-old with no recurrence. The rest of her history is not significant (see report). Pap smear: Atypical squamous cells of undetermined significance. What will be the first step in management?
Which of the following organs is most commonly affected by mumps in a 4-year-old boy?

A 21-year-old patient complained of severe vulvovaginal itching and discharge. There are no urinary or other symptoms, and annual papanicolaou tests have all been normal. On inspection, Vulvar erythema with swelling. Speculum examination revealed a thick, white, curdy discharge adherent to the vaginal walls with no odor. A gross and microscopic examination of the vaginal discharge was done; the wet mount revealed no evidence of clue cells or trichomonas and the KOH prepared slide confirmed several pseudohyphae (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values pH 4.0 3.8-4.5 Whiff test Negative Negative Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 23-year-old woman who just recently had an intrauterine device fitted, complaining of watery brown vaginal discharge and abdominal pain. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 21-year-old patient presented to the clinic complaining of diffuse abdominal pain for the past 3 days. Her history included dyspareunia, nausea, and several episodes of vomiting. On examination, external genitalia and cervix appeared normal. However, the patient reported cervical motion, adnexal, and lower abdominal tenderness. Blood pressure 120/60 mmHg Heart rate 72 /min Respiratory rate 16 /min Temperature 38.4° C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 56-year-old postmenopausal woman presents with a 1-year history of recurrent irritation in the pubic area. The patient reported itchiness with blood-stained discharge at times. The patient has tried a metronidazole suppositories as well as topical cream over the labia with little relief. Recently, the patient feels a pea-sized lump on the labia. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 48-year-old woman patient suffers from several episodes of vaginal discharge and itching around the genital area. The discharge is white and curd-like. The patient's spouse does not have any related condition. Microscopy of the discharge revealed budding yeast and pseudohyphae. The patient's symptoms might be associated with which of the following conditions?

A 33-year-old woman comes to the clinic complaining of recent onset of vaginal itching. Physical examination of the vagina revealed a thick, odorless white cottage cheese-like vaginal discharge that adheres to the mucosa. She has recently finished a course of antibiotics for an upper respiratory tract infection. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman complained of swelling on one side of the labia. The swelling was first noticed 24 hours ago and has not improved despite warm tub soaking. There was a non-tender 3 cm slightly mobile cystic mass at approximately 7 o'clock on the right side of the vaginal introitus. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 37° C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A patient who had a vaginal delivery 3 months ago is complaining of fecal incontinence and passage of vaginal flatus. These symptoms have started 1 week postpartum. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old woman's recent pap smear yielded a result of Atypical squamous cells of undetermined significance (ASC-US). She was given topical vaginal estrogen for one month and was scheduled for another pap smear a week after the treatment cycle has completed. The second pap smear showed ASC-US as well. Which of the following is the most appropriate next investigation?

A 57-year-old menopausal woman, presents for evaluation of post-menopausal bleeding. She is morbidly obese, hypertensive and diabetic. Endometrial sampling showed complex endometrial hyperplasia with atypia. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 55-year-old woman presented to the clinic with urinary leakage for the last 6 weeks. She cannot hold her urine and urine leaks even before she reaches the bathroom. History revealed she has been menopause for the last 6 years. Which of the following is the first step in the evaluation?

A 29-year-old woman complaining of a heavy vaginal discharge for the past 2 weeks. The discharge was thin and a greyish white color with a slight fishy vaginal odor. On examination, a copious, thin, whitish discharge is seen adherent to the vaginal wall. Vaginal pH 5.5 Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 67-year-old woman attended the Gynecological Clinic for the follow up of lichen sclerosus diagnosed few years earlier. Upon examination, a small mass measuring 0.5 x 0.5 cm was identified on her right labia majora. Punch biopsy was taken from the lesion and sent for histopathology. Which of the following is the most likely histopathological finding?

A 65-year-old post-menopausal woman complains of on and off vaginal bleeding that started 2 months ago. The amount of bleeding is mild to moderate of fresh blood. The woman is diabetic, hypertensive and obese. She has used hormone replacement therapy to control post- menopausal symptoms. Body mass index 37 kg/m2 Which of the following investigations is essential to establish the diagnosis?

A 25-year-old woman G5 P2022 presented to the clinic at 10 weeks'' gestation with painless vaginal bleeding. During her hospital admission, she developed heavy vaginal bleeding and lower abdominal pain. She has noticed passage of tissue. On examination, mild pain, vital signs normal. Pelvic examination showed trickling blood through cervix and os is open. Which of the following is most likely diagnosis?

A 24-year-old married nulliparous has an 18-month history of cyclic, debilitating pelvic pain related to menses which are regular and heavy requiring 10-15 thick pads on the days of heaviest flow. For the past year, the patient has been engaging in regular intercourse without contraception in an attempt to conceive. Pelvic examination showed normal-size, immobile, retroverted uterus with nodularity and tenderness on palpation of the uterosacral ligaments. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 27-year-old nulliparous presents for a routine examination. Her gynecological history indicates a 5-year history of oligomenorrhea with only about 2-3 menses a year. She denies inter-cycle spotting or premenstrual symptoms. The last menses was 3 months ago. The physical examination confirmed a moderate amount of facial hair and inflammatory acne while the pelvic examination is normal. Weight 60 kg, Blood pressure 120/75 mmHg, Heart rate 72 /min, Respiratory rate 17 /min. which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 27-year-old nulliparous is concerned about not having conceived after a year of regular, unprotected intercourse. The patient denied any major medical illnesses and takes no medications. The patient''s spouse is reported to be healthy but has never fathered a child. What is the most appropriate initial step in evaluation after history and examination?

A 25-year-old nulliparous presented with an 18-month history of cyclic and debilitating pelvic pain related to menses. Menses is regular and heavy, requiring 10-15 thick pads on the days of heaviest flow. The patient has been engaging in regular intercourse without contraception for 1 year in an attempt to conceive. Pelvic examination revealed a normal-sized, immobile, retroverted uterus with nodularity and tenderness on palpation of the uterosacral ligaments. Which of the following would most likely help in the diagnosis?

A 48-year-old woman nulliparous presented to the Emergency Department with heavy vaginal bleeding associated with slight dizziness. No other significant past medical history except heavy menstruation in the past few months. The examination was normal apart from the presence of a large amount of blood in the vaginal vault (see lab results). Supine: Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 90 /min Standing: Blood pressure 104/66 mmHg, Heart rate 100 /min Temperature 36.6° C Test Result Normal Values Hb 10 120-160 g/L Pregnancy test -ve Which of the following is the first step in management?

A 41-year-old patient presents to the Gynecological Clinic with heavy bleeding. After a complete evaluation, the gynecologist determines the patient has dysfunctional uterine bleeding. Which of the following is the first step in management?

A 14-year-old girl has a 6-month history of lower mid-abdominal pain colicky in nature, radiates to the back and upper thighs. It often begins with onset of menses which lasts for 2-4 days. She has missed several days of school during the last 2 months because the severity of pain. Physical examination is normal for abdomen and pelvis. Normal secondary sexual development is also noted. Which of the following is the first step in management?

A 23-year-old woman presented with breast tenderness, abdominal bloating, and food cravings. She has a 6-month history of tiredness, anxiety, emotional lability, difficulty concentrating and insomnia. There are no menstrual irregularities or prodromal life stressors. The symptoms recurred on a regular basis during the week leading up to the menstrual period but completely resolve within the first 3 days of menses. The patient felt totally incapacitated when symptomatic, and the symptoms adversely affect personal and professional life. Which of the following is the best-evidenced treatment?

A 22-year-old married woman has had severe, recurrent depression associated with multiple suicide attempts. She has been stabilized for the past 3 months on paroxetine and is now pregnant. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option1: Discontinue paroxetine due to avoid prematurity
option2: Continue paroxetine and monitor for depression
option3: Discontinue paroxetine to avoid fetal damage
correct_option: Continue paroxetine and monitor for depression

question_text: A 32-year-old primigravida at 34 weeks' gestation presents for urgent care after falling down a flight of stairs. The patient complains of uterine tenderness and reduced fetal movement in the 45 minutes since the fall. There has been a small amount of dark red vaginal bleeding. The fetal heart monitoring revealed a heart rate of 150 bpm with irregular uterine contractions every 3 to 4 minutes. Her pregnancy has been uncomplicated. She has positive history of cigarettes smoking. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 34-week pregnant woman presents in labor with contractions occurring every 4 minutes, she also has vaginal bleeding which is heavier than normal menstrual flow. The fetus is in a back-down position, transverse lie, and the cervix is dilated 3 cm with bulging membranes. The fetal heart rate is 170 bpm. The sonography showed a posterior fundal placenta with a retroplacental sonolucent area. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial management?

A 40-year-old sexually active patient presents for cervical cancer screening. Physical examination is unremarkable, menstruation is regular with a 28-day cycle and last menstrual period was 5 days ago. Which of the following is the best advice about Pap smear?
option4: Three negative results and no further screening

question_text: A 21-year-old primigravida is currently at 20 weeks gestation. Her past medical history is significant for mitral stenosis secondary to rheumatic heart disease as a child. What physiologic change places her at risk for the development of heart failure during pregnancy?

A 42-year-old obese nulligravida patient with polycystic ovary syndrome has had irregular vaginal bleeding since menarche. She has used no medication to control her periods (see lab results and report). Test Result Normal Values Cortisol 8 a.m. 335 138-635 mol/L Estrogen 630 90-550 pmol/L Follicle-stimulating hormone 38 5-20 IU/L Testosterone 3.1 0.7-2.8 mol/L Prolactin 590 < 870 pmol/L T3 (Total serum) 1.2 0.9-2.8 mol/L Thyroxine (T4 Total serum) 82 64-155 mol/L Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone 4.5 0.4 - 5.0 uU/L Endometrial Biopsy: Adenomatous hyperplasia. Which of the following is the most likely cause of the adenomatous hyperplasia?

A 42-year-old obese woman presented with a 5-year history of irregular vaginal bleeding. Thyroid studies are normal. She noticed that her irregular in menstruation has started to put on weight (see report). Body mass index 35 kg/m2 Endometrial Biopsy: Adenomatous hyperplasia. Which of the following is the most likely cause?
option3: Local genetic mutation of the endometrium
option4: Peripheral conversion of precursors to estrogen
correct_option: Peripheral conversion of precursors to estrogen

question_text: A 43-year-old woman gravida 2 para 2 has had oligoovulation due to polycystic ovary syndrome and used cyclic progesterone for withdrawal bleeding. Approximately 5 years ago, she discontinued taking progesterone. Which of the following risks is increased?

A 30-year-old woman P1+0 presented to Obstetrics and Gynecology Clinic complains of inability to conceive for the last 3 years. During that time, she was not on contraceptive and her menstrual periods every 45 days but no period for the last 6 months (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone 9.2 µU/L 0.4 - 5.0 µU/L Follicular phase 5-20 IU/L Mid cycle peak 15-35 IU/L Follicle-stimulating hormone 5.3 µU/L Luteal phase 5-15 IU/L Postmenopausal women 50-100 IU/L Men 5-20 IU/L Men 3-15 µU/L Luteinizing hormone 7.2 µU/L Follicular phase 5-22 µU/L Luteal phase 5-22 µU/L Ovulatory phase 30-250 µU/L Postmenopausal Women > 30 µU/L Prolactin 140 ng/ml < 25 ng/ml Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A young primigravida presents with significant edema of the lower extremities and hands. The patient was admitted for evaluation and management. Blood pressure 150/95 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18/min Temperature 36.6° C Which of the following would be the most appropriate management?
A 30-year-old woman with a history of infertility for 6 years complains of severe dysmenorrhea unrelieved by naproxen. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 40-year-old woman complains of Post-coital bleeding. Speculum examination reveals cervical mass. Which of the following is the most appropriate next investigation?

A 48-year-old obese woman complains of nausea. Patient LMP was 2 months ago. She is using barrier method (condoms) as a birth control method. Examination is normal except for a slight dusky discoloration of her cervix. Which of the following is the next step in management?

A hemodynamically stable woman has a tubal, ectopic pregnancy measuring 2.5 x 3 cm by ultrasound (see lab result). Test Result Normal Value Human chorionic gonadotropin N 5000 < 5 mlU/mL. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 25-year-old woman in her first trimester of pregnancy has come for a routine prenatal visit. She used to smoke one pack of cigarettes per day, and is now being advised to quit smoking. Which of the following is the greatest risk to her unborn child?

A 32-year-old woman 16 weeks pregnant presents to the clinic with vaginal bleeding, abdominal enlargement and vomiting for the past 4 days. On examination, the uterine size is larger than expected for the gestational age (see lab result and report). Test Result Normal Value Human chorionic gonadotropin 80,000 < 5 mlU/mL Ultrasound: 'Snowstorm-like'" appearance. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"

A 30-year-old woman presents to the Gynecology Clinic with diffuse lower right-sided abdominal pain and low back pain that has been progressively worsening for the past month. The pain intensity worsens after sexual intercourse. Patient menstrual periods occur irregularly and with heavy bleeding. Palpation reveals a tender mass in the lower right quadrant. Ultrasound pelvis scan shows a 9X9 centimetre heterogeneous mass filled with calcifications. Which of the following is most appropriate method of treatment?

A 28-year-old gravida 3, para 2 presents to Obstetric Triage Unit at 37 weeks'' gestation following spontaneous rupture of the amniotic membranes confirmed by physical examination. On admission to labour and delivery room she was complaining of abdominal pain. Digital examination shows a cervical dilation of 4 cm and 80% effacement with the presenting part at 0 station. CTG is reactive with baseline fetal heart of 170 bpm.
