Blood pressure 138/78 mmHg Heart rate 111 /min Respiratory rate 24 /min Temperature 38.4° C oral Which of the following is the next step in management?

A 24-year-old multiparous woman is in the second stage of labour. She has a history of asthma and she uses an albuterol inhaler as needed. She has a prior herpes simplex virus infection and reports outbreaks about once per year. As a child, she contracted rheumatic fever and takes prophylactic antibiotics when needed for mitral valve stenosis. Which significant medical history indicates beneficial use of forceps?

A 20-year-old primigravida at 39 weeks presents in labor and she progresses well. During the delivery, it is necessary to cut an episiotomy. However, the tear extends through the rectal mucosa. What is the classification of this type of perineal tear?

A 30-year-old woman gravida 4, para 3 woman presents to Antenatal Clinic at 32 weeks'' gestation with headache and swelling of the feet. At the initial antenatal visit, there was +2 pitting edema of the lower extremities and the face appears swollen. Dipsick of the urine shows proteinuria. The fundal height is 28 cm and fetal heart rate was 140-150 beats per minute with good variability. She reports regular fetal movements. Blood pressure 150/100 mmHg, Heart rate 90 /min, Respiratory rate 26 /min, Temperature 37.1° C Which additional clinical finding would be associated with intrauterine growth restriction?

A nulliparous woman is admitted to the hospital at 42 weeks'' gestation for induction of labour with cervical prostaglandin gel. On admission, a pelvic examination shows the cervix is closed, thick and posterior facing. A non-stress test shows the fetal heart rate is 130-140 beats per minute with good variability. There are 3 accelerations and 2 fetal movements during the examination. 1 hour after applying the prostaglandin gel, the fetal heart tones are 80 beats per minute and the contractions last for two minutes. Which of the following intervention is the most important?
option1: Administer oxygen at 10 L/min by facemask
option2: Prepare for an urgent caesarean section

question_text: A 25-year-old nulliparous woman at 37 weeks' gestation presents to the hospital with regular contractions and spontaneous rupture of membranes. A pelvic examination shows cervical dilation of 6 cm and 90% effacement. Fetal heart rate 130-140 bpm with good variability. Ultrasound examination shows the fetus in a breech position and the patient is admitted for a caesarean section. When would be the optimal time to administer intravenous antibiotics?
option2: Upon closure of the skin wound

question_text: A 30-year-old woman presented to the Emergency Department at 03:00 o''clock with right-sided abdominal pain in the lower quadrant. She rates the pain at a level 9 on the 1-10 pain scale. The pain was sudden, associated with nausea and vomiting. A human chorionic gonadotropin urine test was negative. On palpation, there is tenderness and a mass is noted. An ultrasound confirms a mass of 6 cm diameter. Blood pressure 120/70 mmHg Heart rate 110 /min Respiratory rate 20 /min Temperature 37.0° C oral Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 34-year-old woman presented to the hospital 7 days before with right-sided lower abdominal pain. In addition, she had slight bright red vaginal bleeding. A blood serum sample of B-human chorionic gonadotropin was 2900 IU/Liter. She was administered intramuscular methotrexate. A week later, she reported to the hospital with severe right-sided lower abdominal pain. A blood serum sample of B-human chorionic gonadotropin was drawn and shown to be 6,000 IU/Liter. Which of the following would be most appropriate?
A 34-year-old multiparous woman presents at 37 weeks' twins gestation with regular contractions. Which of the following fetal presentations is favourable for vaginal delivery?

A 26-year-old primigravida woman presented to the clinic at 37 weeks'' gestation for a routine antenatal care. She reports feeling ill and has a frontal headache. A urine dipstick analysis is performed and shows proteinuria 2+. She is admitted to the hospital High Risk Unit and started on an intravenous infusion of magnesium sulfate. Blood pressure 152/102 mmHg, Heart rate 72 /min, Respiratory rate 16 /min, Temperature 37.2° C oral. Which of the following is the primary reason for administering the intravenous medication?

A 28-year-old primigravida 8 weeks gestation woman attended her first antenatal appointment. During the history assessment, she reports that she never had chickenpox. A serum blood titre is drawn and is negative for antibodies. Which of the following is the next best step in management?

A 34-year-old gravida 2 para 1 woman 10 weeks gestation known to have a hypertension taking labetalol 100 milligrams twice per day. In her first pregnancy had no complications and resulted in a healthy infant. During this pregnancy which of the following risks is increased?

A multiparous woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic at 37 weeks' gestation with regular contractions occurring every 2 minutes lasting for 45-60 seconds with moderate intensity. Last pregnancy ended up with low transverse incision caesarean section. She is now pregnant with twins. Twin A is breech and twin B is cephalic. There has been small amount of clear fluid leaking from the vagina for the past hour. A nitrazine paper test turns blue-green in color. Pelvic examination shows a cervical dilation of 4 cm and 80% effacement. The obstetrician informs her that a caesarean section is necessary. Which of the following is the most likely reason for a caesarean section?

A 35-year-old nulliparous woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic at 24 weeks'' gestation. The significant medical history includes insulin-dependent diabetes class F with nephritis. Additionally, she has chronic hypertension which is controlled with medication. The pregnancy has been uncomplicated so far. The uterine fundus measured 25 cm above the symphysis and fetal heart tones show a baseline of 160 beats per minute. Which of the following complications is most likely to occur?

A 30-year-old para 1 woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic at 20 days postpartum. She has delivered a full-term girl vaginally by forceps-assisted delivery. The weight at birth is 3.54 kg. She presents with a yellow-white colored vaginal discharge. The discharge is odorless and there is no itching or discomfort. An examination shows a pink cervix and absence of uterine tenderness. A microscopic analysis of the vaginal fluid is positive for leukocytes and epithelial cells. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A 66-year-old man presents with shortness of breath. He smokes 20 cigarettes/day but is otherwise well. Spirometry shows a restrictive picture. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 22-year-old woman attends the Women''s Health Clinic for a postnatal examination. She delivered a full-term baby boy 6 weeks earlier and the infant is exclusively breastfeeding and shows a good latch and weight gain. She would like to have more children, but not within the next 2 years. She does not smoke cigarettes and not at risk for heart disease. She inquired about birth control methods. Which of the following methods is most appropriate?

A 40-year-old woman underwent a laparoscopic tubal ligation during the postpartum period following the delivery of her 4th child 4 years ago. She presents to the Maternal Health Clinic complaining of bright red vaginal spotting. Patient last menstrual cycle was 6 weeks ago. A pelvic examination shows fullness over the right adnexa. There is no pain or tenderness. Her last pregnancy resulted in an emergency caesarean section with a mid-line incision. Which of the following is the best next step in management?

A 24-year-old nulliparous woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic in the evening at 34 weeks' gestation and complaining of decreased fetal movement. She says she had been at work all day and then came directly to the hospital when she noticed that the baby has not been moving as much as usual. There has been no leaking of amniotic fluid. There are no uterine contractions and also no vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A gravida 2 para 0 woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic reporting that she has not menstruated for the past 2 months. The previous pregnancy had resulted in a stillbirth at 34 weeks' gestation. A human chorionic gonadotropin test is now positive. She has been trying to conceive for the past 6 months. She is afraid of having another stillbirth and asks to be vaccinated against everything. Which of the following vaccines is most appropriate to administer?

A 30-year-old woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic with the inability to conceive despite trying for the past 18 months. Patient menstrual cycles were regular until 2 years ago, and since then she has had irregular vaginal bleeding. There is hair covering the upper lip and chin. A pelvic examination confirms bilaterally enlarged ovaries. DHEA and 17-OH progesterone values are normal. Body mass index 30 kg/m2 Which of the following laboratory values is most likely finding?
option4: A Follicle Stimulating Hormone to Leutenizing Hormone ratio of 1:3
correct_option: A Follicle Stimulating Hormone to Leutenizing Hormone ratio of 1:3

question_text: A 20-year-old woman presented to the Maternal Health Clinic with severe facial acne and irregular menstrual bleeding. Examination shows fine hair growth on the chin, upper back, breasts and lower abdomen. A pregnancy test is returned negative. She returns to the clinic on the 5th day of her menstrual cycle and has a serum sample drawn for analysis (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Follicle-stimulating hormone 10 5-20 IU/L Luteinizing hormone 28 5-22 U/L Prolactin 890 < 870 pmol/L Testosterone 3.2 0.7-2.8 mol/L Which additional laboratory values should be obtained?
option1: Thyroid stimulating hormone and free T3 and T4

question_text: A paediatrician is called to attend a caesarean section delivery of 38 week''s gestation baby. On delivery, the neonate does not cry immediately, appears cyanotic and floppy. After drying, suctioning and stimulating the baby under the radiant warmer, the following evidence is recorded at 5 minutes (see table). Heart rate 120 beats per minute Breathing Irregular, gasping Reflex irritability Coughs, and cries with stimulation Activity Arms and legs flexed, little movement Appearance Rosy with blue extremities What is the most accurate Apgar score?

A 31-year-old woman P2 presented to the Women''s Health Clinic for a routine physical examination. She has presented to routine health examinations annually for the last 5 years. Records indicate that in the previous year, she has negative tests for both the human papilloma virus and the papanicolaou cervical smear. This year both tests are with no abnormal findings. When should the tests be repeated?
A patient is seen at 8 weeks' gestation in an Antenatal Clinic with a diagnosis of cervical incompetence. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option1: Insertion cervical suture the same week
option2: Confirm the diagnosis by Hegar dilator

question_text: A 15-year-old unmarried girl presents with history of pain during menstruation. Her menarche was at age of 13 and menses are regular. Physical and pelvic examinations are normal for her age. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 23-year-old nulligravida patient complains of inability to become pregnant after 3 months of trying. Patient periods are regular and normal. She had no prior reproductive problems. She currently is not on any contraception. Examination is normal. Her husband is 25-year-old healthy and has one autistic cousin and one with trisomy 21. Which of the following is the best action?

A 24-year-old married woman presented to the clinic with severe abdominal pain followed by vaginal bleeding, for the last 6 hours. Patient''s LMP was 8 weeks ago. On examination: she is drowsy and has a tense abdomen. Blood pressure 90/60 mmHg Heart rate 120 /min Respiratory rate 20 /min Temperature 36.8 ° C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 24-year-old woman presents to the clinic with complaining of failure to conceive for more than 2 years of regular trials. Patient has history of irregular periods occurring once every 6 to 7 weeks and is usually scanty (see report). Body mass index 35 kg/m2 Transvaginal ultrasound: Revealed the presence of multiple small cysts; each is 3-5mm, lining the ovarian periphery, representing pearl necklace appearance. which of the following is the most likely factor contributing to her infertility?
How many times the risk of lung cancer is increased in smokers compared to nonsmokers?

A 34-year-old multiparous woman presents to the Maternal Health Clinic at 28 weeks'' gestation with coughing and difficulty in breathing. She appeared restless and uncomfortable. Auscultation of the lung fields shows bilateral rhonchi. The nail beds and oral mucous membranes appear pale. She is allergic to dust, pollen, mold and animal hair. There is no history of smoking. She reports that the symptoms began 2 days prior and had started with coughing up clear-colored phlegm. Blood pressure 124/72 mmHg Heart rate 120 /min Respiratory rate 24 /min Temperature 37.5° C oral Which of the following tests is initially most appropriate?

A 28-year-old woman with mild intermittent asthma presents to the Health Clinic at 24 weeks'' gestation and says that her symptoms have worsened as the pregnancy progresses. Prior to the pregnancy, she had an occasional exacerbation of asthma and used only an albuterol inhaler for treatment as needed. However, she now complains of daily symptoms and once per week symptoms at night time. A forced expiratory volume in 1 second test shows 70% of normal. Which of the following medication regimen is now most appropriate?
option4: Long acting beta2 agonist and inhaled glucocorticoids

question_text: A 28-year-old woman G 4 P2012 immigrant presented to the Maternal Health Clinic at 20 weeks' gestation with a cough and night chills for the past month. The cough is productive and produces thick sputum. She had been prescribed treatment by a general practitioner for the cough and it had not responded to treatment. Patient nail beds and conjunctiva appeared pale and the cervical lymph nodes are palpable. Auscultation of the chest shows crepitation over the upper left lobe. She had received a BCG vaccine as a child. A blood test is positive for interferon-gamma release assays. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 84 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 37.5° C oral Which of the following is the next best step in obtaining a definitive diagnosis?
A 23-year-old primigravida presented in labour. The midwife advised mediolateral episiotomy during the 2nd stage of labour. What is the advantage of a mediolateral episiotomy?

A 23-year-old G2 P1001 at 22 weeks' gestation presented with a complaint of her abdomen is larger than her previous pregnancy (see report). Ultrasound: Showed dizygotic twin pregnancy. Which of the following best described membranes and placenta of dizygotic twins?
option1: Monochorionic and monoamniotic for conjoined twins
option2: Monochorionic and diamniotic for the fetuses of same sex
option3: Dichorionic and monoamniotic for the fetuses of same sex
option4: Dichorionic and diamniotic regardless of the sex of the fetuses
correct_option: Dichorionic and diamniotic regardless of the sex of the fetuses

question_text: A 25-year-old woman has delivered a boy. On inspection of the umbilical cord, a single artery was noticed. Which of the following is associated with the above finding?

A 33-year-old pregnant woman G4 P2012 at 38 weeks'' gestation presented with a complaint of dizziness, light-headedness and fainting sensation especially in bed for 2 weeks duration (see lab results). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 100 /min, Respiratory rate 24/min, Temperature 36.6° C Test Result Normal Values Hb 95 120-160 g/L WBC 9 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Platelet count 200 150-400 × 109/L Which of the following is the best management?
A 28-year-old woman is diagnosed with an ectopic pregnancy. Which of the following is the most likely predisposing factor?
option4: Use of an intrauterine contraceptive device

question_text: A 21-year-old woman G2 P1001 presents to the Emergency Room with a severe lower abdominal pain for 1 day. Pain is dull and intermittent that last for 10-15 minutes and associated with nausea and vomiting. Examination confirms tenderness and guarding in the lower abdomen, it is more on right side and slight fullness in the right iliac fossa (see report). Blood pressure 90/60 mmHg Heart rate 110 /min Respiratory rate 13 /min Temperature 37.6° C Pregnancy Test: Negative. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 35-year-old pregnant woman at 34 weeks of gestation presented with labour pains. On physical examination, the cervix was 4 cm dilated. Cardiotocography was performed (see image). Blood pressure 135/88 mmHg Heart rate 89 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 37.4° C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old woman G3 P0020 at 5 weeks' gestation presents with vaginal bleeding associated with some blood clots that has started 3 hours ago. She had been diagnosed with cervical incompetence in her 2 prior second-trimester abortions. A history of dilation and curettage in her last abortion declared. On examination; the cervical os is open and no ongoing bleeding. Which of the following is the most likely cause of her current diagnosis of vaginal bleeding?

A 24-year-old woman came to the clinic complaining of yellowish offensive vaginal discharge and itching for 2 weeks duration. She has no other significant medical history. Vaginal swab done which showed motile flagellate organisms on wet film microscopy. Which of the following is the best treatment option?

A 24-year-old married woman came to the clinic complaining of yellowish offensive vaginal discharge and itching for 2 weeks duration. She has no other significant medical history. Vaginal swab done which showed motile flagellate organisms on wet film microscopy. Appropriate treatment was initiated. Which of the following is the best management option for her husband?
What is the most common risk factor for abruption placenta?

A 40-year-old pregnant woman at 32 weeks gestation with a history of 2 previous cesarean sections presents with mild painless vaginal bleeding few hours ago. Ultrasound shows partial placenta previa. Cardiotocogram is reactive. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 26-year-old presents with mild vaginal bleeding after 7 weeks history of amenorrhea. Pelvic examination reveals a closed cervix and the uterus size is compatible with the duration of amenorrhea. Which of the following is the most likely type abortion?

A 33-year-old woman delivered vaginally with right mediolateral episiotomy. An hour later, she was found to have an expanding 7 x 5 cm bluish, tense, and painful swelling of the perineum. Which of the following is the best management?

A 40-year-old woman at 37 weeks of gestation with polyhydramnios presented in active labor following spontaneous rupture of membranes. Cardiotocogram shows persistent fetal bradycardia. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old primigravida with 10 weeks of amenorrhea presents with abdominal pain and vaginal bleeding. Pelvic examination reveals partially dilated cervix, and the product of conception felt at the cervix. Which of the following is the most likely type of abortion?

A 45-year-old P5005 woman is complaining of severe secondary dysmenorrhea and menorrhagia. Pelvic examination reveals the uterus is symmetrically enlarged and tender. Endometrial biopsy is normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old woman with 6 weeks of amenorrhea presents with lower abdominal pain and mild vaginal bleeding. Examination reveals: she is hemodynamically stable and abdominal examination is soft and non-tender. Pregnancy test is positive (see lab result and report). Test Result Normal Values Human chorionic gonadotropin 4945 < 5 mlU/mL Pelvic Ultrasound: Shows a small mass <3.5 cm in right Fallopian tube, intact with no embryonic cardiac activity detected. Which of the following is the best management?
A 33-year-old presented with preterm rupture of membranes at 34 weeks of gestation. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 40-year-old came in active labor at 38 weeks of gestation. It was found that she is having an intrauterine fetal death. The bad news were delivered to her in steps. After delivery she insisted that the baby should be taken to Intensive Care Unit on a ventilator as she believes that he is not really dead. Which of the following best describes her condition?

The patient''s history reveals that she became pregnant while using an intrauterine contraceptive device 4 years ago and in her second pregnancy; she underwent a cesarean section, which was complicated by post-partum endometritis. Her menstrual cycles are irregular for the last 4 months with heavier flow than usual. Which of the following is an absolute contraindication to the insertion of an intrauterine contraceptive device?

A woman presents to the Emergency Department 15 days after giving birth by cesarean section. She complains of persistent vaginal bleeding. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 85 /min Temperature 38.1 °C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 29-year-old woman is planning to become pregnant. She has a normal menstrual period every 30 days. Which of the following is the most appropriate to prescribe?

A 30-year-old pregnant un-booked patient is presenting to the Emergency Room with vaginal bleeding. The patient denies any abdominal pain and did not feel the fetal movements for the last few hours, and she was finally able to attend to the Emergency Room, as she had no form of transport to the hospital. Abdominal examination reveals a 34 cm fundal height. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 101 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option3: Admit the patient to the ward

question_text: A 32-year-old pregnant woman at 36 weeks attends to the clinic complaining of abdominal pain that has been increasing in nature and is noted to be continuous, the patient denies any bleeding. Abdominal examination reveals a 38 cm fundal height, and the uterus was found to be tender and tense. Pelvic examination reveals a closed and un-effaced cervical os. The cardiogram shows category 1. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 37-year-old woman G2 P1001 at 20 weeks' gestation presents for a routine prenatal visit. She has no complaints. Her previous pregnancy has been unremarkable. She has chronic hypertension and history of cholecystectomy. She admits to smoking cigarettes several packs a day for relaxation and says she has read several papers that show no increased risk of congenital anomalies. Doppler shows fetal heart rate at 156 beat per minute. Blood pressure 108/68 mmHg Heart rate 100 /min Respiratory rate 16 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Oxygen saturation 95 % Which of the following is the best course of action?
option1: Schedule her in 4-week time in antenatal clinic
option2: Refer her to a social worker for possible substance abuse
option3: Educate her about the possibility of delivering a small infant
option4: Acknowledge that she is correct about no increased risk of congenital anomalies
correct_option: Educate her about the possibility of delivering a small infant

question_text: A 29-year-old primigravida presented in labor and progressed well and had a mediolateral episiotomy. Which of the following is a theoretical benefit to performing a mediolateral episiotomy compared to a midline episiotomy?
option4: Decreased risk of fourth degree laceration
correct_option: Decreased risk of fourth degree laceration

question_text: A 17-year-old woman presents with her mother to the clinic complaining of never having had menstruation. On examination, she is webbing of her neck and cubitus valgus. Lacks pubic hairs and no breast development noted. The mother is worried about the daughter and would inquire about the simplest test to start with. Height 135 cm Weight 46 kg Which of the following is the simplest and most useful test?

A 32-year-old G3 P0020 admitted to the hospital with an incomplete abortion. She underwent dilation and curettage during which excessive bleeding developed that required vigorous curettage. She returned to the clinic 6 months later complaining of no menstruation since her curettage despite positive premenstrual symptoms. Which layer of her endometrium is damaged based on history?
A 51-year-old woman presents to her appointment complaining of hot flushes. She would like to get an explanation of her symptom. Which of the following involves hot flushes?
option2: Peripheral vasodilation reflecting an elevation of core body temperature
option3: Peripheral vasodilation resulting from direct effect of elevated FSH and LH
option4: Peripheral redistribution of blood flow leading to sweating and increased heart rate
correct_option: Peripheral redistribution of blood flow leading to sweating and increased heart rate

question_text: A 17-year-old healthy girl and gymnast in her school team presents with history of the absence of menstruation. She reports that she develops breasts later than her friends and never began menses. On examination, her Tanner stage V development of breasts and pubic hair. Which of the following is the most likely cause of this girl primary amenorrhea?

A 40-year-old woman has achieved a pregnancy via in-vitro fertilization presents with her spouse at 11 weeks' gestation to the clinic (see report). Ultrasound: Shows a twin pregnancy. A 5-mm nuchal translucency (NT) is discovered in one of the fetus. Which of the following is an implication of NT finding?
option1: The fetus has a high risk of NT enlargement by 18 weeks' gestation
option2: The fetus has a high risk of cardiac malformation
option3: The fetus has a high risk of neural tube defect
option4: The fetus has a high risk of Turner syndrome
correct_option: The fetus has a high risk of cardiac malformation

question_text: A 62-year-old patient presented with vaginal bleeding. Investigations revealed a malignant tumor of uterine fundus. Which of the following groups of lymph nodes is likely affected in this condition and to be dissected during surgery?

A 25-year-old married woman is found to have a 5-cm left adnexal mass during a routine pelvic examination, which is confirmed by ultrasound. She denies symptoms or using any contraceptives. Her last menstrual period was 2 weeks earlier. A urine pregnancy test is negative. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 21-year-old woman primigravida at 38 weeks' gestation is in active labor. Her cervix is 5 cm dilated and fetal vertex is at +1 station. The tocodynamometer is done (see image). What is the best description of the initial acid-base status of the fetus?

A 38-year-old G3 P1011 woman presents for her initial antenatal visit at 6 weeks' gestation. She is known to have type 2 diabetes mellitus for 5 years that is managed by oral hypoglycemic agents. Which of the following is the best indicator of fetal outcome in her pregnancy?
option2: Blood glucose value in the clinic

question_text: A 43-year-old woman G9 P5126 breastfeeding of her 5-month-old daughter comes to the clinic because of positive home pregnancy test. She does not know her last menstrual period. Which of the following would be the most useful method to determine her estimated date of delivery?
option4: Transvaginal ultrasound as early as possible in the first trimester
correct_option: Transvaginal ultrasound as early as possible in the first trimester

question_text: A couple from Al-Ahsa Eastern Province of Saudi Arabia presents for antenatal booking. A complete blood count and blood film is ordered. Accordingly, further assessment of the risk to the fetus and to the pregnancy from the disease that is typified by the blood smear (see image). What is a diagnostic test to be performed at preconception or early in prenatal care?

A 27-year-old primigravida at 39 weeks' gestation presents to the hospital complaining of spontaneous watery gush. A speculum examination is performed and small amount of fluid is seen in the vault. Nitrazine paper test indicates a pH of 7. Which of the following is another reliable test to confirm amniotic fluid leaks?
option1: Indigo carmine injection into the amniotic sac
option2: Methylene blue injection into the amniotic sac
option3: Ultrasonography to assess amniotic fluid volume
option4: Microscopy to detect arborization or ferning of fluid
correct_option: Microscopy to detect arborization or ferning of fluid

question_text: An 80-year-old woman who presents to the clinic with a vulval lump. It has been bleeding intermittently. It is very sore and she is finding it very uncomfortable to sit down. A new vulval lump in an elderly woman is vulval cancer until proven otherwise. It is not unusual for elderly women to put off consulting their doctors because of embarrassment and reluctance to be examined. What is the best step to reduce embarrassment and reluctance to be examined?
option1: Building rapport, taking history and examination
option2: Getting help from social worker in management plan
option3: Brief discussion and reschedule a visit in a nearest future
option4: Introducing oneself followed by history and examination
correct_option: Building rapport, taking history and examination

question_text: A 48-year-old woman presents with a complaint of a 3-year history of stress incontinence, urgency and urge incontinence. She leaks urine about 4 times a day, has to wear sanitary pads all the time and rarely travels due to the urgency and need to pass urine 8 or 9 times during the day. On examination, body mass index normal, pelvic floor; marked reduced muscle strength noted. Which of the following is the recommended first line of management?
option2: Commence an oral anticholinergic medication for 8 weeks
option3: Midstream culture and treatment with antibiotics if indicated
option4: A course of pelvic floor exercises and bladder retraining for 8 weeks
correct_option: A course of pelvic floor exercises and bladder retraining for 8 weeks

question_text: A 58-year-old postmenopausal woman presented to Gynecology Clinic with history of inability to reach the toilet and control her micturition. The complaint was associated with strong desire for emptying her bladder even if the circumstances are not appropriate. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 33-year-old woman presents with amenorrhea of 8-week duration. Pregnancy test is positive (see report). Ultrasound: Shows an empty uterus and right adnexal mass noted. Which abnormality of the following processes is most likely cause of the above case?

A 19-year-old woman presents to Labor and Delivery Ward in labor. She has not had any prenatal care. On examination, cervix is 4 cm dilated; the obstetrician palpate a bulging membrane but no fetal parts. Ultrasound demonstrates that the fetal head is in the fundus, the fetal spine is parallel to the mother''s spine and the knees and hips are flexed. Both arms are flexed at the elbows. Which of the following is the best description of lie of this fetus?

A 36-year-old G3 P1011 woman is seen in preconception clinic for considering a pregnancy. She concerned of getting pregnant given the outcome of her first pregnancy. At 32 years of age, she delivered a term infant with Down syndrome. During that gestation, she had a second-trimester multiple marker screen been performed. Which of the following results would have been helpful?
option1: Low hCG. low MSAFP, high estriol, high inhibin A
option2: High hCG, low MSAFP, low estriol, high inhibin A
option3: Low hCG, high MSAFP, high estriol, low inhibin A
option4: Low hCG, high MSAFP, low estriol, low inhibin A
correct_option: High hCG, low MSAFP, low estriol, high inhibin A

question_text: A 29-year-old woman presents to the clinic with a history of irregular menstrual cycles for the last 2 years. Her last delivery 5 years ago. Currently, she is not on any contraception. Examination shows all within normal limits except breast examination revealed frank galactorrhea. Which of the following is most helpful investigation?

A 34-year-old woman G2 P0010 presents at 32 weeks gestation for a routine antenatal appointment. She reports mild headache, but no right upper quadrant pain or visual disturbance. Physical examination is otherwise unremarkable (see lab result). Blood pressure 150/100 mmHg Urine dipstick Test Result Normal Value Protein +3 absent Which of the following is the best next step of management?

A 36-year-old primigravida woman just delivered spontaneously baby girl weighing 4.4 kg. After 5 minutes placenta is delivered complete and intact. Massaging of the uterine is performed along with 20 units of oxytocin in 1000 mL of lactated Ringers fast drip. After careful inspection of the genital tract, a second-degree laceration was identified and a 2-cm left lateral vaginal wall laceration, suturing is difficult because of brisk bleeding from above the site of laceration. Physical examination reveals a soft, boggy uterine fundus. Blood pressure 164/92 mmHg Heart rate 130 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 37 °C Which of the following is the next best step in management?

A 20 year-old woman primigravida at 29 weeks'' gestation presents with continuous vaginal bleeding and back pain. She denies sexual intercourse within the last 48 hours or trauma to the abdomen. Vaginal Examination reveals about 100 mL of blood in the vaginal vault and cervix is closed (see image and report). Blood pressure 110/60 mmHg Heart rate 90 /min Respiratory rate 16 /min Temperature 36.0 °C Pelvic Ultrasound: Cephalic fetal presentation, amniotic fluid index of 10, and anterior-fundal placenta Which of the following is the most appropriate medication?

A 30-year-old woman G2 P1001 at 34+2 weeks'' gestation presents to Emergency Room reporting painless vaginal bleeding. Immediately transvaginal ultrasound shows placenta completely overlying the internal os, a fetus in cephalic presentation, and an amniotic fluid index of 14. The cervical appears long and closed on speculum examination. She has slow, continuous vaginal bleeding. Fetal heart is monitored (see image). Blood pressure 110/78 mmHg Heart rate 106 /min Respiratory rate 14 /min Temperature 36.9 °C Which of the following is the most appropriate in management?

A 27-year-old woman primigravida at 39 weeks'' gestation has been laboring for a few hours. Her cervix is 6 cm dilated, 80% effaced, and fetal vertex is at 0 station. Membranes have been ruptured for 20 hours and her labor is being augmented with oxytocin. Non-stress test is done (see image). Blood pressure 110/76 mmHg Heart rate 82 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 37.2 °C Oxygen saturation 95 % Which of the following is the most appropriate in management?

A 25-year-old woman primigravida at 31+5 weeks' gestation presents with abdominal cramping and lower back pain. She denies leaking of fluid from the vagina. Speculum examination shows no pooling liquor and nitrazine paper stays yellow. Cervical cultures are taken. Her cervix changes from closed and 50% effaced to 2 to 3 cm and 80% effaced. She is placed on fetal monitoring (see image). Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 34-year-old woman G3 P1011 at 30 weeks'' gestation is in preterm labor. She has received an initial bolus of 6 g of MgSO4 over 30 minutes and continuing on a maintenance rate of 4 g/hr for the last 2 days to reduce her contraction pattern to 1 every 15 minutes. Currently, the contractions are barely noticeable to her. Deep tendon reflexes are 0 bilaterally. Auscultation reveals crackles on her lung bases on deep inspiration. Blood pressure 90/50 mmHg Heart rate 88 /min Respiratory rate 9 /min Temperature 37.1 °C Oxygen saturation 95 % Which of the following is the best next step in management?

A 25-years-old pregnant woman at 20 weeks of gestation presented with twisted ovarian cyst, which needed an emergency laparotomy. The patient was explained about the condition, but she refused the surgery. Which of the following is the most appropriate action should be taken?

A 39-year-old woman primigravida presents at 39 weeks gestation for a routine visit. Physical examination is unremarkable. Cervix is 2 cm dilated and 90% effaced, with the fetal vertex at 0 station. Urine dipstick is done (see image). Blood pressure 140/90 mmHg Which of the following is the most appropriate management option?

A 36-year-old woman with 10-year history of infertility due to male factor is brought to Emergency Room with vaginal bleeding and abdominal pain. Thereafter, she progresses to complete abortion at 8 weeks'' gestation while awaiting for admission. She is crying and her husband is an overwhelmed emotionally. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?
option2: Explore, identify and respond to the couple emotion
option3: Admission to postpartum ward based on her condition
option4: Explore, identify the cause and respond to the couple with management plan
correct_option: Explore, identify and respond to the couple emotion

question_text: A 30-year-old woman G5 P0030 and her husband present to the clinic at 9 weeks'' gestation with mild spotting after an intercourse, no abdominal pain reported. She has a history of 3 previous first trimester abortions. The couple are blaming themselves for the bleeding. They show a strong presentation of emotion. Which of the following is best way to deal with this situation?
option1: Identify their emotion and calm them down
option2: Empathic response is sufficient to calm them down
option3: Understand their emotion without showing that to them
option4: Explore and validate their emotion with empathic response
correct_option: Explore and validate their emotion with empathic response

question_text: A 21-year-old woman married for 6 months brought to the Emergency Department by police with bruises on the face, chest and both hands. On examination, she looks sad with tears, sitting quietly, and facing down to the floor. Which of the following is the most appropriate approach for history and examination?
option1: Invest time with clinical findings at the beginning
option2: Explore and identify her sadness and find out more what is causing it
option3: Introducing yourself with a big smile and proceed to a chief complain
option4: History and examination should be with the presence of the family member
correct_option: Explore and identify her sadness and find out more what is causing it

question_text: A 28-year-old G4 P0121 at 28 weeks'' gestation, visitor to the country, presents with labor-like pain for 2 hours. On arrival, while trying to take history and to perform examination, the patient could not understand due to language barrier. She looks anxious and worries about her pregnancy, which is similar experience to her 4-year-old boy whom delivered prematurely. Which of the following is the most appropriate step in communication?
option1: Showing an understanding to her emotion is inappropriate because of language barrier
option2: Showing that her emotions cannot be validated because she had similar experience
option3: Showing that her emotions are understood
option4: Calling a help from other colleagues
correct_option: Showing that her emotions are understood

question_text: A 35-year-old pregnant woman at 13-weeks of pregnancy presented with painless vaginal bleeding. Vaginal examination shows a suspicious lesion of the cervix (see report). Fetal ultrasound: Shows normal viable fetus and size corresponds to her gestational age. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 26-year-old patient with 6-weeks history of amenorrhea presented with mild lower pelvic pain and vaginal bleeding. Pelvic examination revealed a soft uterus of normal size with mild tenderness at the right adnexa (see lab results and report). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 82 /min Respiratory rate 22 /min Temperature 37.6 °C Test Result Normal Values Human chorionic gonadotropin 1000 < 5 mlU/mL B-hCG 1000 1080-56500 mIU/ml Pelvic ultrasound: Shows a thickened endometrium with no intrauterine sac, minimal amount of fluid in the cul-de-sac and right adnexal mass measuring 1.2 cm. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A 6-year-old girl brought by her mother with vulvar itching and bloody vaginal discharge for few days. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 55-year-old menopausal woman presented with vulvar irritation associated with urgency and frequency. Examination of the external genitalia revealed odorless watery discharge and generalized vulvar erythema with excoriation. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 22-year-old woman presented with headache, malaise and fever. Examination revealed multiple, bilateral painful anogenital vesicles. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 32-year-old pregnant woman at 26-weeks of gestation diagnosed with large genital warts in the vulva. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A 34-year-old patient at 38-weeks of pregnancy with genital chlamydia trachomatis. Which of the following is most commonly acquired infection by the newborn in such a condition?

A 32-year-old pregnant woman with polyhydramnios presented at 37-weeks of gestation with painful vaginal bleeding and uterine tenderness following spontaneous rupture of membranes. Cardiotocography, shows persistent bradycardia. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 23-year-old primigravida presented at 32-weeks of gestation with seizures (see lab result). Blood pressure 160/110 mmHg Heart rate 78 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Test Result Normal Values Protein +++ absent which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 38-year-old primigravida presents at 38-weeks of gestation with history of spontaneous rupture of membranes 24-hours ago. She has no labour pains. On abdominal examination, the uterus is soft and the fetus is in cephalic presentation. Cardiotocography shows normal fetal heart rate. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
A 27-year-old woman presented at 24-weeks of gestation with dysuria and lower abdominal pain. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 29-year-old primigravida presented at 22 weeks of gestation with mild lower abdominal pain. Abdominal examination reveals that the uterus is corresponding to the gestational age with positive fetal heart. Vaginal examination reveals a short soft cervix, which is 1.5 cm dilated with herniation of fetal membranes. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 27-year-old primigravida presented at 36-weeks of gestation with headache, blurred vision (see lab result). Blood pressure 160/110 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.8 °C Test Result Normal Values Protein ++ absent Which of the following is the most appropriate medication for acute treatment of hypertension?

A 39-year-old G6 P5005 is seen in the Antenatal Clinic for routine check-up at 22 weeks of gestation. Her blood pressure found to be 140/90 mmHg and no proteinuria. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?
A 27-year-old woman presented to the police department with bruises all over her body. Which of the following is the main predictor of a domestic violence?
A 25-year-old newly married woman presented to the Outpatient Clinic for routine gynecological examination. Which of the following has the highest diagnostic value?

A 50-year-old woman with menorrhagia due to a large uterine fibroid was seen in the clinic to complete the consent form of an elective hysterectomy. What is the most appropriate step in this condition?
A 36-year-old woman came at 10 weeks of gestation for an ultrasound. Which of the following has the highest diagnostic value?

A 37-year-old primigravida was seen in the Antenatal Clinic for a follow-up. She is a known smoker for 3 years and has a history of subfertility. A positive family history of diabetes mellitus in a second degree relative. Which of the following is a significant risk factor in her history?
A 35-year-old woman came for an ultrasound scan to confirm gestational age of her pregnancy. Which of the following is most appropriate timing?
A 34-year-old woman with a history of recurrent pregnancy loss was seen in the clinic. Which of following is the best predictor for pregnancy complication?

A 28-year-old woman presented to Preconception Clinic with a known diagnosis of epilepsy 6 years ago, poorly controlled and frequent epileptic attacks. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 38-year-old presented to Labor Ward with severe abruptio placenta. She was diagnosed with intrauterine fetal death and disseminated intravascular coagulopathy (DIC). Vaginal examination revealed 6 cm dilated cervix. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 22-year-old patient presented to the Emergency Department with history of expulsion of products of conception at home. On examination, cervix were closed with minimal vaginal bleeding. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 29-year-old woman was seen in the clinic with a history of recurrent pregnancy loss between 9-12 weeks of gestation. Which of the following is the most likely cause of her condition?
A 27-year-old sexually active woman came for annual examination. 3 years ago, she had a pap smear which was reported to be normal. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A 40-year-old pregnant woman at 24 weeks of gestation with fever and did not seek any medical advice. What is the most likely reason for illness denial?
A 34-year-old woman came to the clinic with history of primary infertility for 3 years. She was anxious, worried and depressed. Which of the following is a positive body language?

A 33-year-old woman was seen in the clinic with her husband, they have a history of primary infertility. The investigations of the husband had showed that he is HIV positive. What is the most appropriate next step?
A 38-year-old multipara had a postpartum hemorrhage after a normal vaginal delivery of a macrosomic baby. Which of the following is the best management of this condition?

A 38-year-old woman had postpartum hemorrhage after a normal vaginal delivery. She was started on oxytocin and bimanual massage of the uterus, but failed to control the bleeding. What is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 23-year-old newly married woman came to the police department after beaten up by her husband. Which of the following is the most consistent predictor of attitudes supporting the use of violence against women?
A 33-year-old woman was seen at 34 weeks of gestation with a broken arm after beaten up by her husband. When is the most common time that increases the risk of violence by an intimate partner?

A 59-year-old woman attends to the clinic complaining of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last 2 months and concerned about her condition. She had the last menstrual cycle 12 years ago. On examination, the vagina is found to be pale with dry, smooth and shiny epithelium losing of most of its rugation. Which of the following is the most probable cause of the condition?

A 59-year-old woman attends to the clinic complaining of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last 2 months. She had the last menstrual cycle 12 years ago. On examination, Vagina is noted to be pale, smooth and shiny dry epithelium along with loss of vaginal rugae (see report), Ultrasound Scan: The endometrial lining is 15 mm thick, the uterus is average in size with no abnormality in shape. The ovaries are normal and no adnexal masses or free fluid detected. Which of the following is the most appropriate procedure at this stage?

A 59-year-old woman attends to the clinic complaining of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last 2 months. She had the last menstrual cycle 12 years ago. An Ultrasound Scan was taken and endometrial sampling is planned (see report). Ultrasound Scan: The endometrial lining is 15 mm thickness. Which of the following best describes a prerequisite for this procedure?

A 59-year-old woman attends for the first time to the clinic complaining of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last 2 months and she is extremely concerned about her condition. The last menstrual cycle was 12 years ago. The patient requests admission, preparation and booking for a hysterectomy. Which of the following is the most appropriate response?
option2: Admit into the hospital to gain confidence
option3: Discuss in referring to an oncologist because of age and risk of malignancy
option4: Calm her down and conduct a detailed informative discussion about best options of her
correct_option: Calm her down and conduct a detailed informative discussion about best options of her

question_text: A 59-year-old woman attends to the clinic complaining of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last 2 months. All her investigations are normal and endometrial atrophy is the cause of her condition. The patient is not convinced and requesting a second opinion by another physician. Which of the following is the most appropriate response at this point?
option2: Perform further imaging studies to gain her trust
option3: Provide a detailed medical report as it is her right
option4: Convince her not to waste time by getting a second opinion
correct_option: Provide a detailed medical report as it is her right

question_text: A 59-year-old woman attends to the clinic complaining of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last 2 months and concerned about her condition, she had the last menstrual cycle 12 years ago. An ultrasound scan was taken and endometrial polyp is suspected (see report). Ultrasound Scan: 19 mm endometrial lining. Which of the following should be offered to the patient at this stage?
A 59-year-old woman with a complaint of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last 2 months undergoes a procedure (see image). What is the name of this procedure?
A 59-year-old woman undergoes a hysteroscopy secondary to her complain of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last couple of months (see image). Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old woman is complaining of abnormal genital bleeding. She describes the amount of bleeding has been scanty, spotting and just staining the pad. Which of the following would be the most likely source of bleeding?
A 45-year-old woman is complaining of abnormal genital bleeding. Which of the following would highly suggest a uterine source of bleeding?
A 35-year-old woman is complaining of abnormal genital bleeding. Which of the following would suggest a cervical source of bleeding?

A 35-year-old woman is complaining of abnormal genital bleeding, she states that the amount is scanty and not associated with blood clots, but noticeable for post-coital bleeding. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 40-year-old woman is complaining of abnormal uterine bleeding. She is known diabetic on oral hypoglycemic agent for 5 years and her menarche at the age of 13. The last 3 cycles were heavy, however, denies any weight loss or palpitation. Which of the following would make the highest risk of endometrial cancer?

A 43-year-old never married woman is complaining of abnormal uterine bleeding for the last 3 months. She was placed on progesterone tablets to control her bleeding by a family physician prior to referral. One of the sisters had a diagnosis of thyroid cancer 2 years ago. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 16 /min BMI 17 kg/m2 Which of the following would make the highest risk of endometrial cancer?

A 47-year-old woman presents with abnormal uterine bleeding, she describes the interval between the onset of bleeding episodes to be less than 21 days, heavy and prolonged. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 27-year-old woman presents with abnormal uterine bleeding, she describes the interval between the onset of bleeding episodes to be less than 21 days, heavy and prolonged. BMI 21 kg/m2 Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A newborn child presents with cyanosis, tachypnea, irritability and difficulty feeding. Physical examination findings are a parasternal heave and a loud, single S2 on cardiac auscultation. No murmurs were heard. Which of the following is essential in the early management?

A 36-year-old woman presents with abnormal uterine bleeding. The bleeding is persistent for the last 8 months. She denies any significant family history of endometrial cancer, BMI 41 kg/m2 Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in assessment?

A 32-year-old married woman presents to the clinic with vague abdominal pain of 1-week duration, she claims an irregular menstrual cycles and her last cycle occurred 2 months ago. Blood pressure 142/85 mmHg Heart rate 101 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 32-year-old married woman presents to the clinic with vague abdominal pain of 1-week duration. She claims an irregular menstrual cycles and her last menstrual cycle occurred 2 months ago. The patient is a known smoker with surgical history of open appendectomy due to ruptured appendicitis when she was 14 years of age Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 102 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Which of the following diagnoses is to be rule out initially?

A 32-year-old married woman presents with vague, sharp abdominal pain of 1-week duration, she claims an irregular menstrual cycles and her last menstrual cycle occurred 2 months ago. The patient is a known smoker with history of open appendectomy due to ruptured appendicitis at the age of 14 years (see lab results). Blood pressure 90/40 mmHg Heart rate 105 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Test Result Normal Values Human chorionic gonadotropin 1800 < 5 mlU/mL Hb 11 130-170 g/L. (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) HCT 0.39 0.42-0.52 (Male) 0.37-0.48 (Female) MCH 29 28-33 pg/cell MCHC 332 320-360 g/L MCV 89 80-95 fL Which of the following is the best next step in management?
option4: Consult general surgery team to rule out acute abdomen

question_text: A 33-year-old woman is day 2 post laparotomy where salpingostomy was performed for right tubal pregnancy, the procedure was performed without complications and the patient is suitable to be discharged from the hospital (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 12 130-170 g/L. (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) 3500 < 5 mlU/mL Which of the following is the most appropriate follow up approach?
option1: Follow up appointment is not required
option3: Perform a pelvic ultrasound scan 1 week post-operative
option4: Serum HCG to be measured weekly until the level is undetectable
correct_option: Serum HCG to be measured weekly until the level is undetectable

question_text: A 29-year-old woman is day 2 post laparotomy where salpingectomy was performed for left tubal pregnancy, the procedure was performed without complications and the patient is suitable to be discharged from the hospital (see lab results). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Test Result Normal Values Hb 13 130-170 g/L. (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) 6488 < 5 mlU/mL Which of the following is the most appropriate follow up approach?
option1: Single postoperative hCG testing to confirm a large decline in the level
option2: Weekly hCG level measurement until the level is undetectable
option3: Pelvic ultrasound to be performed 6 days post-operative
correct_option: Single postoperative hCG testing to confirm a large decline in the level
