A 71-year-old man who has been admitted due to Clostridium difficile infection last year. In order to search for possible risk factors that increase the risk of recurrent infection, his medications have been reviewed thoroughly. Which of the following medications is associated with increased risk of C. difficile infection?

A 20-year-old man presented with 3-months history of watery diarrhea, weight loss of 3 kg and abdominal cramps. He gave a history of travel to Mecca few months back. No other symptoms. Upper Gl endoscopy was normal. Smear from duodenal aspirate revealed many trophozoites (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 109 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Platelets count 459 150-400 x 109/L Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 19-year-old man presented with 3-months history of watery diarrhea, weight loss of 3 kg and abdominal cramps. He gave a history of travel to India few months back. No other symptoms. A smear from duodenal aspirate revealed many trophozoites (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 108 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Platelets count 458 150-400 × 109/L Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
What is the best and simplest way to prevent giardiasis infection?

A 67-year-old diabetic patient admitted to Intensive Care Unit with sepsis and urinary tract infection. Blood culture revealed methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment option?
What is the best and simplest precaution to prevent spread of MRSA infection?

A 25-year-old man developed fever, malaise and rash on his palm and soles. He gave a history of painless ulcer on his penis 6 weeks earlier. Examination was unremarkable apart from generalized lymphadenopathy. Which of the following is the most likely organism causing his presentation?

A 33-year-old man presented with 2-weeks of lethargy, fever, generalized lymphadenopathy and cough. History revealed he is a businessman travels frequently to Far East. On examination, he has oral candidiasis (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values RBC 4 4.7-6.1 x 1012/L (Male) 4.2-5.4 x 1012/L (Female) Hb 13 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Platelets count 145 150-400 x 109/L WBC 11 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Neutrophils 22 40-60% Lymphocytes 69 20-40 % Monocytes 9 2-8 % Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 56-year-old diabetic man presented with cough and fever. Chest-XR showed cavity at upper lobe of the left lung. What is the type of precaution required when dealing with this patient?

A 21-year-old man presents with paroxysms of coughing for the past week, following a mild flu illness. The paroxysms have been so severe that they have resulted vomiting and chest pain. He had been immunized for pertussis as a child. What is the best advice about the protection following immunization?
option1: Immunization offers lifelong immunity against pertussis
option2: Immunity following immunization wanes within 3 years
option3: Immunity following immunization wanes within 10 years
option4: Immunity following immunization wanes after 25 years
correct_option: Immunity following immunization wanes within 10 years

question_text: A 22-year-old woman develops severe vomiting 4 hours after having lunch at a restaurant. What is the most likely causative organism?
Which of the following stool electrolyte testing results would most represent a Vibrio cholerae?

A 35-year-old man presented with fever, rigors and weight loss for 10 days. He underwent Mitral valve replacement by prosthetic valve a month ago. Echo showed small vegetation. Blood pressure 90/70 mmHg, Heart rate 100 /min, Temperature 38.7 °C Which of the following organisms is the most likely to be responsible for his presentation?

A 25-year-old man developed fever, malaise and rash on the palms of his hands as well as on the soles of both feet. 6 weeks earlier, he had a painless ulcer on his penis. He gave a history of recent travel and extramarital unprotected sex. On examination, he has generalized lymphadenopathy. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 87 /min, Temperature 38.6 °C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 43-year-old veterinarian man presents with 2-months history of progressive fatigue, back pain and fever. He also noticed a recent history of headache, mood /behavior changes and paraesthesia. He is does not smoke with no history of alcohol or other significant medical history. On examination, he was uncooperative and agitated. Abdomen examination showed mild hepatosplenomegaly and tender right sacroiliac joint (see lab results). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 88 /min, Temperature 38.6 °C Test Result Normal Values RBC 4 4.7-6.1 x 1012/L (Male) 4.2-5.4 x 1012/L (Female) Hb 93 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Platelets count 120 150-400 x 109/L WBC 9 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 41-year-old veterinarian man presents with 3-months history of progressive fatigue. back pain and fever. He also noticed a recent history of headache, mood /behavior changes and paraesthesia. He does not smoke and with no history of alcohol or other significant medical history. On examination, he was uncooperative and agitated. Abdomen examination showed mild hepatosplenomegaly and tender right sacroiliac joint (see lab results). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 86 /min, Temperature 38.6 °C Test Result Normal Values Hb 98 130-170 g/L. (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Platelets count 122 150-400 x 109/L WBC 8 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 43-year-old man was recently diagnosed with neurobrucellosis. He is started on rifampicin (600 mg/day) and doxycycline (100 mg twice daily orally). which of the following is the recommended treatment duration?

A 22-year-old man known case with AIDS on active antiretroviral therapy (HART) presented with history of headache and weakness at left side of the body. On examination, he is found to have left hemiparesis, hemianopia, aphasia, ataxia, and 6/7 cranial nerve palsies. Investigation showed a rising titers of anti- toxoplasma immunoglobulin G (IgG) antibodies and positive immunoglobulin M (IgM) antibody (see report). Brain MRI scans: Demonstrated multiple hypodense lesions in white matter and basal ganglia with mass effects. Which of the following is the best treatment option?

A 25-year-old woman presented with 4-days history of confusion, lethargy and fever. On examination, she was drowsy with mild neck stiffness (see report). CT scan brain: Showed area of low attenuation at right tempo-parietal region. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 22-year-old woman previously healthy was admitted with 3-days history of fever, myalgia and pharyngitis. She gave history of 1-day macular rash over the trunk after she received amoxicillin from private clinic. On examination, she has generalised lymphadenopathy. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
An 82-year-old woman admitted to the hospital for meningitis. Her CSF was obtained and Gram stain showed Gram negative coccobacilli. Which chemo-prophylaxis should be offered to her contacts?

A 35-year-old man presented to Emergency Room with recurrent attacks of high-grade fever with chills for 1 week. He was on a work trip to Sudan 3 weeks ago. Each attack begins with chills for few hours followed by fever up to 40.5 degree, which resolves with profuse sweating. The attacks comes every other day. On examination, mild splenomegaly was detected. Blood smear at Emergency Room was negative for parasites. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 120 /min, Temperature 39 °C. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in diagnosis?
option2: Repeat thick blood smear once more
option3: Repeat blood smear during the attack
option4: Repeat blood smear every 8 hours for 2 days
correct_option: Repeat blood smear every 8 hours for 2 days

question_text: A 51-year-old woman presented to the hospital with 2-days history of malaise and headache. The headache became more intense and was associated with pain in her neck. Her husband reported that she had also fever. She was previously well with no significant medical history. On examination, she looked unwell confused, febrile and with positive Kernig''s sign. CT scan brain was normal. Lumbar puncture done (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Colour Turbid colourless Cells 200 0-3 /pL Total protein (Women) 0.9 0.15-0.45 g/L Glucose 1.6 2.50-3.89 mmol/L. Which of the following is the most likely causative organism?

A 42-year-old man with no medical problems presents with a 2-day history of profuse bloody diarrhea, severe abdominal cramping and fever. He has recently returned from a week-long trip to Indonesia. His stool culture is positive for Campylobacter. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 31-year-old man complains of persistent watery diarrhea and has noticed some weight loss. He returned 5 weeks ago from Egypt. He submits a stool sample that came back negative. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 38-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department with 5-day history of fever and a productive cough (see lab results and reports). Blood pressure 100/65 mmHg. Temperature 39 °C. Test Result Normal Values WBC 12 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. ESR 26 2-10 mm/h (Male) 3-15 mm/h (Female). Chest X-ray: Right middle lobe infiltrate. Sputum culture: Pending. Which of the following is the most appropriate immediate therapy?

A 27-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department with 2-days history of confusion and 5-hours history of seizures. Positive history of multiple unprotected sexual contact for the last 6 years. On examination, he was disoriented to time, place and person (see lab results and report). Temperature 39 °C. Test Result Normal Values WBC 1.3 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. Serum Cryptococcal Antigen: Positive. Which of the following is the most appropriate diagnostic test?
A 22-year-old man recently diagnosed with HIV presented to the clinic for counselling. Whom should he disclose his HIV status to?
A 53-year-old man recently diagnosed with HIV came to the clinic for counselling. Who should be offered HIV testing?

A 69-year-old man known to have diabetes mellitus, presented to Emergency department with 4-day history of productive cough and tachypnea. On examination, oriented to time, place and person. Chest exam revealed right lower lobe crepitations (see lab results and report). Respiratory rate 23 /min. Temperature 38.5 °C. Test Result Normal Values WBC 12 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. Urea 5 2.75-7.4 mmol/L. Chest X-Ray: Right lower lobe infiltrate. Which of the following is the most appropriate management approach?
option1: Admit to general ward and start IV antibiotics
option2: Admit to Intensive care unit and start IV antibiotics
option3: Start oral antibiotics and observe in Emergency Room for 24 hours
option4: Start oral antibiotics and see in outpatient clinic in 7-days
correct_option: Admit to general ward and start IV antibiotics

question_text: A 70-year-old woman known to have hypertension on medication. Presented to the Emergency Department with 4-days history of productive cough, shortness of breath and tachypnea. Chest exam, right lobe zone crepitations. Temperature 38.5 °C. Which of the following is the most appropriate next diagnostic test to confirm the diagnosis?

A 54-year-old man known to have diabetes and hypertension presented to Emergency Department with 9-days history of cough, headache, shortness of breath and fever. Cough was productive of white sputum, which has become yellowish lately. On chest examination, wheezing, and right side crepitations (see lab results and reports). Temperature 38 °C. Test Result Normal Values Reticulocyte 8 0.2-1.2 %. WBC 12 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. Cold agglutinin titer: 1:256. Chest X-Ray: Bilateral shadowing both lungs with more density on the right middle lobe. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old woman referred to the clinic with 3-weeks history of generalized fatigability, malaise, arthralgia, back pain and fever. Positive history of raw milk ingestion. Abdominal examination, mild hepatosplenomegaly (see lab results and report). Temperature 39 °C. Test Result Normal Values Hb 10.5 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female). Platelets count 120 150-400 x 109/L. Blood culture: Gram negative coccobacilli. Which of the following is the first line in treatment option?

A 28-year-old woman referred to the clinic with 3-week history of generalized fatigability, malaise, arthralgia, back pain and fever. Positive history of raw milk ingestion. Abdominal examination, mild hepatosplenomegaly (see lab results and reports). Temperature 38.5 °C. Test Result Normal Values Hb 10.5 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female). Platelets count 120 150-400 x 109/L. Blood culture: Gram negative coccobacilli. MRI of the back: Sacroiliac joint spondylitis. Which of the following is the optimal duration of therapy?

A 45-year-old housemaid from Southeast Asia, recently came to Saudi Arabia, presented to the Emergency Department with 2-weeks history of productive cough and shortness of breath. Positive history of hemoptysis. Chest exam showed right upper zone crepitations (see lab results and report). Temperature 38.5 °C. Test Result Normal Values WBC 14 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. ESR 73 2-10 mm/h (Male) 3-15 mm/h (Female). Chest X-Ray: Right upper lobe infiltrate and cavitation. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 45-year-old man admitted to the hospital with history of productive cough and shortness of breath. He was found to have cavitatory pulmonary tuberculosis (TB). He was started on 4 anti-TB drugs. Few days later, he noticed that the urine color is red. Detailed history revealed family history of urethral stones (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 11 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female). Platelets count 110 150-400 x 109/L. Which of the following is the most likely cause of his new symptom?

A 45-year-old man admitted to the hospital with history of productive cough and shortness of breath. He was found to have positive acid fast bacilli (AFB) in sputum. He was started on isoniazid, rifampin, pyrazinamide and ethambutol. He came back to the clinic for follow up after 3- weeks. Cough was better and no other symptoms (see lab results) Admission lab results: Test Result Normal Values Total bilirubin 5 3.5-16.5 mol/L Aspartate aminotransferase 28 12-40 IU/L Alanine aminotransferase 32 5-40 IU/L ESR 75 2-10 mm/h (Male) 3-15 mm/h (Female) Follow-up lab results: Test Result Normal Values Total bilirubin 0.7 3.5-16.5 mol/L Aspartate aminotransferase 75 12-40 IU/L Alanine aminotransferase 80 5-40 IU/L ESR 35 2-10 mm/h (Male) 3-15 mm/h (Female) Sputum AFB Negative Which of the following is the best management approach?
option1: Continue anti-TB and repeat LFT in 1 week
option2: Stop all anti-TB and repeat LFT in 1 weeks
option3: Stop pyrazinamide and repeat LFT in 2 weeks
option4: Stop isoniazid and rifampin and repeat LFT in 2 weeks
correct_option: Continue anti-TB and repeat LFT in 1 week

question_text: A 60-year-old man known to have hypertension and post aortic valve replacement on lisinopril 20 mg. amlodipine 5 mg and warfarin 3 mg once daily. He was admitted to the hospital with history of productive cough and shortness of breath, and was found to have positive acid fast bacilli (AFB) in sputum. First line four anti-TB drugs were started. Which of the following regarding medications should be done?
option1: Lisinopril dose needs to be decreased

question_text: A 60-year-old man from India presented to the Emergency Department with headache and low-grade fever for 3-weeks. The headache became worse over the last 2-days and was associated with vomiting. In the emergency room, he developed generalized seizures (see lab results and reports), Test Result Normal Values WBC 19 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Pressure 280 200-250 mmH20 (Male) 300-400 mmH20 (Female) Cells 380 0-3 /ML Total protein (Men) 3 0.22-0.33 g/L Glucose 1.2 2.50-3.89 mmol/L CSF differential WBC count: 73% lymphocytes and 13 % neutrophils. CSF culture: Pending. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 53-year-old woman known diabetic, hypertensive and cardiac arrhythmia on amiodarone, presented to Emergency Department with 3-days history of progressive redness and pain on her right leg. Examination showed a sharply demarcated red lesion, which was tender with no ulcer, Temperature 38.8 °C. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 53-year-old woman known diabetic presented to the Emergency Department with 5-days history of progressive redness and pain on her right thigh. On examination, right thigh showed 15 cm red lesion, which was tender with no ulcer (see report). Temperature 38.3 °C Ultrasound: Showed inflammatory changes and 10 x 8 cm deep abscess collection. Which of the following is the best management approach?
option1: Admit to ward and start IV antibiotics
option2: Admit to ICU and start IV antibiotics
option3: Discharge home on oral antibiotics with 1-week follow up
option4: Admit to ward, start IV antibiotics, and surgical consultation
correct_option: Admit to ward, start IV antibiotics, and surgical consultation

question_text: A 29-year-old woman is diagnosed with rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis. Which of the following pathological changes is characteristic of this diagnosis?

A 53-year-old woman recently returned from Egypt, known diabetic, presented to the Emergency Department with 3-days history of progressive redness and pain on her right shin. Positive history of allergy to penicillin. On examination, right leg has red lesion, which was tender with no ulcer. She was started on oral antibiotics. Next day she complained of abdominal pain, watery diarrhoea and fever. Temperature 37.8 °C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 63-year-old woman known diabetic for the last 20-years on insulin, presented to the Emergency Department with 3-days history of right knee pain and swelling. Right knee examination revealed, red, swollen and tender with severely limited range of movement. Other joints were normal (see lab result and report). Temperature 39 °C Test Result Normal Values WBC 17 4.5-10.5 × 109/L Synovial fluid: WBC: 55.000 Crystals: Pending Culture: Negative Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 32-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with 3-days history of generalized body aches, sore throat, runny nose, productive cough and shortness of breath. Chest exam shows right lung crepitations. The patient was admitted and according to the results of throat swab was started on oseltamivir (see report). Temperature 39 °C. Chest X-Ray: Right middle lobe infiltrate. Which of the following isolation precautions should be considered?

A 33-year-old woman presents with headache and myalgia for 5 days. 2 days ago, she started to have fever and noticed a skin rash on her face. She returned from Jeddah 10-days ago (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 8 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Platelets count 80 150-400 x 109/L Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 27-year-old soldier going to a military mission at the Southern border of Saudi Arabia. His medical history is insignificant apart from a positive history of depression, which is controlled by medication. He came for counselling regarding malaria prevention. Which of the following is the best malaria chemoprophylaxis regimen?

A 30-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with history of fever and headache. He recently came from a trip to Sudan, where he stayed there for 10-days. Abdominal examination, palpable spleen 3 cm below costal margin (see lab results). Temperature 40 °C Test Result Normal Values Hb 9 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Platelets count 90 150-400 x 109/L Aspartate aminotransferase 88 12-40 IU/L Alanine aminotransferase 94 5-40 IU/L Which of the following is the first line treatment option?

A 45-year-old man, heavy smoker, presents to the Emergency Department with 5-days history of fever and skin rash and 3-days of dyspnea and dry cough. Skin examination shows vesicular lesions over the trunk and extremities, some of the lesions were crusted. Chest examination shows bilateral crepitations (see report). Respiratory rate 28/min Temperature 39 °C Oxygen saturation 82 % Chest X-Ray: Diffuse bilateral infiltrate. Which of the following is the most appropriate intravenous treatment option?

A 22-year-old man recently returned from Umrah, presents to Emergency Department with 1- day history of fever and headache. Upon arrival to the Emergency Department, he started to have seizures on the right extremities. On examination, positive Kernig sign (see lab results and report). Temperature 39 °C Test Result Normal Values Cells 380 0-3 /pL Total protein (Men) 1.2 0.22-0.33 g/L CSF analysis: WBC differential count: 87% neutrophils and 5% lymphocytes. Which of the following is the most appropriate intravenous treatment option?

A 22-year-old man presents to Emergency Department with 4-days history of fever, headache and confusion. On examination, Kemig''s sign is present (see lab results and reports). Temperature 39 °C Test Result Normal Values Cells 380 0-3 /pL Total protein (Men) 0.8 0.22-0.33 g/L Glucose 3.7 2.50-3.89 mmol/L CSF analysis: WBC differential count: 5% neutrophil and 90% lymphocytes. CSF Culture: Pending. Which of the following is the most appropriate intravenous treatment option?

A 22-year-old man recently returned from Hajj, presents to Emergency Department with 1-day history of fever and headache. On examination, Kernig''s sign is present. Skin examination showed petechial rash on extremities and trunk (see lab results and report). Temperature 39 °C Test Result Normal Values Cells 380 0-3 /pL Total protein (Men) 0.9 0.22-0.33 g/L Glucose 1.2 2.50-3.89 mmol/L CSF analysis: WBC differential: 87% neutrophils and 5% lymphocytes. Which of the following isolation precautions should be considered?

A 27-year-old man recently returned from Umrah. Presents to Emergency Department with 1- day history of fever and headache. On examination, positive Kernig''s sign and petechial rash on extremities and trunk. He was isolated and started on antibiotics (see lab results and report). Temperature 39 °C Test Result Normal Values Cells 380 0-3 /pL Total protein (Men) 0.9 0.22-0.33 g/L Glucose 1.2 2.50-3.89 mmol/L CSF analysis: WBC differential 87 % neutrophil and 5 % lymphocytes. How many hours after starting antibiotics should isolation be discontinued?

A 44-year-old man with poorly controlled epilepsy presented with fever, cough and left side chest pain for 6 weeks. The cough is productive of copious foul-smelling sputum. Physical examination revealed digital clubbing and course crepitations in the right infra-clavicular area. Heart examination is normal (see image and report). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 100 /min Respiratory rate 25 /min Temperature 39 °C. Sputum culture: Mixture of aerobic and anaerobic organisms. What is the most likely mechanism for the underlying condition?
option4: Direct extension from infected chest wall

question_text: A 51-year-old woman with asthma complaining of increasing shortness of breath and wheeze in the past few hours. She has a long-standing diagnosis of asthma but her medication history suggests a lack of compliance. Her best peak flow measurements in previous consultations have been 300 L/min. Which of the following features suggests acute severe asthma in this patient?
option4: Inability to complete sentences in one breath
correct_option: Inability to complete sentences in one breath

question_text: A 30-year-old woman with bronchial asthma developed worsening dyspnea and cough following paint inhalation from neighborhood apartment. On examination, she is in respiratory distress, cannot complete a sentence and cyanosed. Chest auscultation revealed reduced breath sounds in both sides with short inspiratory and expiratory rhonchi (see lab results). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 130 /min Respiratory rate 20 /min Oxygen saturation 85 %. Test Result Normal Values ABG HCO3- 24 22-28 mmol/L. ABG PCO2 7.8 4.7-6.0 kPa. pH 7.3 7.36-7.45. ABG P02 7.5 10.6-14.2 kPa. Which of the following is the best immediate action in management?
option4: Nasal continuous positive airway pressure ventilation

question_text: A patient with poorly controlled bronchial asthma developed hypertension, which of the following antihypertensive medications is contraindicated?

A 60-year-old man with advanced squamous cell bronchogenic carcinoma presents with massive malignant right pleural effusion. He has been admitted several times with similar findings for which he undergoes several therapeutic pleural taps. Which of the following is the next best option in management?

A 40-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough and reddish nodular skin lesion over both shins. On examination, she has palpable cervical lymph nodes and bilateral diffuse fine crepitations in both lungs (see reports). Chest radiograph: Diffuse bilateral interstitial infiltrates with bilateral hilar lymph nodes enlargement. Purified protein derivative (PPD): Negative. Cervical lymph node biopsy: Non-caseating granuloma. Which of the following is the next best option in management?

A 60-year-old man with post pulmonary tuberculosis bronchiectasis presents with massive hemoptysis. Physical examination shows course crepitations in the right scapular area (see image). Which of the following is the most appropriate initial management?

A 78-year-old woman is admitted with fever and confusion due to pneumonia. The doctors decided to start her IV antibiotics. However, her son, who is the next of kin, disagreed and argued that antibiotics will cause more harm. What is the best way to deal with this situation?
option1: Ignore the opinion of the son and give the antibiotics to save the patient
option2: Counsel the son in the presence of the consultants
option3: Respect the opinion of the next of kin
option4: Take the opinion of the patient
correct_option: Counsel the son in the presence of the consultants

question_text: A young woman with long-standing rheumatoid arthritis is diagnosed to have non-specific interstitial lung disease. The physician decided to start her on oral glucocorticoid. However, the patient is concerned about the side effects related to this treatment. What is the best approach to deal with this situation?
option1: Stress the importance of the treatment and give the patient time to decide
option2: Inform the patient that refusing this treatment might cause death
option3: Inform the patient that there is no alternative treatment
option4: Tell the patient that most of the side effects are not serious
correct_option: Stress the importance of the treatment and give the patient time to decide

question_text: An elderly patient is diagnosed with pulmonary tuberculosis and started on anti-TB treatment. He developed jaundice, which progressed to liver failure for which he was admitted to the Critical Care Unit. His son was very angry and decided to complain against the treating team. What is the appropriate response to the son?
option1: Tell him that this incident can happen to anyone
option2: Inform him that the doctors offered the right management
option3: Advise him against complaining as there is no medical error
option4: Calm him down and inform him that this incident will be investigated
correct_option: Calm him down and inform him that this incident will be investigated

question_text: An elderly patient who has poorly controlled bronchial asthma with repeated admission to the hospital is found to be non-compliant with his medications. Which of the following is the most appropriate action?
option1: Tell the patient that it is not acceptable to disrespect the advice of doctors
option2: As the patient is noncompliant, just keep him in the hospital
option3: Involve the next of kin to help to give his medications as prescribed
option4: Tell the patient that he might die if he does not comply with the medications
correct_option: Involve the next of kin to help to give his medications as prescribed

question_text: A 69-year-old man with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease on oxygen therapy for the last 5 years. Which of the following markers is most useful for monitoring the progression of this patient?

A 31-year-old man with asthma complaining of increasing wheeze in the past few hours. He rarely attends asthma clinic and his previous best peak flow readings has been 400 L/min. Which of the following would suggest acute severe asthma?

A 38-year-old woman presents for annual asthma review. Her symptoms are very well controlled and she has not had any attacks since her last review a year ago and has not used her salbutamol inhaler once in that year. She is prescribed an inhaled beclomethasone steroid twice daily in addition to her salbutamol inhaler. What is the most appropriate next step in modifying her medication?
option2: Double the dose of the inhaled corticosteroid
option3: Stop both the salbutamol and beclomethasone inhalers
option4: Half the beclomethasone dose and review in 6 months
correct_option: Half the beclomethasone dose and review in 6 months

question_text: A 38-year-old man is admitted with a sudden onset dyspnoea and pleuritic chest pain. He is a smoker but has no history of respiratory disease. He considers himself healthy and regularly plays football. On admission, he has a chest x-ray that shows a pneumothorax with a 3cm rim of air. Aspiration is successful and he is discharged. A follow-up chest x-ray 2 weeks later shows a complete resolution. What is the single most important piece of advice to reduce his risk of further pneumothoraces?
option4: Arrange a course of respiratory physiotherapy pneumothorax

question_text: A 28-year-old wood worker presents to the clinic complaining of episodic shortness of breath. He describes feeling wheezy and having a tendency to cough. A diagnosis of occupational asthma is suspected. Which of the following is the most appropriate diagnostic investigation?
option1: Serial peak flow measurements at work and at home
correct_option: Serial peak flow measurements at work and at home

question_text: A 31-year-old man with asthma complaining of increasing wheeze in the past few hours. His medication history suggests a lack of compliance. His best peak flow measurements in previous consultations have been 600 L/min. Which of the following features suggests acute severe asthma in this patient?
option1: A peak flow rate of 250 L/min
correct_option: A peak flow rate of 250 L/min

question_text: A 22-year-old man, whose asthma is uncontrolled on beta agonist therapy, is commenced on a steroid inhaler. Which of the following is the most likely adverse effect?

A 58-year-old man presents with a 6-month history of breathlessness. He has been smoking all his adult life, he smokes 10-15 cigarettes per day. On examination, he has lost the nail fold angle of his fingers. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 36-year-old man with multiple health problems presents to the Emergency Department with an episode of bronchospasm. He has a history of asthma and hypertension. He is undergoing a medication review. Which of the following medications is most likely to be responsible?

A 58-year-old man with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is ready to be discharged home after acute exacerbation of the disease. Which of the following parameters indicate offering long-term oxygen therapy?
option1: 1 arterial blood gas measurement with p02 < 7,7 kPa
option2: 2 arterial blood gases measurements with p02 < 7.3 kPa
option3: 1 arterial blood gas measurement with p02 < 8.0 kPa
option4: 2 arterial blood gases measurements with p02 < 6.3 kPa
correct_option: 2 arterial blood gases measurements with p02 < 7.3 kPa

question_text: A 22-year-old woman with cystic fibrosis presents with a 4-month history of weight loss, diarrhoea and abdominal bloating. She has pale stools. There are no specific abnormalities on examination of the abdomen other than she is clubbed with scattered chest crackles. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 59-year-old man comes to the clinic, with a 45 cigarette pack-year history, complaining of breathlessness on exertion and a productive cough of white sputum over the last 6 months. Cardiological investigations are normal (see report). Spirometry: Shows an FEV1/FVC of 60% with minimal reversibility after a 2-week trial of oral steroids Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 62-year-old woman is seen in the clinic with a history of weight loss, loss of appetite and shortness of breath for the last 6 weeks. On examination, trachea is shifted to left side; there is reduced air entry and dullness to percussion on the lower to midzones of the right lung. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 102 /min Respiratory rate 22 /min Temperature 37 °C Oxygen saturation 91 % Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 19-year-old man known to have asthma. He has never been admitted to hospital with an asthma exacerbation and experiences symptoms once or twice a week but he is not on any medication. Today he come to discuss the treatment options and he prefer to start medications only if needed during the attacks. His peak expiratory flow reading is currently 85% of the normal predicted value expected for his age and height. Which of the following is the most appropriate first step in treatment?

A 40-year-old man diagnosed with pulmonary TB who started treatment 3 months ago, presents to the clinic with a week''s history of pins and needles in his hands and feet with associated numbness. He tells that his symptoms started since he stopped taking the vitamins given to him at the start of his TB treatment. Which of the following drugs is responsible for the symptoms described by the patient?

A 44-year-old woman with heart failure diagnosed 9 months ago is evaluated for 3-month history of non-productive cough. No history of chest pain, dyspnoea or orthopnoea. She has never smoked. Her current medications are lisinopril, carvedilol and furosemide. Unremarkable cardiac and chest examination (see reports). Blood pressure 100/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18/min Oxygen saturation 95 % Chest X-ray: Normal ECHO: Showed left ventricular ejection fraction 40%. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
A 45-year-old man has been diagnosed as a case of pulmonary tuberculosis. which of the following investigations is essential prior initiation of the therapy?

A 39-year-old man came to the clinic for follow-up. He is recently diagnosed as pulmonary tuberculosis and was started on rifampicin, Isoniazid, pyrazinamide and ethambutol for 4 weeks. Basic lab workup were normal. LFT test was repeated (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Direct bilirubin 56 1.5-6.5 mol/L Total bilirubin 76 3.5-16.5 mol/L Aspartate aminotransferase 465 12-40 IU/L Alanine aminotransferase 620 5-40 IU/L Alkaline phosphatase 720 39-117 IU/L Gamma glutamyltransferase 387 6 to 37 IU/L Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 54-year-old man presents with shortness of breath and fever for 1 week. On physical examination, the patient is febrile and dyspneic. His oxygen''s saturation is 89% on room air. Chest examination shows absent breath sounds and dullness to percussion over right lung field. Chest radiography confirms a large right-sided pleural effusion. Which of the following is the most useful to establish a diagnosis of exudative pleural effusion?
option3: Pleural fluid LDH >1/3 normal upper limit for serum

question_text: A 54-year-old man presents with shortness of breath and fever for 1 week. On physical examination, the patient is febrile and dyspneic. His oxygen''s saturation is 89% on room air Chest examination shows absent breath sounds and dullness to percussion over right lung field. Chest radiography confirms a large right-sided pleural effusion. Which of the following factors indicating the need for therapeutic thoracentesis?
option3: Pleural fluid glucose more than 80 mg/dL
option4: Negative gram stain of the pleural fluid

question_text: A 38-year-old man who is smoker for several years presents to the clinic with progressive shortness of breath. Pulmonary function test (PFT) done which results in the following parameters: forced expiratory volume (FEV1)/forced vital capacity (FVC) ratio < 0.7 with minimal improvement after bronchodilators low diffusing capacity for carbon monoxide (DLCO). Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 50-year-old man presents to the clinic with chronic history of productive cough and shortness of breath. A diagnosis of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is suspected. Pulmonary function test is ordered. Which of the following pulmonary function tests is most likely to represent COPD?
option1: Increased total lung capacity (TLC), decreased vital capacity (VC), decreased FEV1/FVC ratio
option2: Increased total lung capacity (TLC), decreased vital capacity (VC), increased FEV1/FVC ratio
option3: Decreased total lung capacity (TLC), increased vital capacity (VC), decreased FEV1/FVC ratio
option4: Increased total lung capacity (TLC), normal vital capacity (VC), decreased FEV1/FVC ratio
correct_option: Increased total lung capacity (TLC), decreased vital capacity (VC), decreased FEV1/FVC ratio

question_text: A 35-year-old-man presented to the Emergency Department with 5-day history of fever, shortness of breath and productive cough. Lung examination reveals dullness to percussion to approximately half of the lung field on the right and his breath sounds are markedly diminished. Chest radiography, shows right lower and middle lobes consolidation with moderate pleural effusion. Which of the following would indicate the need for tube thoracostomy?
option3: Pleural fluid with more than 90% polymorphonucleocytes (PMNs)
option4: Pleural fluid/ serum protein less than 0.4

question_text: A 36-year-old man presents to Emergency Department with 5-day history of fever, productive cough and shortness of breath. On examination, he is febrile with temperature 38.5 C and his lung examination reveals dullness to percussion with markedly diminished breath sound over half of the lung field on the right side. Chest radiography shows right lower and middle lobes consolidation and moderate pleural effusion. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option3: Start azithromycin, ceftriaxone and do thoracentesis
option4: Start azithromycin, ceftriaxone and do CT scan of the chest
correct_option: Start azithromycin, ceftriaxone and do thoracentesis

question_text: A 36-year-old man presents to Emergency Department with 4-week history of low-grade fever, non-productive cough and shortness of breath. On examination, he is febrile with temperature 37.9 °C and his lung examination reveals dullness to percussion with markedly diminished breath sound over half of the lung field on the right side. A diagnosis of tuberculous pleural effusion is suspected. Chest radiography shows moderate pleural effusion (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Leukocytes 4500 <1000/pL Total Protein 6 10-20 g/L Glucose 1.6 >2.2 mmol/L LDH 650 <200/UL Which of the following tests is the most sensitive to establish the diagnosis?
option3: Pleural fluid with very low glucose concentration
option4: Pleural fluid adenosine deaminase more than 45 IU/L
correct_option: Pleural fluid adenosine deaminase more than 45 IU/L

question_text: A patient with a diagnosis of advanced chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) who is discussing with his doctor the therapeutic benefits of different therapies on his survival rate Which of the following therapies is most likely to improve his survival?

A 64-year-old diabetic woman was recently started on hemo-dialysis through a femoral central line. During dialysis, the nurse notices that she is shivering. Detailed history reveals a 3-day history of fever. On examination, central line site was red with pus discharge. Which of the following is the most appropriate management approach?
option1: Withdraw blood culture and start IV antibiotics
option2: Start IV antibiotics and hold dialysis for 3 days
option3: Withdraw blood culture, change the line and resume dialysis
option4: Withdraw blood culture, start antibiotics and remove the line
correct_option: Withdraw blood culture, start antibiotics and remove the line

question_text: A 64-year-old diabetic woman is receiving hemo-dialysis through a femoral central line. She develops line sepsis. Blood culture grows MRSA (methicillin resistant staphylococcus aureus). She was started on IV vancomycin 1000 mg every 72 hours (to be infused over 20 min). Immediately after starting vancomycin, she started to have flushing, redness and itching over the face, neck and trunk. Which of the following is the most appropriate management approach?
option1: Stop vancomycin and label her allergic to vancomycin
option2: Continue vancomycin but a lower dose
option3: Continue vancomycin but slower infusion rate
option4: Change vancomycin to cefazolin 1000 mg every 24 hours
correct_option: Continue vancomycin but slower infusion rate

question_text: A 38-year-old woman came for routine follow up 2-months after delivery. She has no symptoms and her baby is well and lactating normally. Upon reviewing her labs, a positive urine culture: >100,000 cfu/ml E. coli was noticed which was sensitive to trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole, ciprofloxacin, and nitrofurantoin. Which of the following is the best treatment option?

A 38-year-old woman, 28-weeks pregnant came for routine follow up. She has no symptoms and her pregnancy is all well. Upon reviewing her labs, a positive urine culture: > 100,000 cfu/ml E.coli was found which was sensitive to ciprofloxacin, nitrofurantoin, trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole and ceftriaxone. Which of the following is the best treatment option?

A 45-year-old diabetic woman presented to Emergency Department with fever, flank pain and dysuria. She is allergic to penicillin. Examination shows flank tenderness (see lab result and report). Temperature 39 °C Test Result Normal Values WBC 17 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Urine culture: > 100.000 cfu/ml E.coli. Which of the following is absolutely contraindicated?

A 45-year-old woman presented to Emergency Department with fever, flank pain and dysuria (see lab result and report). Test Result Normal Values WBC 14 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Urine culture: > 100,000 cfu/ml E.coli. Which of the following is the best empirical treatment option?

A 48-year-old diabetic woman presented to Emergency Department with dysuria (see lab result and report). Test Result Normal Values WBC 14 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Creatinine 230 44-115 µmol/L Urine culture: > 100,000 cfu/ml E.coli. Which of the following medications is contraindicated?

A 62-year-old man presented with 2-weeks history of tiredness, dyspnea and 5-days history of fever. No previous surgical history. Cardiac exam shows pan-systolic murmur at the apex. Hands exam shows splinter hemorrhage on nails. Temperature 38.8 °C Which of the following is the most likely causative organism?

A 58-year-old man presented with 2-weeks history of tiredness, dyspnea and 5-days history of fever. No previous surgical history. Cardiac exam shows pan systolic murmur at the apex. Hands exam shows splinter hemorrhage on nails. Temperature 38.5 °C Which of the following is the most appropriate empirical therapy?
A 24-year-old woman is 10-weeks pregnant. She has dysuria and is diagnosed with a urinary tract infection. Which of the following antibiotics is contraindicated?

A young man comes into the clinic with intermittent hemoptysis for 2 months, associated with some blood in his urine. He is a non-smoker and was previously in excellent health. He also describes a weight gain of 20 kg over the past 3 weeks, with 'puffy eyes'" in the morning. He has an elevated serum creatinine concentration (see report). Lung Biopsy: Serum immunoglobulin on basement membranes. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"
Which of the following sites is the best to aspirate a sample of right pleural effusion with a syringe and needle?

A 45-year-old man presents with difficulty in breathing and a nocturnal cough for the last 3 weeks. Spirometry results show a forced vital capacity and the FEV/FVC ratio lower than normal. Which of the following is consistent with these findings?
What is the optimal treatment for community-acquired pneumonia in an otherwise healthy patient?
What is the optimal duration of anticoagulation following an initial episode of pulmonary embolism?
In which of the following conditions is interstitial pneumonitis a characteristic histopathologic finding?
Which of the following pneumoconiosis will commonly predispose to the development of pulmonary tuberculosis?
A 38-year-old woman with pleurisy requires a thoracentesis. The needle should be placed on the midaxillary line of the affected side. Which rib level should the needle penetrate through?

A 72-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with fever, night sweats, and cough for 2 weeks (see report). Chest X-ray: Right upper lobe consolidation. Which of the following is the best next step in management?
option2: Admit the patient to the Intensive Unit
option4: Isolate the patient in a single, negative pressure room
correct_option: Isolate the patient in a single, negative pressure room

question_text: Which of the following can improve survival in patients with severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease?

A 25-year-old patient with moderate persistent asthma is currently on a low-dose, inhaled corticosteroid but needs to use a beta-2 agonist rescue inhaler at least once a day. Which of the following should be added to the current treatment?
option2: Leukotriene inhibitor to the steroid regimen
option3: Long-acting beta-2 agonist to steroid regimen
correct_option: Long-acting beta-2 agonist to steroid regimen

question_text: A 55-year-old patient with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease presents with shortness of breath. The patient is in severe respiratory distress and hypoxemic with decreased breath sounds and a prolonged expiration phase. There is no improvement after bronchodilators. The patient is conscious with a pH of 7.3. Which of the following is the best next step in management?
Which of the following is the most effective treatment for adults with obstructive sleep apnea?
option2: Maintain a BMI less than 30 kg/m2

question_text: A 28-year-old patient with mild intermittent asthma has daytime symptoms about once to twice a week and none at night. Which of the following initial treatment should be given when necessary?

A 28-year-old woman has had mild intermittent asthma for years. She uses a short-acting bronchodilator, as needed. Over the past 2 months, she has noticed that she is using her inhaler at least 3 times a week, and on occasions has had to wake up in the middle of the night to use her inhaler. Which of the following treatments should be added?

A 55-year-old man presents with shortness of breath and a productive cough. The patient has a history of smoking. On auscultation, there is wheezing throughout the chest (see reports). Chest X-ray: Normal. Arterial Blood Gases: Normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 58-year-old woman with acute onset shortness of breath and pleuritic chest pain. She is diagnosed with pulmonary embolism. Which of the following is an indication to do thrombophilia workup?
option3: Has a history of connective tissue disease
option4: Has negative family history of thrombosis

question_text: Which of the following criteria is supporting the use of a screening test for community screening program?
option3: The test is quick and easy
option4: Natural history of the disease is not yet clear
correct_option: The test is quick and easy

question_text: A 45-year old man is just admitted to the Critical Care Unit with polytrauma following road traffic accident because of hypotension. He is sedated and mechanically ventilated. His heart sounds are distant with no added sounds. Lung auscultation showed equal air entry to both sides with no added sounds (see report). Blood pressure 85/55 mmHg. Heart rate 110 /min. Temperature 36.0 °C. Oxygen saturation 100 %. Central venous pressure: Right atrial pressure 15 mmHg. Chest radiograph: Cardiomegaly and fractured sternum. Both lungs are normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 35-year-old man is evaluated in the Orthopedic Ward because of dyspnea, tachycardia and confusion. The patient had fractured left femur for which he undergone internal fixation 2-days ago. He is on enoxaparin as prophylaxis and pain-killer as needed. On examination, the heart sounds are normal and there are scattered crepitations in both lung fields. Petechial rash around the neck and axillae are noticed (see lab result and reports). Blood pressure 120/70 mmHg. Heart rate 110 /min. Respiratory rate 26 /min. Temperature 37.8 °C. Oxygen saturation 85 %. Test Result Normal Values ABG HCO3- 22 22-28 mmol/L. ABG PCO2 4.7 4.7-6.0 kPa. pH 7.26 7.36-7.45. ABG P02 9.5 10.6-14.2 kPa. Chest radiograph: Bilateral diffuse alveolar infiltrate. Electrocardiogram: Sinus tachycardia. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 65-year-old woman with diabetes mellitus and old myocardial infarction is admitted to the Intensive Care Unit with urosepsis and septic shock. Her blood pressure did not respond to fluid challenge for which she is started on norepinephrine infusion. She has hypoxia despite optimizing the ventilator parameters and Fi02 of 100% (see table and report). Pulmonary Artery Catheter Test Result Normal Values Right atrial pressure 3 2-8 mm Hg. Pulmonary artery pressure 13/3 16-24/5-12 mm Hg. Wedge pressure 5 5-12 mm Hg. Cardiac output 4 4-6 L/min. Chest X-ray: Bilateral lung infiltrate. Which of the following is the most likely cause of the lung infiltrate?

A 55-year-old woman with a chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) on combination of long-acting β2-agonist and inhaled glucocorticoid and albuterol as needed presents to the clinic with worsening of her symptoms. On physical examination, there is no jugular venous distention. Pulmonary examination reveals decreased breath sounds. Bilateral lower extremity edema is noted to a level above the ankles. Echocardiogram is normal apart of high mean pulmonary artery pressure, 52 mmHg (see lab results). Blood pressure 125/75 mmHg. Heart rate 84 /min. Respiratory rate 20 /min. Temperature 36.6 °C. Oxygen saturation 86 %. BMI 30 kg/m2. Test Result Normal Values ABG PCO2 7.5 4.7-6.0 kPa. pH 7.6 7.36-7.45. ABG PO2 8.6 10.6-14.2 kPa. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment at this time?

A 66-year-old woman is evaluated for a 6-week history of increasing dyspnea and right-sided pleuritic chest pain with occasional cough. She has lost significant body weight in the last 3 months. She has no other medical problems and takes no medications. Her physical examination reveals dullness to percussion and decreased breath sounds over both lower lung zones. A 4-cm right breast mass is palpated. Chest radiograph demonstrates bilateral pleural effusions but no evidence of infiltrate or pulmonary vascular congestion. Thoracentesis is performed. Pleural fluid Gram stain is negative (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Total Protein 40 10-20 g/L. Fluid Protein/Serum Protein 0.67 <0.5. Glucose 3.5 >2.2 mmol/L. LDH 350 <200/UL. Total Proteins 60 60-84 g/L. Lactate dehydrogenase 125 60-160 IU/L. Leukocytes 3600 <1000/pL. Which of the following is the most appropriate statement about the diagnosis?
option1: Exudative pleural effusion due to malignancy
option2: Transudative pleural effusion due to malignancy
option3: Exudative pleural effusion due to heart failure
option4: Transudative pleural effusion due to empyema
correct_option: Exudative pleural effusion due to malignancy

question_text: A 28-year-old woman with bronchial asthma that controlled on budesonide and albuterol inhalers is evaluated in the clinic as she is 6 weeks pregnant. Her symptoms are well controlled on the treatment. However, symptoms usually flare in fall and she treats these symptoms with an add-on oral leukotriene inhibitor. She currently has no asthma symptoms. On examination, vital signs are normal. The lungs show good air entry and no wheezes. She is so concern about the safety of medications during pregnancy. What is the best information to be delivered to her with regard to medication safety?
option1: Continue the current treatments as they are safe during pregnancy
option2: Stop budesonide and continue albuterol inhaler
option3: Stop albuterol and continue budesonide inhaler
correct_option: Continue the current treatments as they are safe during pregnancy

question_text: A 26-year-old man presents to the clinic with shortness of breath and occasional wheeze that occurs several times per week. He develops significant shortness of breath when playing football. He notes that it takes several minutes to recover his breath when this occurs. He also notes coughing during exercise as well. He takes no medications. On physical examination, vital signs are normal. Mild nasal congestion is noted. His systemic examinations are unremarkable. A chest radiograph is normal. Spirometry findings are within normal parameters. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in assessment?
option4: Helical (spiral) computed tomography (CT) for the chest

question_text: A 31-year-old woman is evaluated because of daily wheezing and breathlessness that limit her activities several times a week. Medical history is otherwise unremarkable. She has never smoked. She is diagnosed with moderate persistent asthma. In addition to a short-acting β2-agonist, which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?
option1: Add a low-dose inhaled corticosteroid and long-acting β2-agonist
correct_option: Add a low-dose inhaled corticosteroid and long-acting β2-agonist

question_text: A 66-year-old woman with severe COPD is admitted with 4 days of dyspnoea and cough. She is alert, in moderate respiratory distress, and has an oxygen saturation of 93% on 3 L/min supplemental oxygen. A chest radiography demonstrates no consolidation. She receives intensive bronchodilator therapy and systemic steroids (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values ABG PCO2 8.8 4.7-6.0 kPa. pH 7.28 7.36-7.45. ABG PO2 9.9 10.6-14.2 kPa. Which of the following is the best next step in the management?
What is the most common HCV genotype in Saudi Arabia?

A 32-year-old man is evaluated in the clinic because of an abnormal chest computerised tomography (CT) scan that revealed a small lung lesion (7 mm) without any other abnormalities. He is non-smoker and he is asymptomatic. Physical examination shows no lymphadenopathy and normal lung examinations (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 140 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female). Platelets count 300 150-400 x 109/L. WBC 8 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. ESR 11 2-10 mm/h (Male) 3-15 mm/h (Female). Calcium 2.25 2.15-2.62 mol/L. Which of the following is the most appropriate procedure to do next?

A 30-year-old overweight man presents to the general physician with 6-month intermittent epigastric burning pain. He has also noted that for the past 2 months he has been experiencing episodes of nausea, an unpleasant taste in the mouth, hoarseness, and a nocturnal cough. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 40-year-old man with Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease presents to the general physician with a 3-month history of intermittent retrosternal burning pain, worse on bending, nausea, regurgitation with an unpleasant taste in the mouth, and a nocturnal cough. The patient has been using over-the-counter antacids with no relief. Which of the following is the most appropriate course of action?
An individual who has received all 3 doses of hepatitis B vaccine and has never had hepatitis B virus infection. Which of the following serologic markers is expected to be positive?
What is the immunological status of a patient with negative HBsAg, positive anti-HBc, and positive anti-HBs?
option1: Immune due to hepatitis B vaccination

question_text: A 25-year-old patient suddenly develops chest pain and dyspnea. Vocal and tactile fremitus are reduced, and breath sounds are diminished (see report). Chest X-ray: It shows absent lung markings on the left side. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which of the following is the most likely cause of lobar pneumonia in adults?

A 45-year-old man is admitted with general tiredness and fever. There is a past history of a chest infection of 4-week duration, for which he was given oral antibiotics. He felt better initially, but never fully recovered and is now becoming worse (see report). Chest X-ray: It shows a right-sided pleural effusion. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old nurse working in the pulmonary clinic complains of fever and a productive cough for the last 3 months. She has received 2 courses of oral antibiotics but showed no improvement (see image). Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 55-year-old patient presents to the clinic with shortness of breath and a cough productive of yellow sputum for the past 2 days. There has also been blood-stained sputum for the last 3 hours. The patient has smoked 2 packs of cigarettes a day for the past 35 years. On examination, there are rhonchi, wheezing, and crepitatons heard over the right hemithorax (see report). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 84 /min, Respiratory rate 22 /min, Temperature 38.8° C Oxygen saturation 95% on room air Chest X-ray: It shows a dense infiltrate in the right lower lobe. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 27-year-old woman with asthma has not had a severe attack for the last 3 months. However, she has a history of cough and wheezing 2-3 times each week. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 21-year-old woman comes to the clinic with episodes of shortness of breath on exertion for one year. She has to stop after 15-20 minutes of exercise, because of dyspnea and coughing, which get better after about one hour of rest. There are no symptoms at rest. Physical examination and spirometry are normal. Blood pressure 125/75 mmHg Heart rate 80 /min Respiratory rate 12 /min Temperature 37.0° C Which of the following is the most appropriate inhaled treatment before exercise?

A 42-year-old man with asthma presents with daily symptoms despite regularly using a long-acting beta-agonist inhaler and an inhaled high-dose corticosteroid. He also uses a short-acting beta-agonist inhaler as 'rescue'" medication. These episodes also wake him from sleep 2-3 times a week. Physical examination shows bilateral (see table). Blood pressure 140/85 mmHg Heart rate 90 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.7° C Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 Second Normal value Before bronchodilator 65 % 25-75 % After bronchodilators 91 % Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?"
option3: Observe the patient using his inhaler
correct_option: Observe the patient using his inhaler

question_text: A 67-year-old patient presents to the Emergency Department with chest pain, shortness of breath, and hemoptysis following a car trip for more than 5 hours (see report). ECG: Right axis deviation, an S1-Q3-T3 pattern, and a right bundle branch block. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 55-year-old smoker presents to a clinic with progressive shortness of breath (see report). Chest X-ray: It reveals a massive right pleural effusion. Which of the following is the best next step in management?
A 28-year-old patient develops the sudden onset of dyspnea and tachycardia 5 days post a knee surgery. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A patient with stage IIIB small cell lung cancer complains of new onset of back pain for the past 12 hours. Initial neurological examination is normal. Which of the following is the most appropriate immediate action to take?
option4: High dose IV corticosteroids and MRI
correct_option: High dose IV corticosteroids and MRI

question_text: On a follow-up consultation of a patient with a squamous cell carcinoma, some abnormalities are noted in blood analysis (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Parathyroid hormone 1.0 1.1-5.3 pmol/L Calcium 3.30 2.15-2.62 mmol/L Calcium ionized 1.6 1.1-1.3 mmol/L Sodium 140 134-146 mmol/L Potassium 4.3 3.5-5.1 mmol/L Which of the following is the most likely cause of the metabolic abnormality?
option4: Secretion of PTH-related peptide by the tumor
correct_option: Secretion of PTH-related peptide by the tumor

question_text: A 78-year-old man and current smoker, with a 50 pack-year smoking history, complains of shortness of breath, dry cough and fatigue progressing over the past 8 weeks. The patient also reports a significant weight loss, corresponding to approximately 15% of body weight over the last 12 weeks. Physical examination is normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 40-year-old man presents to the clinic complaining of numbness of the feet for 1 month. He was diagnosed with pulmonary tuberculosis 4 months ago. He has been taking isoniazid and rifampin and has 2 months of treatment remaining. He has well-controlled diabetes mellitus for the last 3 years. The physical examination is normal. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 80 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 37.7° C Which of the following drugs should be given?
Which of the following is the main method for prevention of tuberculosis?
option4: Treatment of the patients with tuberculosis
correct_option: Treatment of the patients with tuberculosis

question_text: A 55-year-old man who is a heavy smoker is being evaluated for increasing bilateral lower limb swellings. Chest examination confirms resonant lungs with reduced breath sounds and occasional rhonchi. The apex beat could not be easily located. No cardiac murmur is heard, but P2 is markedly loud. The JVP is elevated, and bilateral pitting edema was confirmed. Which of the following is the most likely cause of the lower limb swelling?

A 40-year-old man is brought to the Emergency Department with progressive difficulty in breathing. History reveals, he is being treated for bronchogenic carcinoma. On examination, the JVP is elevated; lungs clear and heart sounds are very quiet. Blood pressure 88/50 mmHg Heart rate 125 /min (regular) Respiratory rate 24 /min Temperature 36.8 C° Which of the following is the most appropriate test to confirm the diagnosis?
A patient with active rheumatoid arthritis presents with pleural effusion. Which of the following is typical finding in the pleural fluid analysis?

A 40-year-old woman presents with recurrent attacks of dyspnea and cough when exposed to dust and perfumes. Physical examination is normal except for mild expiratory wheeze on lung auscultation. Which of the following tests has the highest diagnostic value?

A 44-year-old obese woman presents with sudden onset dyspnea. No cough, chest pain orthopnea or PND. History is significant for hemorrhoidectomy 2 weeks ago (see image, lab results and report). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 105 /min Test Result Normal Values ABG HCO3- 20 22-28 mmol/L ABG PCO2 4.5 4.7-6.0 kPa pH 7.5 7.36-7.45 ABG PO2 10 10.6-14.2 kPa Chest x-ray: Normal except for the obliterated left costophrenic angle. Which of the following has the highest diagnostic value?

A 30-year-old woman with rheumatoid arthritis presents with progressive dyspnea and dry cough for 3 months. No fever or weight loss. Physical examination revealed cyanosis, digital clubbing and bibasilar end-inspiratory crackles (velcro type) on lung auscultation. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old man presents with epigastric pain and early satiety associated with 10% weight loss over 8 months (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Hb 98 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) MCV 70 80-95 fL Which of the following is the best initial action in management?
option4: Trial of proton pump inhibitor for 6 weeks

question_text: A 30-year-old woman complains of epigastric pain, heartburn and bloating after meals for 3 months. No history of hematemesis or weight loss. A trial of esomeprazole 40mg for 8 weeks did not relief her symptoms (see lab result and reports). Test Result Normal Values Hb 140 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Gastroscopy: Normal. Urea breath test: Negative. Ultrasound of abdomen: Normal. Which of the following is the best next step in management?
option4: Increase the dose of esomeprazole to 80mg/day

question_text: In a patient with liver cirrhosis and bleeding esophageal varices, treated with esophageal banding, which of the following drugs is effective in preventing future bleeding?

An 18-year-old girl presents with chronic diarrhea and intermittent abdominal pain. No history of upper or lower GI bleeding. On examination, she is slim and has pallor.
