A 67-year-old woman diagnosed with heart failure being treated with loop diuretic and ACE inhibitor with good control of her symptoms. Which of the following medications helps in reducing her long-term mortality?

A 22-year-old woman presents with retrosternal burning chest pain, which is worse during breathing and coughing. Precordial auscultation showed an added sound of 3 components; systolic, diastolic and presystolic (see image). Which of the following is the best initial treatment?

A 67-year-old diabetic patient with atrial fibrillation is hesitant to start anticoagulant due to its risk of inducing bleeding. which of the following factors is the most important in making the decision?
option1: The patient has no risk for bleeding
option2: The patient has mild risk of thrombosis
option3: The bleeding risk is higher than thrombosis risk
option4: His risk of thromboembolic events is higher than bleeding
correct_option: His risk of thromboembolic events is higher than bleeding

question_text: A 60-year-old woman admitted to the hospital for cardiac catheterization but she is concerned about its complications. Which of the following is the best way to address her concerns?
option1: Tell her it is very rare and no need to worry
option2: Inform her that her husband will sign the consent
option3: Listen to her concern and explain what are the expected complications
option4: Listen to her concern and explain what are the expected complications and give alternative
correct_option: Listen to her concern and explain what are the expected complications and give alternative

question_text: A 57-year-old man with known history of asthma, congestive heart failure and peptic ulcer disease was admitted with asthma exacerbation and rapid atrial fibrillation. He was managed with nebulized salbutamol, IV digoxin, and his home medications was continued (ACE inhibitor, Ranitidine, Spironolactone). 24 hours later, his serum potassium is noted to be 2.8 mEq/L. Which of the following medications is the most likely to cause this abnormality?

A 70-year-old man, with no past medical history, presents to the Emergency Department 4 hours after the onset of a severe crushing substernal chest pain radiating to the neck and left arm (see report). Electrocardiography: Significant ST-segment elevation in leads I, AVL, V5, and V6. Where is the most likely site of cardiac infarction?

A 60-year-old patient presents to the hospital, complaining of a sudden onset of chest pain radiating to the back. The blood pressure is low in the left arm, and the left femoral pulse is not palpable (see reports). Chest X-ray: A left pleural effusion. ECG: Left ventricular hypertrophy. Which of the following is most likely to confirm the diagnosis?

A 46-year-old man recovering from an acute myocardial infarction 10 days ago. He develops a sharp pain behind the sternum, which is made worse by coughing. The patient gets relief when sitting forward. On examination, a scratchy superficial sound is heard at the left sternal edge, and heart sounds are muffled. There is ascites and a 2 cm raised JVP is confirmed. Which of the following ECG changes would most likely be found?

A 60-year-old man is admitted to the Coronary Care Unit with an acute myocardial infarction. His hemodynamic parameters 2 hours later are: Blood pressure 80/50 mmHg Heart rate 40 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6° C Oxygen saturation 95% on room air Which of the following would be the appropriate management?

A 65-year-old patient presents with 2 hours of chest pain that feels like indigestion. In the past medical history, hypertension has been diagnosed, but no evidence of myocardial infarction or angina. This Emergency Department does not have facilities for a percutaneous transluminal angioplasty (see report). Blood pressure 170/94 mmHg Heart rate 104 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6° C Oxygen saturation 95% on room air ECG: ST segment elevation in the anterolateral leads. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial intervention?

A 60-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with heavy retrosternal chest discomfort. The discomfort started 3 days earlier as intermittent brief episodes which became more frequent and prolonged over the last 24 hours. In the Emergency Department, he experienced a similar type of discomfort (5/10 on the pain scale of 1-10) which responded well to sublingual nitroglycerin. Serial cardiac enzymes are normal (see report). ECG: An initial ECG showed ST segment depression of 0.2 mV in the antero-lateral chest leads, which later normalized completely. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 65-year-old woman is brought to the Emergency Department of a tertiary care hospital with rapid-onset shortness of breath and left shoulder and arm ache (see image). Blood pressure 100/50 mmHg Heart rate 90 /min Respiratory rate 25 /min Temperature 36.9 °C Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment after the initial Emergency Department management?

A 75-year-old man who has been followed up routinely for hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus was found to have an irregular pulse, which was not found 6 months earlier. In addition to hypertension and diabetes, his past history confirms a previous transient ischemic attack. He is currently asymptomatic and is compliant with his medications (see report). ECG: Atrial fibrillation at a rate of 70 beats per minute. Which of the following is the most appropriate management plan?
option1: Add digoxin to his current treatment regimen
option3: Start anticoagulation therapy to prevent stroke
option4: No further step is needed as he is asymptomatic
correct_option: Start anticoagulation therapy to prevent stroke

question_text: A 29-year-old woman presents to the Emergency Department complaining of being unable to sleep flat for the last 2 nights due to difficulty in breathing. She has just delivered 1 week earlier. Physical examination confirms a laterally displaced apex beat and the presence of a third heart sound. The jugular veins were distended, and bilateral lung crackles could be easily heard. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 55-year-old man had a myocardial infarction 6 days before. He suddenly develops dyspnea, cough, and frothy sputum. For the first time, a harsh systolic murmur is heard over the precordium. Which of the following is the most likely cause of this development?
In a patient with heart failure due to dilated cardiomyopathy presenting with fast atrial fibrillation, which of the following drugs is the most appropriate to control the heart rate?

A 40-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department after fainting while exercising. He reports past episodes of chest pain, which have occurred both at rest and with exercise. On examination of the chest, there is a IV systolic ejection murmur heard at the left sternal border, which does not radiate. The intensity of the murmur increases when the patient bears down or stands up. The lung fields are clear (see report). Blood pressure 125/75 mm Hg Heart rate 110 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6° C ECG: Non-specific ST and T wave changes with left atrial enlargement. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 55-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with continuous retrosternal chest pain. An ECG was obtained (see image). Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
option3: Acute coronary syndrome with unstable angina

question_text: A 60-year-old woman with congestive heart failure complains of shortness of breath for 3 months. It is precipitated by daily activity at home. The shortness of breath awakened her several times at night. Her medication included fosinopril, carvedilol, and furosemide. On examination, there is bilateral basilar crepitation (see report). Echocardiogram: There is dilated left ventricular chamber. The left ventricular ejection fraction is 30%. Blood pressure 125/80 mmHg Heart rate 60 /min Respiratory rate 16 /min Temperature 37.1° C Which of the following drugs is indicated?
Which of the following agents has been shown to reduce mortality in patients with congestive heart failure?
Which of the following is a recognized side effect of furosemide in congestive heart failure?

A 40-year-old woman presents to the Emergency Department with vomiting and generalized abdominal pain. She does not know her medications. She states that her vision has been slightly yellow tinged recently (see image). Blood pressure 90/50 mmHg Heart rate 60 /min Respiratory rate 16 /min Temperature 37° C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old patient with a previous ischemic stroke presents with a regular narrow complex tachycardia. The patient is hemodynamically stable. The decision is made to administer IV adenosine. In the presence of which of the following should the dose of adenosine be reduced?
Which of the following drugs used in the treatment of chronic heart failure has been shown to increase survival?

A 39-year-old man presents to the clinic with unexplained fever 19 days after an attack of acute rheumatic fever. Several of his teeth have been extracted prior to the hospital admission. Clinical examination confirms a systolic murmur and splenomegaly (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values WBC 14.0 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Neutrophils 72 40-60% Urinalysis: Test Result Normal Values: Erythrocytes 25 0-2 per HPF Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 62-year-old man presents with recurrent retrosternal chest pain, which he said has become much worse over the last 3 weeks. He describes the pain as lasting much longer than before and that it occurs even while he is sitting in a chair or in bed at night reading. Which of the following is the most likely type of angina?

A 72-year-old man presented with acute severe chest pain with an ECG revealing ST segment elevation in II, III, AVF leads was treated with thrombolysis but 2 days later acutely unwell with severe dyspnea. On examination, he has loud systolic murmur at the apex, which radiates to the axilla and bilateral crackles at the base of the lungs. Blood pressure 90/60 mmHg Heart rate 100 /min Respiratory rate 25 /min Oxygen saturation 88 % Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 65-year-old man with heart failure requires rate control to treat coexisting atrial fibrillation. Which of the following is the best treatment option?
Asymptomatic healthy 24-year-old woman came for a check-up visit and found to have grade II mid-diastolic murmur over the apex. Which of the following is the best next step in diagnosis?

A 70-year-old man presented with progressive dyspnea, palpitation and fatigue for 3 months. He is known to have hypertension on captopril. He underwent echo which showed poor left ventricular function so furosemide was added with good control of his symptoms. His vital signs upon the last clinic visit shown below. Blood pressure 150/90 mmHg Heart rate 90 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Oxygen saturation 95 % Which of the following medication should be added at this stage?

A 60-year-old man is evaluated in the clinic for cardiac risk assessment. He is asymptomatic and physically active. He has never smoked. He has no chronic health issues and takes no medications. His physical examinations are normal. Cardiovascular risk calculation using the Pooled Cohort Equations predicts a 6.5% risk of a myocardial infarction in the next 10 years that indicate an intermediate risk of myocardial infarction. Which of the following investigations should be done next?

A 58-year-old woman with hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus presents to the clinic for routine follow-up. She has no symptoms. Medications are hydrochlorothiazide, losartan, atorvastatin, and metformin. Cardiac examination shows a grade 2/6 diastolic murmur heard best over the apex. Peripheral pulses are normal. Electrocardiogram shows sinus rhythm with nonspecific ST changes. Blood pressure 160/80 mmHg Heart rate 78 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Oxygen saturation 96 % Which of the following is the most appropriate test to perform next?

A 55-year-old woman presents to the clinic for her regular follow-up. 6 months ago, she presented to Emergency Department with palpitations and dyspnea and diagnosed to have atrial fibrillation. She is currently asymptomatic with no palpitations. Her medical history is also significant for a transient ischemic attack and hypertension. Her medications are warfarin, metoprolol and candesartan, Her physical examination is unremarkable. Her most recent electrocardiogram shows normal sinus rhythm. which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 68-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department with progressive exertional dyspnoea, orthopnea and dry cough for 7 days. His medical history is significant for hypertension. Physical examination reveals raised central venous pressure, fine crackles at lung bases and hepatomegaly. His extremity examination reveals bilateral pitting edema. Echocardiogram shows a left ventricular ejection fraction of 60% and normal valves. Chest radiograph shows evidence of pulmonary edema (see lab results). Blood pressure 160/85 mmHg Heart rate 86 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Test Result Normal Values Sodium 140 134-146 mmol/L Potassium 4 3.5-5.1 mmol/L Urea 6 2.75-7.4 mmol/L Creatinine 88 44-115 mol/L Brain natriuretic peptide 900 0-300 ng/L Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in the management?

A 26-year-old pregnant woman visits the clinic for regular follow-up. She is asymptomatic. She is in her 30th week of pregnancy. Her physical examination reveals a mid-systolic ejection murmur at the right upper sternal border. The murmur radiates to carotids. No diastolic murmur is heard. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old man is evaluated in the Emergency Department for severe persistent chest pain for 1-day. The pain is constant but exacerbated when leaning forward and not associated with other symptoms. Physical examination is unremarkable apart of a friction rub at the left lower sternal border. Electrocardiogram shows diffuse, concave upward ST-segment elevations and PR-segment depression most prominent in leads V1 through V6. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 58-year-old woman was admitted with a diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction received appropriate treatments and now she is ready for discharge. She is so concern about the secondary prevention of infarction. Which of the following statements is the most appropriate?
option1: Anti-platelet agents should be used for short period to reduce the risk of recurrent infarction
option2: Calcium channel blockers should be used indefinitely to reduce the risk of recurrent infarction
option3: Hormonal replacement therapy should be given to postmenopausal women to prevent coronary events
option4: Angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) Inhibitors should be used indefinitely by patient with clinically evident heart failure
correct_option: Angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) Inhibitors should be used indefinitely by patient with clinically evident heart failure

question_text: A 55-year-old woman with non-decompensated systolic heart failure and ejection fraction of 25% presents to the clinic for routine follow-up. Which of the following medications is the most appropriate management?

A 45-year-old woman is evaluated for a 3-week history of progressive shortness of breath that limit her activities. Medical history is significant for asthma and moderate mitral regurgitation. Her only medication is an albuterol inhaler as needed. On physical examination, her vital signs are normal. Cardiac examination reveals a grade 3/6 holosystolic murmur radiating to the axilla. Lungs are clear to auscultation. Which of the following is the most appropriate diagnostic test?
option4: Helical (or spiral) computed tomography (CT) of the chest

question_text: A 65-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with exertional syncope and progressive shortness of breath. Clinical examination reveals fine crackles at both lung bases and mid-systolic murmur at right upper sternal border that radiated to carotids. Chest radiography demonstrated clear evidence of pulmonary edema. Which of the following statements about the assessment and diagnosis is the most accurate?
option1: The murmur will be augmented by standing position
option2: The main treatment for symptomatic patient is diuretics
option3: Electrocardiogram (ECG) will show right bundle branch block (RBBB)
option4: Presence of symptoms of congestive heart failure are poor prognosis
correct_option: Presence of symptoms of congestive heart failure are poor prognosis

question_text: A 48-year-old woman is seen in the clinic because of recurrent severe nocturnal chest pain for several months that rapidly resolved with sublingual nitrate. She had a normal cardiac catheterization and positive ergonovine echocardiography testing. She does not want to use multiple medications and she accept to use only one drug as a long-term maintenance therapy. Which of the following drugs is the best strategy for long-term management of this patient?

A 65-year-old man presents to the clinic with progressive exertional shortness of breath that associated with syncope. His examinations show a mid-systolic murmur at right upper sternal border that radiates to carotids. Chest x-ray shows cardiomegaly. ECG reveals left ventricular hypertrophy. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option1: Start antihypertensive with tight control of his blood pressure

question_text: A 28-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with sudden onset of chest pain that reduced with sitting forward associated with shortness of breath class II. No history of fever. Examinations reveal a normal heart sound with friction rub. His cardiac enzymes are within normal range. ECG shows diffuse ST segment elevation. Blood pressure 130/70 mmHg Heart rate 90 /min Respiratory rate 24 /min Temperature 37.9 °C Oxygen saturation 95 % What is the most appropriate treatment for this patient?

A 55-year-old man with congestive heart failure on multiple treatments came to the clinic for follow up. The physician discussed with him the importance of compliance and the effect of the treatments on his life. Which of the following medications is associated with reduced mortality rate in congestive heart failure?

A 70-year-old man is found to have ejection systolic murmur in the aortic area during routine medical check-up. He denied symptoms of dyspnea, chest pain or syncope. Physical examination revealed slow rising carotid pulsation; normal JVP and normal lung auscultation (see reports). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Electrocardiogram: Normal sinus rhythm with LVH. Echocardiogram: Shows concentric LVH, normal ejection fraction and severely stenosed aortic valve. Which of the following is the next step in management?

A 30-year-old woman with history of rheumatic heart disease since childhood presented with exertional dyspnea and occasional paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea. Physical examination showed JVP of 5 cm above sternal angle, loud S1, loud P2 and mid diastolic rumbling murmur in the apex. Fine basal crackles are present on lung auscultation. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old man presents with abdominal distension dyspnea and orthopnea. The JVP is markedly elevated and fails to descend during inspiration. Lower limbs showed massive pitting edema up to the thighs. Heart sounds are normal. There are bibasilar crackles on lung auscultation. Abdominal examination showed hepatomegaly and positive shifting dullness (see report). Echocardiogram: Both atria are dilated. Left and right ventricles are normal in size and LVEF is 60%. No valve lesions. Which of the following has the highest diagnostic value?

An 18-year-old boy presents with recurrent syncope with exercise. Physical examination showed normal JVP, jerky carotid pulsations and thrusting apex. There is an ejection systolic murmur, which is best heard in the left parasternal area that increases in intensity with standing and reduced during handgrip (see report). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Electrocardiogram: Shows left ventricular hypertrophy. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A young man with diagnosis of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy presented with recurrent exertional chest pain and syncope. Which of the following is the best management?

A 65-year-old woman with diabetes and hypertension, presented with retrosternal chest pain at rest for 3 hours. Physical examination is normal except for S4 gallop (see lab result and report). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 76 /min, Oxygen saturation 95 % Test Result Normal Values Troponin 10 < 0.2 pg/L Electrocardiogram: T-wave inversion in leads V2-V5. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old man with diabetes and hypertension is admitted to the Coronary Care Unit with diagnosis of unstable angina. Physical examination is normal except for S4 gallop. Aspirin, bisoprolol, enoxaparin, atorvastatin and nitrate are started (see lab result and reports). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Test Result Normal Values Troponin .001 < 0.2 pg/L Electrocardiogram: Showed ST depression in leads II, III, AVF. Echocardiogram: Normal. What additional medication is indicated?

A 30-year-old man presents with burning retrosternal chest pain associated with dyspnea. His history is significant only for common cold 2 weeks ago (see image). Which of the following clinical signs is likely to be present?

A 70-year-old man presents with dyspnea, fatigue and presyncope. On examination, the JVP showed Cannon a-wave and variations in the intensity of the first heart sound (see image). Which of the following is the definitive treatment?

A 64-year-old woman with advanced breast cancer complains of dyspnea on exertion. On examination, her systolic blood pressure is noted to markedly decrease during inspiration. Heart sounds are distant to auscultation. Chest is clear (see report). Blood pressure 95/65 mmHg, Heart rate 100 /min, Respiratory rate 24 /min, Temperature 36.6 °C, Oxygen saturation 95 % Chest x-ray: Marked cardiomegaly with clear lung fields. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 65-year-old woman with diabetes and hypercholesterolemia presents to the Emergency Department with chest tightness associated with sweating and nausea of 4-hour duration. Physical examination showed elevated JVP, which increases further during inspiration, S4 gallop and clear lung fields on chest auscultation. Aspirin and clopidogrel were given. Results of cardiac enzymes are pending (see image). Blood pressure 90/55 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Oxygen saturation 95 % Which of the following immediate action should be taken?

A 25-year-old woman presented with dyspnea and cyanosis. Physical examination revealed elevated JVP with predominant a-wave and left parasternal heave. There is an ejection systolic murmur best heard in the left second intercostal space that increases in intensity during inspiration. An ejection click is also heard in the same area Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 20 /min Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 70-year-old man is admitted for stomach resection for gastric cancer. On the 4th postoperative day, the patient developed retrosternal chest pain accompanied with dyspnea and profuse sweating. Physical examination is remarkable only for S4. Aspirin, clopidogrel and nitrate were administered (see report) Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 76 /min, Respiratory rate 18 /min, Temperature 36.6 °C Oxygen saturation 95 % Electrocardiogram: Shows ST elevation in leads V2-V5. Which of the following is the most appropriate step in management?

A 70-year-old man with hypertension, diabetes and aortic sclerosis discovered to have atrial fibrillation on routine health check-up. He denies dyspnea, chest pain or palpitations. Which of the following drugs is recommended to prevent systemic embolization?

A 40-year-old man is admitted with palpitations of 4-hour duration. He had similar attack a month ago but did not seek medical advice. His initial electrocardiogram showed atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular rate. He subsequently converted to sinus rhythm. Which of the following drugs is the most appropriate to maintain sinus rhythm?

An 18-year-old girl is recently diagnosed to have rheumatic fever with carditis. Echocardiogram showed mild mitral stenosis with mild regurgitation. To what age the rheumatic fever prophylaxis is recommended?
Which of the following is an absolute contraindication for thrombolytic therapy?
option4: Blood pressure at presentation of 180/110 mmHg

question_text: A 28-year-old woman presents to the Cardiology Outpatient Department with recurrent palpitations for the last 6 months. She describes the palpitations as fast heartbeat, which starts and ends suddenly. Each attack lasts 5-10 min. No associated chest pain, dyspnea, syncope or presyncope (see lab result and report). Test Result Normal Values Hb 132 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone 3.8 0.4 - 5.0 uU/mL. Electrocardiogram: Normal sinus rhythm with rate of 70/min. Which of the following is the best next investigation?

A 29-year-old man is seen in the pre-employment clinic for routine exam, He is asymptomatic. Physical examination revealed a systolic murmur that heard at the apex. The murmur is augmented with sustained handgrip and reduced with Valsalva manoeuvre. Which of the following is the most likely cause for this murmur?

A 52-year-old man develops sudden severe retrosternal chest pain that radiates to his back. Within minutes, he became unconscious. He is a heavy smoker and has history of hypertension. Blood pressure 85/56 mmHg Heart rate 92 /min. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 23-year-old man is brought to the hospital after an episode of collapse. He states that on a few occasions while playing football he has had some chest pain, breathlessness and felt as he was going to faint. Last night during training, he collapsed and blacked out for a few seconds. Clinical examination revealed a harsh ejection systolic murmur with a palpable systolic thrill at the left sternal edge; and a prominent jerky carotid pulse. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 68-year-old man is seen for a follow-up. He has longstanding breathlessness and symptoms of cardiac failure. The ECG confirmed a diagnosis of heart failure due to left ventricular dysfunction. His currently on enalopril 10 mg OD, simvastatin 40 mg OD, furosemide 40 mg OD and omeprazole 20 mg OD. Examination revealed that the chest is clear and heart sounds are normal. There is no peripheral edema. Blood pressure 125/83 mmHg, Heart rate 76 /min. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in the management at this stage?

A 78-year-old man diagnosed with heart failure due to left ventricular dysfunction. His currently on aspirin 75 mg OD, simvastatin 40 mg OD and furosemide 40 mg OD. On examination, heart sounds are normal, his chest is clear and there is no peripheral edema. Which of the following is the most appropriate drug therapy to initiate at this stage?

A 55-year-old man presents with a 3-month history of exertional chest pain. He has previous history of myocardial infarction. Which of the following is modifiable risk factors in the treatment of ischemic heart disease?

A 78-year-old woman presents with exertional syncope. On clinical examination, she has a slow rising carotid pulse and a loud ejection systolic murmur at the upper right sternal edge, radiating to the carotids. Which of the following is the most likely cause of these findings?
A 43-year-old healthy man concerned about heart disease. Which of the following is the most important risk factor of ischemic heart disease?
option4: 2 separate fasting plasma glucose of 8.2 and 8.3 mmol/L.
correct_option: 2 separate fasting plasma glucose of 8.2 and 8.3 mmol/L.

question_text: A 72-year-old man presented with an episode of right-sided weakness that lasted 10 minutes and fully resolved and he is clinical stable. He has no other medical illness. On clinical examination, he is in atrial fibrillation. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 95 /min, Temperature 36.6 °C. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in the management?

A 58-year-old man comes to the clinic for a follow-up. He has been discharged after an acute inferior myocardial infarction. He is diabetic type 2, on metformin 1g BD, aspirin 75 mg. atorvastatin 10 mg OD and lisinopril 10 mg OD. Examination revealed bilateral basal crackles on auscultation of the chest. Blood pressure 148/88 mmHg, Heart rate 76 /min, BMI 24 kg/m2 Which of the following is the most appropriate medication to add to his regime?

A 61-year-old woman comes to the clinic complaining of palpitations. She has also noticed some weight loss over the last 2 months. On clinical examination, she has an irregularly irregular pulse and a fine tremor. Her ECG shows atrial fibrillation. Which of the following is the next most appropriate investigation to do?

A 66-year-old man comes for follow-up. He has shortness of breath on exercise, which have increased over the past few months. He became unable to walk more than 200-300 meters on the corridor and cannot climb the stairs easily. He has history of hypertension and currently on amlodipine 5 mg OD and indapamide 2.5 mg OD. On examination, he has bilateral basal crackles and mild pitting ankle edema (see lab results). Blood pressure 188/96 mmHg Heart rate 79 /min Test Result Normal Values Hb 133 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Sodium 138 134-146 mmol/L Potassium 4.2 3.5-5.1 mmol/L Creatinine 133 44-115 µmol/L Which of the following is the most appropriate next therapy?

A 77-year-old woman comes to the clinic for a check-up. She has an irregular pulse when the nurse checked her vitals. An ECG has confirmed persistent atrial fibrillation. She is completely asymptomatic. She has a past medical history of migraine and asthma and on salbutamol PRN for asthma and PRN ibuprofen for her migraines. Other clinical examination is normal. Blood pressure 138/85 mmHg, Heart rate 88 /min. What antithrombotic treatment should be considered first line in this patient?

A 63-year-old smoker man presents with pain in his left leg, which is exacerbated by exercise and relieved by rest. Examination revealed weak pulses in the left leg compared to the right. The ankle brachial pressure Index is 0.84. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
option1: Peripheral arterial disease with critical ischemia

question_text: A 65-year-old man with diabetes and hypertension is admitted with extensive anterior ST elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI). had successful angioplasty and stenting to left anterior descending artery. Aspirin, clopidogrel, bisoprolol and enalapril were started (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Cholesterol (HDL) 1.0 > 1.03 mmol/L Cholesterol (LDL) 3.36 <3.36 mmol/L Total Cholesterol 5.0 <5.1 mmol/L Triglycerides 2.2 <2.16 mmol/L Which of the following is recommended?

A 55-year-old man with diabetes presented to the Cardiology Clinic with vague chest pain with exertion for 2 months (see report). Electrocardiogram: Normal. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 60-year-old man with poorly controlled hypertension presents with exertional dyspnea and occasional paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea. No chest pain or palpitation. On examination, he is comfortable at rest; the JVP is 3 cm above sternal border, loud A2 and left ventricular S4, no murmur (see reports). Blood pressure 160/100 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Oxygen saturation 95 % Electrocardiogram: Shows sinus rhythm and left ventricular hypertrophy. Echocardiogram: Normal cardiac chambers size, concentric left ventricular hypertrophy and normal left ventricular ejection fraction. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old man is found to have cardiomegaly on chest X-ray during pre-employment check-up. He denies chest pain, dyspnea, orthopnea or PND. Physical examination unremarkable (see reports). Blood pressure 120/80 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Electrocardiogram: Normal. Echocardiogram: Dilated left ventricle with ejection fraction of 40%. Which of the following is the best management?

A 45-year-old man with previous history of rheumatic heart disease presents with worsening dyspnea, orthopnea and PND. Physical examination shows holosystolic murmur in the apex and bibasilar crackles on lung auscultation. His symptoms improved with furosemide, spironolactone, enalapril and carvedilol (see report). Echocardiogram: Showed mildly dilated left ventricle, LVEF 45% and severe rheumatic mitral regurgitation. Which of the following is the best next step in management?

A 53-year-old woman presents to the Outpatient Department. She has been unwell for 6 months with arthralgia, fever, weight loss, and more recently has developed pleuritic chest pains. She also has synovitis of the small joints of the hands. There were no rashes or nail fold infarcts. Heart sounds are normal (see lab results and reports). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 70 /min Respiratory rate 18/min Test Result Normal Values Hb 89 120-160 g/L WBC 3.2 4-10.5 x 109/L Platelet count 99 150-400 × 109/L Neutrophils 80 40-60% Lymphocytes 17 20-40 % MCV 90 80-95 fL C-reactive peptide 99 <8.2 mg/L ESR 56 3-15 mm/h Clotting time 7 5-10 min Ferritin 920 22-340 pg/L ANA 1:320 =1:40 Pulmonary Angiography CT: Confirms a small left pleural effusion. There is no consolidation, pulmonary embolus, and no lymphadenopathy. ECG: Sinus tachycardia. Which of the following the most likely diagnosis?

A 47-year-old patient presents with 12 hours of severe pain and swelling of the right first metatarsophalangeal joint. On examination, the affected joint is erythematous and tender. Blood pressure 110/65 mmHg, Heart rate 105 /min, Respiratory rate 12 /min, Temperature 38°C. Which of the following is the most likely cause?

A 52-year-old patient presents with isolated pain and swelling of the left knee. Arthrocentesis demonstrates innumerable needle-shaped negatively birefringent crystals. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 32-year-old woman complains of severe joint pain in the fingers of both hands, especially in the morning during the winter months. Examination confirms deformity of her fingers. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

On examination, he had unremarkable general physical and systemic examination. Blood pressure 130/70 mmHg, BMI 26 kg/m2 Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

question_text: A 35-year-old man was found to have a 5.5-cm left adrenal mass with irregular borders and a density of > 10 HU on non-contrast CT of the abdomen. In view of the suspicious radiological features of the mass, the patient was referred to the endocrine surgical team for an elective adrenalectomy. Which of the following investigations should be done prior to an elective adrenalectomy?
option2: Aldosterone to plasma renin activity ratio

question_text: A 75-year-old type 2 diabetic undergoes a bowel resection for colon cancer. He is well prior to the operation with well-controlled diabetes and no other underlying condition. The operation is successful and the patient is given postoperative insulin and IV dextrose. 2 days after the operation, he becomes very agitated (see lab results). On examination, he is euvolemic (see lab results). An 18-year-old man is complaining of red urine for 5 days. He has no abdominal pain and no dysuria, urgency or frequency. His history is significant for tonsillitis 3 weeks ago. Physical examination is normal except for elevated blood pressure. Blood pressure 160/100 mmHg, Heart rate 76 /min Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 73-year-old diabetic man presents with right-sided weakness. The weakness is equal in the right face, arm and leg. Sensation, speech and comprehension are intact. Which of the following arteries is affected?

A 70-year-old man complains of a sudden severe headache while bending down to pick up his keys this morning. He describes it as the worst headache he has ever had; he says it was like being hit on the back of the neck. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 48-year-old woman has a 2-year history of a slowly progressive bilateral tremor. The tremor interferes with her writing and eating. She has recently noted head bobbing and a change in her voice. Which of the following would be an appropriate first-line medication for her problem?

A 45-year-old man presents with a sudden onset of unilateral peripheral facial nerve weakness 1 hour ago. He has no medical illness and not on any medication. Which of the following is most likely to shorten his symptoms?

A 36-year-old woman presented with 2-year history of recurrent headaches. These headaches occur 3 to 4 times per month and last 12 to 24 hours. The headaches are usually located over the right or left frontal sinuses. This particular headache has lasted 2 days now, and she has some nausea associated with it. The patient has been using an over-the counter analgesic-decongestant combination without significant relief. Neurological examination is within normal limits. The optic fundi are normal, Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Temperature 36.6 °C What is the appropriate next step to determine the etiology of this headache?
option2: CT scan of the paranasal sinuses

question_text: A 50-year-old diabetic woman admitted to the medical ward with chest pain. The chest X-ray is suggestive of empyema. The radiologist was consulted and advised to do CT guided aspiration. Who should perform the informed consent process?
option4: Any member of the treating team

question_text: An internal medicine resident is teaching the medical students how to examine a diabetic patient. He forgot to close the curtain while exposing the patient's abdomen. Which one of the ethical principles is violated?
A 70-year-old man is diagnosed with metastatic cancer. His family asked the oncologist not to tell their father of the diagnosis. Which of the following is the most appropriate action?
option2: Inform the patient about his diagnosis
option4: Do not inform the patient about his diagnosis
correct_option: Inform the patient about his diagnosis

question_text: A 78-year man with post chemotherapy and radiotherapy end stage cancer is suffering from unbearable pain. His family asked the oncologist to increase the pain killer doses to relieve their father's pain. The oncologist agreed after explaining to the family that the higher doses may hasten their father's death. Which of the following principles is used to make the decision?

A 70-year-old woman admitted for a hip replacement operation. The surgeon was interrupted by the patient when he was trying to explain the surgery and its possible complications. The patient expressed her wish to not knowing the details. The surgeon respected her wish and informed the anesthesiologist who insisted to explain to the patient all details. Which of the following actions should be taken by the surgeon?
option2: Let the anesthesiologist deal with the patient
option3: Inform the patient about the anesthesiologist decision
option4: Reschedule the operation and find another anesthesiologist

question_text: A 40-year-old diabetic man presents to the Emergency Room with charcot knee. He was stabilized and transferred to the Surgical Ward. The surgical team explained to the patient the need for amputation. The patient refused. Which of the following is the most appropriate next action?
option2: Refer the patient to another hospital
option3: Consult the hospitals clinical ethics committee
option4: Ask the patient to sign discharge against medical advice form

question_text: A 20-year-old man was admitted for a simple tonsillectomy. The operation took longer time than usual because of a bleeding resulted from a surgical error. The surgery was successful and the patient fully recovered with no complications. Next day the patient asked the surgeon why his operation took longer time than usual. Which of the following is the most appropriate action?
option2: Reassure the patient and tell him what happened
option3: Ask another surgeon from the team to tell the patient
option4: Reassure the patient but do not tell him what happened
correct_option: Reassure the patient and tell him what happened

question_text: A 30-year-old woman presented to the Emergency Room with abdominal pain. The signs and symptoms are suggestive of acute appendicitis. During the surgery, the appendix found in normal state. Appendectomy was done as it is the common practice is to remove the appendix even if it is not inflamed. The surgeon does not remember that he informed the patient about this decision during the consent procedure. What should the surgeon do when the patient recovered next day?
option1: Tell the patient that it is part of the standard procedure
option2: Ask another surgeon from the team to tell the patient
option4: Do not tell the patient what happened
correct_option: Tell the patient that it is part of the standard procedure

question_text: A 90-year-old man with advanced metastatic lung cancer was admitted to the Surgical Intensive Care Unit with deteriorating pulmonary functions and labelled a do not resuscitate case by the intensivist. The thoracic surgeon would like to do a de-bulking operation to enable the patient to breath but not sure, if the patient could survive the operation, Which of the following is the most appropriate action?
option1: Explain to the patient and ask him to sign high risk consent form
option2: Do not operate since the patient is likely not going to improve
option4: Discuss the case with the intensivist
correct_option: Do not operate since the patient is likely not going to improve

question_text: Which of the following is the percentage prevalence of smoking among Saudi adult men?

A 42-year-old man presented to the Primary Care Clinic for the routine checkup. He has been smoking 1- pack of cigarettes a day for 22 years. He has no intention of quitting and feels fine. Which of the following is the most appropriate cessation approach?
option1: Set a 'quit date'" with the patient"
option3: Give clear, personalized advice to quit
option4: Refer him to classes for smoking cessation
correct_option: Give clear, personalized advice to quit

question_text: A 45-year-old smoker came to the clinic for his diabetes follow-up. During discussion, he acknowledged that smoking is not good for his health. He plans to quit this year. According to the Stages of Change Model, at what stage of change is this patient?
Tobacco products consists of thousands of chemical compounds including nicotine. Which of the following is the most outcome related to nicotine substance?

option1: Ask, advise, assess, assist, arrange follow-up
option2: Ask, assist, advise, advocate, arrange follow-up
option3: Assess, advocate, ask, advise, arrange follow-up
option4: Advocate, ask, assess, advise, arrange follow-up
correct_option: Ask, advise, assess, assist, arrange follow-up

question_text: What is the minimum number of days per week recommended to practice muscle-strengthening physical activity?

A 55-year-old woman has just completed her screening mammogram and the results were normal. She has no symptoms and no family history of breast cancer. When she should have another mammogram?

An 18-year-old boy known to have grand mal epilepsy since childhood is brought to the Emergency Department after having repeated tonic-clonic seizures at home. He is compliant with his medication, which is sodium valproate. In the Emergency Department, he is unconscious and continuously convulsing. After oxygenation and securing the airway, which of the following intravenous drugs should be administered?

A 79-year-old man was admitted to the Intensive Care Unit with features of sepsis due to severe urinary tract infection. His blood tests showed thyroid dysfunction that was attributed to a non-thyroidal illness or sick euthyroid state. Which one of the following is the common typical pattern of thyroid dysfunction seen in a sick euthyroid illness?
option1: Elevated levels of T3 and T4; low levels of reverse T3
option2: Elevated levels of T3, T4, and reverse T3
option3: Low levels of T3 and T4; elevated levels of reverse T3
option4: Low levels of T3 and T4; normal level of reverse T3
correct_option: Low levels of T3 and T4; elevated levels of reverse T3

question_text: A 24-year-old man presented to thyroid clinic with a 5-month history of feeling unwell, weight loss, anxiety, and panic attacks. He also complained of redness and grittiness of eyes, with painful eye movements. On examination, he had bilateral tremors, proptosis, and lid retraction. Which one of the following factors is associated with increased risk for the development of severe Grave''s orbitopathy?

A 39-year-old man incidentally found to have a 2-cm left adrenal gland mass while he underwent CT of the abdomen to investigate the cause of her pelvic pain. Which one of the following is the most common cause of an incidentally detected adrenal mass?

A 40-year-old woman was incidentally detected to have a 13-mm pituitary macro-adenoma on a CT of the head arranged to evaluate cause for her recurrent headaches. Apart from the headaches that she had suffered for the last 8 months, she had no history of any significant medical illness and was not on any regular medications. Her general physical and systemic examination was unremarkable. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in her management?

A 25-year-old woman complains of dizziness and feeling light-headed. She is a teacher and most frequently experiences this when she stand up in the class. She has never fainted. She lost 5 kg, but attributes this to eating healthy food. She has noticed a recent scar on the back of her hand, which has started to turn very dark as well as generalized skin hyperpigmentation. Which of the following DPP4 inhibitor does not require any dose modification in renal disease?
A 50-year-old woman recently diagnosed with papillary thyroid carcinoma. Which of the following is an example of positive communication skill while explaining the management option of her disease?
option2: Ask a patient to repeat your instructions
option3: Interrupt a patient when she ask questions
option4: Inform patients when you cannot spend any more time with them
correct_option: Ask a patient to repeat your instructions

question_text: A 30-year-old woman recently diagnosed with Cushing's syndrome. Which of the following will be considered as negative communication skill while explaining for her the course of her disease?
option2: Looking directly at a patient when speaking
option3: Interrupt a patient when he/she does not understand
option4: Encouraging the patient to ask questions
correct_option: Interrupt a patient when he/she does not understand

question_text: At which estimated GFR level, metformin should be stopped in diabetic patients?

A 35-year-old pregnant woman with gestational age of 30-weeks comes for a follow-up. The physician decided to start insulin but she is totally refusing injections and she asks about any safe oral medication to be used in pregnancy. Which of the following oral hypoglycemic agents has safety data and recommended to be used in pregnancy?

A 44-year-old man with a strong family history of type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease was recently diagnosed with pre-diabetes. BMI 31 kg/m² Test Result Normal Values HbA1C 6.1 4.7-5.6 % In helping this individual to achieve and maintain his target weight loss, which of the following is the best advice?
option2: Exercising 30 minutes per day on 5 days per week
option3: Diet low in protein and relatively higher in carbohydrates
option4: Exercise for at least 30 minutes most days of the week and low carbohydrate diet
correct_option: Exercise for at least 30 minutes most days of the week and low carbohydrate diet

question_text: A 29-year-old woman comes to the clinic for a follow-up. She is diagnosed with gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) at week 24. She is asking for an advice regarding the best dietary habits she can follow to control her blood sugar and avoid insulin injection. Which of the following is the best diet option?
option4: Energy restriction is not recommended during pregnancy

question_text: A 62-year-old woman receives a diagnosis of prediabetes based on routine lab work showing mildly elevated glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c). She has had obesity for many years. She eats a diet rich in processed foods. She rarely exercises. Physical exam reveals mildly elevated blood pressure along with trace acanthosis nigricans at the nape of the neck. Blood Pressure 145/87 mmHg BMI 30 kg/m² What advice should be given to reduce her risk of developing diabetes, in addition to increasing her physical activity to >150 minutes per week?
option1: Low glycemic index diet to produce 5-10 % weight loss
option2: Low fat diet to produce 5-10 % weight loss
option3: Diet is less effective than metformin to reduce the risk of diabetes
option4: Low carbohydrate diets are more likely than low fat diets to prevent diabetes in the long-term
correct_option: Low fat diet to produce 5-10 % weight loss

question_text: In a patient with Guillain Barre Syndrome, which of the following is the best treatment option?

A 40-year-old man comes to the clinic for evaluation and management of obesity. option1: Emphasizing the importance of adherence to his medications
option2: Referral to an orthopedic surgeon for knee arthroplasty

Her insulin doses are all administered through a continuous insulin infusion pump using insulin aspart. She reported that she would try to become pregnant next month (see lab results). option3: Her age and risk factors indicate the need to begin taking a statin at this time

correct_option: Delay conception until her HbA1c is as close to normal as possible, if this can be safely achieved

question_text: A 25-year-old woman pregnant at 15 weeks presents to the clinic complaining of palpitations, tremor and diarrhea for the last 3 weeks. option4: Explain clearly the risk for her and the fetus and try to convince her to start the treatment Immediately
correct_option: Explain clearly the risk for her and the fetus and try to convince her to start the treatment Immediately

Test Result Normal Values Calcium 2.72 2.15-2.62 mol/L Creatinine 196 44-115 mol/L Urinary protein: creatinine ratio 154 mg/mmol (<30) Which of the following is most likely diagnosis?

A 58-year-old hypertensive patient presented with a 10-day history of a left-sided hemiparesis. The CT scan of the brain confirmed an area of infarction. There was no significant finding on a clinical examination apart from his hemiparesis. The patient had already started physiotherapy and is taking 20 mg lisinopril and hydrochlorothiazide once daily. Blood pressure 140/90 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6° C Oxygen saturation 95% on room air Which of the following is the best management?

A 55-year-old woman presents with sudden left eye visual loss for 20 minutes. The vision returned to normal after this episode. History reveals, she has diabetes mellitus. The physical examination is normal. Blood pressure 125/75 mmHg Heart rate 75 /min Respiratory rate 16/min Temperature 37.2° C Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old patient presents to the clinic with a 3-month history of stumbling, weak grip, dysphagia, and generalized weakness, and also had 2 episodes of aspiration pneumonia in the last 2 months. Examination confirms brisk reflexes, spastic muscles, and fasciculation of the tongue and thigh. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A man on treatment for multiple sclerosis presents with acute onset diplopia, ataxia, and weakness of the right hand (see report). MRI brain scan: New active lesions compared to previous scans. Which of the following is the best treatment for this development?

A 71-year-old man comes to the Outpatient Clinic with difficulty walking and a hand tremor at rest for the last 3 months. There is no history of trauma or past illness. General examination reveals an expressionless face, slow and slurred speech, and a hand tremor at rest. There is no sensory abnormality, but the motor system showed brisk reflexes and hypertonia. The gait is quick, with short steps and diminished associated movements, such as no arm swinging. Which of the following is the most probable location of the defect?

A 70-year-old patient presents with a 6-month history of decline in cognitive capacity and conscious level. On examination, the patient has bradykinesia, resting tremors, a mask-like face, and a shuffling gait. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 32-year-old patient is having repetitive generalized seizures that continue for 35 minutes. The patient received 20 mg of diazepam intravenously, but there has been no response. Which of the following is the best next step in management?

A 44-year-old woman comes to her physician as she is unable to close her left eye. There is a facial asymmetry when she smiles and inability to wrinkle the left side of her forehead. Which of the following nerves is mostly affected?
A 35-year-old woman comes to the clinic complaining of severe 'pins and needles'" sensations on the dorsum of her left hand over parts of the thumb
option1: index and middle fingers. History reveals
option2: she works as a typist. On physical examination
option3: the patient has weakness of wrist dorsiflexion and fingers extension. Which of the following nerves is most likely to be injured?"

Which of the following would be the most suitable treatment?

A 77-year-old woman with poorly controlled long-standing type 2 diabetes and hypertension is complaining of dizziness when standing up rapidly. Sitting: Blood pressure 140/85 mmHg Standing: Blood pressure 115/80 mmHg Heart rate 95 /min (unchanged on standing) Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6° C Which of the following is the most likely explanation?

A 41-year-old man complains of double vision. Whilst looking forward the patient''s left eye turns towards the nose. On looking to the patient''s right there is no obvious squint. A 42-year-old man comes to the clinic with his brother. The brother is concerned that he has been acting strangely over the past 4 months. In particular, he has been irritable and criticizes everyone at home. The changes were subtle at first but now arguments arise every other day. He has difficulty initiating conversation and even pauses halfway through sentences. There is no family history of note and he is not taking any medications. A 66-year-old man is referred to Neurology Outpatients with arm pain, stiffness and imbalance. Following investigations, he is diagnosed with degenerative cervical myelopathy. question_text: A 50-year-old man with diabetes and hypertension, presents to the Emergency Department with sudden onset left sided body weakness for 4 hours. A 16-year-old boy presents with a several days history of progressive arm and leg weakness. No diplopia or loss of sphincter control. He has been well except for an upper respiratory infection 2 weeks ago. On examination, his breathing is slow and shallow. His heart rate and blood pressure are fluctuant. There is symmetrical weakness of the face and all four extremities. Deep tendon reflexes are absent, and sensations are intact. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old man presents with an 8-week history of severe headache. The headaches have occurred at the same time each day, about 2 a.m. and last around 30 minutes. A 55-year-old man with diabetes and hypertension complains of pain in the right eye and double vision when looking to the left. On examination, there is ptosis, limited adduction, elevation, and depression of the right eye. The pupils are equal and reactive in both sides. Fundoscopy and other cranial nerve are normal as well as the rest of neurological examination of the upper and lower limbs. Which of the following is the most likely cause of these findings?

A 30-year-old woman presents with pain in the right eye associated progressive visual loss over 2 days. She also reported a numbness in the left side of the face and weakness in both lower limbs. Physical examination revealed reduced visual acuity, loss of direct light reflex with preserved indirect light reflex in the right eye. Lower limbs examination showed weakness, clasp knife rigidity, hyperreflexia in both knee and ankle jerks. There is loss of all modalities of sensation in both lower limbs.
