An 80-year-old man has 2-month history of progressive gait disturbance and incontinence. Lately he has been forgetful, confused, and withdrawn. His gait is slow, short-stepped and with tendency to fall backward (see report). Brain CT scan: shows dilated ventricles. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

An elderly patient sustained a major stroke and he is intubated in the intensive Care Unit. However, even he was in a very critical state, he managed to write in the bed clothes that he wants to die in peace. Few days later, he passed away without resuscitation according to what he wrote. His son was very angry and said that his father had no mental capacity to decide. What is the most appropriate stand when dealing with this situation?
option1: Find out whether the son is the real next of kin
option2: Tell the son that in anyway his father would have never made it
option3: Inform the son that the doctors respected the wish of your father
option4: Inform the son that we do not resuscitate patients who are brain dead
correct_option: Find out whether the son is the real next of kin

question_text: A young woman is diagnosed to have multiple sclerosis. She went through an emotional breakdown during breaking bad news. Which of the following is the most appropriate way to express empathy?
option1: I share you your feelings and will support you
option2: Pull yourself up and follow our instructions
option3: I am very sorry for what happened
option4: Do not worry, you will be fine
correct_option: I share you your feelings and will support you

question_text: A 65-year-old man presented with 6-month symptoms of Parkinsonism, early dementia, fluctuations in cognition and visual hallucinations. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
An 81-year-old man with memory loss and difficulty recognizing his grandsons. He has no cardiovascular risk factors. Which of the following is the most common cause of his cognitive impairment?

An 82-year-old woman at long term nursing facility who wears hearing aids and is found to have bilateral cerumen impaction, a Weber test that lateralizes to the right, and a bilaterally positive Rinne test. Which of the following is the most likely explanation?
option2: Presbycusis that is greater on the right
option3: Conductive hearing loss that is greater on the right
option4: Chronic serous otitis media that is greater on the left
correct_option: Presbycusis that is greater on the right

question_text: A 79-year-old man comes to the clinic for a follow-up. He has a 1-year history of progressive memory impairment and clinical evidence of early Alzheimer dementia. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial diagnostic tests?

A 75-year-old man comes to the clinic with hypertension and gradual loss of cognitive function. He has predominant executive function impairment (see report). MRI: Reveals diffuse periventricular white-matter hyperintensities. Which of the following is most likely diagnosis?

A 77-year-old man with a 7-years history of Parkinson disease reports hallucinations, confusion and worsening motor function. On physical examination, he is kinetic and rigid with no tremor. He has prominent gait disorder and postural instability. Which of the following symptoms is most strongly associated with an increased risk for dementia?
option4: Difficulty reading an article in a single sitting

question_text: A son of 78-year-old patient brought a medical report of his father cognitive function status stating that he has 'mild cognitive impairment'". Which of the following is indicating mild cognitive impairment?"
option4: Impairment in at least 1 activity of daily living

question_text: A 67-year-old man comes to the clinic complaining that he has a tremor in the right hand. It is best displayed when he reaches for a pen and is manifested easily in the finger-to-nose test. In which of the following locations is the lesion?

A 72-year-old woman with Parkinson''s disease, complaining of a very annoying tremor of the dominant right hand. She is not on any medication. She states that her tremor is the only feature that disturbs her functioning. Which of the medications would be most likely to improve her tremor?

A 69-year-old man comes to the clinic complaining of increasing fatigue and drowsiness and difficulty to arouse. History reveals he was in a vacation 2 weeks ago. Although he admits to taking several falls, he is unaware of any head injury. Neurological examination showed no lateralizing or focal findings. Head and scalp examinations are negative for focal tenderness and deformity. Plain X-ray of the skull is negative (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values RBC 5.1 4.7-6.1 x 1012/L (Male) 4.2-5.4 x 1012/L (Female). Hb 132 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female). HCT 0.40 0.42-0.52 (Male) 0.37-0.48 (Female). MCH 30 28-33 pg/cell. MCHC 322 320-360 g/L. MCV 88 80-95 fL. Reticulocyte 0.9 0.2-1.2 %. Platelets count 200 150-400 x 109/L. WBC 5.5 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. Neutrophils 50 40-60%. Eosinophils 2 1-4 %. Basophils 0.8 0.5-1 %. Lymphocytes 32 20-40 %. Monocytes 4.1 2-8 %. Calcium 2.3 2.15-2.62 mmol/L. Calcium ionized 1.2 1.1-1.3 mmol/L. Magnesium 0.8 0.7-1.2 mmol/L. Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone 4.4 0.4 - 5.0 µU/mL. Thyroxine (T4 free serum) 12 8.5 - 15.2 pmol/L. Sodium 136 134-146 mmol/L. Potassium 4.2 3.5-5.1 mmol/L. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which level of prevention we apply when detecting a pregnant woman infected with hepatitis B?

A 65-year-old man with a history of myocardial infarction (see lab results). Blood pressure 140/90 mmHg BMI 25 kg/m2 Test Result Normal Values Cholesterol (LDL) 3.7 <3.36 mmol/L Cholesterol (HDL) 1.1 > 1.03 mmol/L Apo A-1 1.37 0.88-1.8 g/L (Male) 0.98-2.1 g/L (Female) Apo B-100 1.16 0.55-1.51 g/L (Male) 0.44-1.48 g/L (Female) Which of the following is the most powerful risk prediction value for cardiovascular disease?

A 78-year-old man presents with a 2-year history of slow and progressive deterioration in his memory. The patient is able to perform some activities of daily living, including dressing and bathing. His personality has changed from that of a kind and caring person into someone who has periods of agitation and aggression. Neurologic and musculoskeletal examination cannot be carried out. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 46-year-old woman with schizophrenia presents to the clinic with a 1-month history of abnormal orofacial movements. She has been prescribed amisulpride 600mg for many years and procyclidine 5 mg daily. Which of the following is the most likely cause?
Which of the following is the peak of nicotine withdrawal symptoms after smoking cessation?
Which of the following is an alternative treatment for severe depression?
Which of the following is the antidote for an opiate overdose?
Which of the following is the most likely side effect of bupropion when prescribed for smoking cessation?

A 24-year-old inactive woman presents to the clinic with a 5-month history of amenorrhea. On examination, she has a body mass index of 20, stable for the last 5 years (see lab results). Pregnancy test: Negative. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which of the following factors is the most important in determining the success of a smoking cessation program?

A 45-year-old man newly diagnosed with type 2 diabetes presents to the clinic for further management. He is on time for the appointment, but because of an emergency case, the physician arrives late to the clinic. When the physician walks in, the patient appears extremely angry. Which of the following is the most appropriate response by the physician?
option2: Explore the reasons for the anger
option3: Help the patient understand their anger
option4: Empathize with newly diagnosed diabetic patient

question_text: A 56-year-old man presents to the clinic for evaluation. The physician has difficulty in obtaining accurate information regarding the reason for attendance. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in consultation?
option1: Use medical terms that the patient understands
option2: Control the interview with direct questions
option3: Involve the patient in the treatment plan

question_text: A 76-year-old man admitted with a diagnosis of pneumonia has fluctuating levels of attention, changes in awareness, and a marked disturbance of the sleep/wake cycle. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial management?
option1: Isolation in a quiet, dark room
option2: Allow a relative to stay with the patient
correct_option: Allow a relative to stay with the patient

question_text: A 27-year-old woman delivered a healthy baby 3 months ago. She is currently undergoing psychotherapy for postpartum depression. Which of the following is the most appropriate to be added to optimize treatment?
option1: Prescribe very small doses of imipramine

question_text: A 23-year-old man complains of having an unusually prominent jaw. He has been seen by 3 plastic surgeons, but each one has refused to do anything. On examination, he appears depressed and has no visible protrusion, the jaw and face are normal. He has a past medical history of rhinoplasty and blepharoplasty. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 41-year-old man presents to the clinic requesting a complete examination to exclude colon cancer. He recently had some investigations done and provided a list of test results. He complains that this fear is interfering severely with his work and social activities. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 16-year-old boy learns about the death of his father and within hours, his speech becomes disorganized, he is walking outside of his house with no clothes on, and he claims that his father is inside the house and telling him to do these things in his honor. The symptoms resolved after 3 days, and the son returned to full pre-morbid level of function but is obviously confused over the events of the past few days. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which of the following is a prediction of the feeling of hopelessness?

A 20-year-old woman presents to the clinic with a history of abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, localized weakness to the right leg, and has a history of irregular menses. In the past, she has sought treatment for head, back, joint, and chest pain. Factitious disorder and malingering have been excluded as diagnoses. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which of the following investigations should be recommended for an elderly patient with cognitive deficit?

An 8-year-old boy recently moved to another city. Soon after moving, school started, but the boy hesitated to make friends with anyone and had a hard time paying attention in class. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 48-year-old man presents for a routine visit. While reporting his medical history, he reveals a significant alcohol intake. The physician wants to assess the drinking further using the cut- annoyed-guilty-eye (CAGE) screening questionnaire. Which of the following is a question in the CAGE screening questionnaire?
option1: Do you eat more after you drink?
option2: Do you think you are an alcoholic?
option3: Have you ever felt guilty about your drinking?
option4: How many drinks do you consume?
correct_option: Have you ever felt guilty about your drinking?

question_text: A 46-year-old woman presents to the clinic with an 8-month history of irritability, excessive worry, and difficulty falling asleep and concentrating. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 42-year-old woman is concerned about losing her job because of tiredness and would like to take antidepressants because of having a difficult time falling asleep and getting up in the morning. Her sleep disturbance is related to the inability to relax after getting into bed. She repeatedly checks to make sure that the door is locked, the oven is off, and the children''s school supplies are organized. A similar routine occurs in the morning. She states that her behavior is out of control but if is not done, she will not be able to sleep or leave the house. Which of the following disorders is the most likely diagnosis?
What is the recommended age in years to start screening for high blood pressure in adults with no risk factors?
Which of the following is a recommended age in years for a low-risk adult man to start screening for colon cancer?

A screening test for liver cirrhosis was administered to 800 people with proven liver cirrhosis and to 800 people without liver cirrhosis. The test results were positive for 200 of the proven cases and 100 of the normal persons. What is the sensitivity of the screening test?
A new screening test for diabetes has a sensitivity of 90% and specificity of 80%. Which of the following is the best interpretation?
option2: 80% of diabetic patients had positive result
option3: The test was positive for 90% of diabetic patients
option4: For each 100 positive test results, 90% were diabetics
correct_option: The test was positive for 90% of diabetic patients

question_text: An 82-year-old woman is admitted at noon with pneumonia to the General Medical Ward. At night, she is found disoriented and restless by the nursing staff. Blood pressure 152/88 mmHg Heart rate 99 /min Respiratory rate 22 /min Temperature 38.2 °C Oxygen saturation 94 % Which one of the following is the best initial step the nurse should do?
option1: Call her family to come and watch her at bedside
option2: Restrain the patient physically in her bed
option3: Orient the patient to her environment, start nasal O2 and raise the rails of her bed
option4: Call the duty doctor for Lorazepam order of 5 mg IV Stat
correct_option: Orient the patient to her environment, start nasal O2 and raise the rails of her bed

question_text: A 77-year-old woman is admitted to Neurology Ward with right hemispheric intracranial bleeding. 5 days after admission the physiotherapist reported verbally to the nurse that the patient left buttock is getting red with skin loss. The nurse forgot to tell the resident and no action was taken. 2 days later, the patient spiked a fever with hypotension. On examination, the resident found purulent discharge from the pressure sore. Which of the following is the best approach to prevent this incident?

A 92-year-old man was admitted after fall at home. There was no fracture. Foley catheter was inserted due to immobility from pain. His appetite was good. Acetaminophen with codeine was prescribed for pain. 4 days later, the patient became confused with agitation. The nurse noted cloudy urine in the bag. Blood pressure 168/92 mmHg Heart rate 105 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 38.2 °C Oxygen saturation 98 % What is the best action to prevent this complication?
option1: Avoid Codeine in this age group
option2: Request frequent turning of the patient by family
option3: Review the need for urinary catheter daily
correct_option: Review the need for urinary catheter daily

question_text: A 45-year-old woman underwent open laparotomy for a ruptured appendicitis. 3 days postoperatively, she developed fever again with severe abdominal pain and vomiting. On examination, her abdomen was rigid with decreased bowel sounds. CT scan showed foreign body in right iliac fossa. Patient was taken urgently back to Operating Room and was found to have a missed infected sponge in her peritoneal cavity (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values WBC 27 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Neutrophils 95 40-60% Creatinine 87 44-115 µmol/L Which of the following is the most appropriate action by the surgeon?
option1: Report the surgical assistant mistake to the administration
option2: Do not inform the patient about the finding
option3: Call his malpractice lawyer and report the incident
option4: Inform the patient with apology about the mistake
correct_option: Inform the patient with apology about the mistake

question_text: An intern was attending Operating Room for laparoscopic nephrectomy for suspicious renal mass. The urologist asked to prep for the right kidney approach. However, the intern remember from his history and exam that it was the left kidney. Which of the following is the most appropriate action by the intern?
option1: Keep quiet since he might be wrong
option2: Leave OR and check the electronic note
option3: Call the chief intern to ask for guidance
option4: Inform the surgeon about his concern
correct_option: Inform the surgeon about his concern

question_text: While doing ward round the consultant surgeon asked the resident to write Pipercillin-Tazobactam for their patient. The pharmacist reported that the patient has a questionable allergy to penicillin. The consultant insisted to write the order. Which of the following is the most appropriate action by the resident?
option1: Write the order and let the consultant sign it
option2: Write the order then call chief surgeon to complain
option3: Gently refuse to write the order
option4: Write the order and keep quiet fearing retaliation
correct_option: Gently refuse to write the order

question_text: Which of the following is considered a secondary prevention for diabetes?
In cardiovascular disease prevention, which of the following is considered primordial prevention?
option1: Prevention of coronary heart disease risk factors
option2: Initiation of therapy to reduce recurrent coronary heart disease events
option3: Decrease cardiac mortality in patients with established coronary heart disease
option4: Modification of risk factors in order to prevent or delay the onset of coronary heart disease
correct_option: Prevention of coronary heart disease risk factors

question_text: Which of the following factors is directly related to increased risk of stroke?

A mother brings her 2-week-old infant to the Well-Baby Clinic for a check-up. During the visit, the physician found the child to be normal but the mother gives inappropriate answers to the questions. She appeared disoriented in time and date. She states that the child is deformed and unlikely to live long. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 34-year-old woman presents to the clinic for postpartum care. She has experienced tearfulness and irritability during the 1 week following the delivery of her child. This episode resolved spontaneously and her mood has been normal since then. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which of the following conditions is most often associated with manic behavior?

A 21-year-old woman presents to the clinic with a history of changes in the voice that she hears. Until now, it had been merely making critical comments about everything she did. For the past 2 weeks, however, she thinks the voices seemed to have been able to 'suck thoughts out'" of her and send them to others. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"

A 26-year-old man presents to the clinic with a history of unusual behavior. He keeps a blanket over the television believing that the government uses the actors to spy through the TV. He believes that the instructions to cover the TV came from God who spoke through the light bulb. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old healthy man complains of episodes of chest tightness, palpitations, dizziness and breathlessness. These episodes resolved after about 5 minutes. He remains normal between episodes and the interval may extend for several days before a new episode occurs. He has had constant anxiety about having another attack and feels sad about the situation. The patient cannot identify a triggering mechanism. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old man gives a history of pancreatic carcinoma and reports having lost all his prescriptions. He says that despite having recently completed chemotherapy he has developed new symptoms and is afraid of a recurrence. There are no medical records for him in the hospitals. When confronted with this information, the patient ran away from the clinic. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 40-year-old woman presents to the clinic with a 2-month history of a sleep disturbance, loss of interest, low energy, poor memory, and suicidal ideation. On examination, she looks weak and inactive. Laboratory investigations are normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 19-year-old healthy man is brought to the clinic for evaluation of progressive erratic behaviour. Over the past 4 months, he has believed that people on television are reading his thoughts. He expressed the presence of supernatural powers and that he has begun to act out in response to these ideas. He has not caused injuries, but the trends are becoming more violent. On examination, he is normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which of the following is the most appropriate management plan for major depression?
option1: Select any drug to start, as all have equal efficacy
option2: Initiate therapy with one medication for 2 weeks
option4: Change medications if the patient doesn't respond
correct_option: Select any drug to start, as all have equal efficacy

question_text: Which of the following systems is stimulated by stressful situations?

Stress leads to an increase in cortisol and catecholamine secretion that can alter the levels of lymphocyte production that fight disease. Which of the following is the most likely explanation for these effects?
Which of the following is the main difference between delirium and dementia?

A 72-year-old man experiences inability to sustain attention, disorganized thinking, hallucinations, and disorientation commencing 12 hours after an aortic femoral popliteal bypass. The symptoms fluctuated during the next 2 days. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 70-year-old man with Alzheimer''s dementia becomes agitated and aggressive after developing hallucinations and delusions. A complete evaluation does not reveal any environmental, medical, or psychiatric cause, and behavioral interventions failed to improve the agitation. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 78-year-old man is brought to the Outpatient Clinic with a 2-year history of slow and progressive deterioration in his memory. He is able to perform some activities of daily living, including dressing and bathing, as well as cooking. He has personality changes, once a kind and caring parent; he now displays periods of both agitation and aggression. Neurologic and musculoskeletal examinations cannot be carried out. Which of the following is the best management?
option3: Admission to a chronic care facility

question_text: A 30-year-old man presents to the Outpatient Clinic with manifestations of opioid withdrawal. After full investigations, he was diagnosed as a heroin addict. As part of the rehabilitation process, he was prescribed a substitute for the addictive drug. Which of the following drugs should be prescribed?
Which of the following is described as 'False belief not in accordance with a person''s intelligence or culture'"?"

A 43-year-old woman delivered a full-term infant. On examination, he has epicanthic folds, delayed milestones, flat facial profile, simian palmar creases, and an atrial septal defect. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 65-year-old man with congestive heart failure and osteoarthritis moved into a nursing home 3 months ago. During the past 4 weeks, the patient has lost 3.6 kg, lost interest in all social activities, and has been crying almost every day. The mood is worse in the morning. At times, especially when the mood is impaired, the patient''s short-term memory appears to be lost. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 19-year-old girl presents with a 12 kg weight loss over the last 6 months. She feels fat with an intense fear of gaining weight. Over the last 4 months, the patient has had amenorrhea. On examination, she has lanugo hair distribution. Height 167 cm Weight 42 kg Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 29-year-old woman presents to the clinic with recurrent attacks of palpitations, chest pain, difficulty in breathing, and a fear of dying. She also trembles, experiences numbness and tingling sensations in the hands and feet. The past medical history is not significant. On examination, she looks tense, and the hands are cold with fine tremors. Heart rate 108 /min, regular Temperature 37.2° C Blood pressure 120/82 mmHg Respiratory rate 14 /min Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 29-year-old man presents with a 2-year history of intense fear before giving classes as a teacher in a secondary school. He claims that it is only a matter of time before a major teaching mistake is made. Which of the following phobias is the most likely diagnosis?

A 55-year-old man presents to the clinic with a 2-month history of anxiety, irritability, impotence, difficulty in sleeping, and a weight loss of 10 kg. There is no evidence of marital disharmony or any external causes for the patient''s anxiety. The physical examination was normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which of the following is the most likely a characteristic of a major depressive illness?

A 52-year-old woman presents with a 5-day history of insomnia and crying because of the death of a sibling. The patient has not slept for the last 2 days. Which of the following is the most appropriate short course of therapy?
Which of the following factors indicates a poor prognosis for schizophrenia?
A 72-year-old man who lost his spouse 2 months ago after a prolonged illness presents with a sad mood, decreased appetite, and sleep disturbance. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Which of the following drug mechanisms of action is most appropriate to treat obsessional behavioral disorder?
Which of the following medications causes insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness?

A 76-year-old man newly diagnosed with depression started on 30 mg amitriptyline each night. Shortly after the beginning of treatment, he complains of dizziness every morning. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option1: Change to a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor
option2: Advise to take the medication with food
option3: Advice to take the medication in the morning
option4: Change amitriptyline dose to 10 mg 3 times a day
correct_option: Change amitriptyline dose to 10 mg 3 times a day

question_text: Which of the following is a potential side effect of amitriptyline?

A 12-year-old obese boy presents to the Primary Care Clinic with a history of being teased by children at school and, as a result, has considered 'taking a lot of pills to just go to sleep and not wake up.'" Which of the following is the most appropriate action?"

A 76-year-old man presents to the Psychiatry Clinic for follow-up accompanied by his son. He has progressive memory loss, difficulty in finding words, wandering, and agitation, as well as urinary and fecal incontinence for the last 3 years. On examination, he was conscious but not oriented in time and place; his short memory was severely impaired with an intact long memory (see report). Brain CT scan: Atrophied hippocampal and temporal lobes. Which of the following chromosomes harbors the mutated gene is responsible for this presentation?
Which of the following clinical signs caused by a lesion in Wernicke's area?
option1: Can understand but unable to say words easily
option2: Cannot understand spoken or written words
option3: Loss of ability to recognize faces
correct_option: Cannot understand spoken or written words

question_text: A 55-year-old woman presents to the Primary Health Care Centre complaining of loss of interest and pleasure in daily activities for 3 weeks. She also complains that she has difficulty in concentrating and sleeping. Further, she has also expressed that she feels that she is worthless in the world and therefore wants to end her life. Which of the following biochemical substance deficiency is the most likely cause?

A 55-year-old woman presents to the Primary Health Care Centre complaining of loss of interest and pleasure in daily activities for 3 weeks. She also complains that she has difficulty in concentrating and sleeping. Further, she has also expressed that she feels that she is worthless in the world and therefore wants to end her life. Which of the following reuptake inhibitors is the best treatment?
Which of the following drugs used in Alzheimer's disease is most likely to cause hepatotoxicity?
Which of the following antipsychotic medications is most likely to cause weight gain?

A 35-year-old depressed housewife was started with antidepressants. After 2 weeks of taking the medication, the patient started complaining of constipation. Which of the following groups of antidepressants is the most likely cause?
Which of the following conditions is the most likely cause of dementia?

A 15-year-old girl who had a serious disagreement with her friend. She did not want to meet her friend or talk to her anymore. If the girl saw her friend coming towards her or sitting in a room, she would move away and not go in that direction. Which of the following defense mechanisms is the most likely explanation for this behavior?

A 65-year-old man with depression presents to the clinic for a follow-up visit. The physician did his consultation using a bio-psycho-social model of treatment. Which of the following is a characteristic of this treatment model?

A 36-year-old diabetic woman presents to the clinic for a follow-up visit, and after an initial assessment, the physician wished to perform a physical examination. He first asked her permission, which was granted. Which of the following ethical measures was the physician adopting?

A 40-year-old diabetic man was treated appropriately with small doses of insulin. On the next follow-up visit, he bought a very expensive watch as a gesture of gratitude to his physician. Which of the following is the most appropriate response?
option1: Accept and advise not to repeat

question_text: A 36-year-old newly diagnosed hypertensive man presents for follow-up visit. After a thorough assessment, the physician discussed with him his diagnosis, the etiology of his illness, the probable side effects of the prescribed medicine, and the prognosis of his illness and finally the restrictions the patient might experience due to the illness. Which of the following professional skills is the physician used?
Which of the following is the most useful communication technique when taking a patient history?
Which of the following is an example of the open-ended questions in consultation?
option2: Tell me about the pain in your chest.
option3: Point to the area of pain in your chest.
option4: Have you seen a doctor in these 76 months?

question_text: A 6-year-old boy is brought to the clinic by his mother. She noticed that the child has been eating clay and paper for the last 4 months. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial treatment?

A 34-year-old woman presents to the clinic with a 6-month history of extreme fear of going to the park, the zoo, and sporting events. This has worsened to the point where she has stopped going out of the house. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old man presents in an anxious, tearful state. He complains of chronic, upper abdominal pain and is certain that he has stomach cancer, but no one believes him. He has seen 6 different physicians in the past year and has had a GI series and barium enema performed, an ultrasound of the abdomen twice, and a CT scan. The patient was thinking of next going to a Herbal Clinic. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 35-year-old healthy man presents to the clinic with a 3-month history of feeling depressed. He is distressed at work and burnt-out. Four months earlier, he was promoted to a managerial position and was having an interpersonal conflict with 2 of the employees. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

An 18-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with a 20-minute history of lightheadedness, headaches, and nausea. He complains of tingling around the mouth and in the fingertips. On examination, he is anxious, tremulous, sweating, and breathing heavily. Which of the following is the most appropriate first action?
option1: Patient rebreathe into a paper bag
correct_option: Patient rebreathe into a paper bag

question_text: Which of the following atypical antipsychotic agents is associated with the most weight gain?
Which of the following is the best treatment for generalized anxiety disorder?
For which of the following childhood psychiatric disorders, clozapine is the most appropriate medication?
Which of the following antidepressants has high efficacy in childhood and adolescent depression?
A 30-year-old man presents to the clinic with a 3-month history of aliens talking to him and putting thoughts into his mind. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

A 55-year-old man presents to the clinic with a history of difficulty maintaining erections and impotence that occurs during stressful periods of life. He is currently under stress at work. On examination, he has normal physical findings. Which of the following is the first step in management?
option3: Refer to a urologist for evaluation

question_text: A 16-year-old girl is brought by her father to the Emergency Department with complaints of tachypnea, light-headedness, paraesthesia, and palpitations. Her father gives the history that she has recently failed her math test. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 38-year-old woman presents with a 3-month history of recurrent intense fear accompanied by irritability, excessive worry, palpitations, and chest tightness. She is avoiding social gatherings fearing of having the same symptoms. Physical examination findings are normal. Which of the following medications is the most appropriate first line of treatment?

A 5-year-old boy is brought to the clinic by his mother with a history of impaired language development, impaired intelligence, compulsive repetitive behavior, and a preoccupation with inanimate objects. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A mother of a 3-month-old baby presents to the clinic and tells the physician that she is 'going crazy.'" She states that since the baby has been born
option1: she has continuously visualized a snake crawling into the baby''s crib at night. She states that she knows it is not likely to happen but cannot get the thought out of her mind. She checks the baby''s crib up to 50 times per night to ensure that there is no snake. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"

question_text: A 50-year-old woman presents to the clinic with a history of anxiety. Two months ago, during a meeting at her job, she became very anxious, sweaty, and short of breath. She had to leave the meeting to calm down. Since then, she has avoided meeting rooms out of fear that she will have a recurrence of her symptoms. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 24-year-old man presents to the clinic with a history of gradual fear of automobiles. He has always been anxious about cars, but now can no longer look at them without experiencing shortness of breath, sweating, and fear. Which of the following disorders is the most likely diagnosis?

A 21-year-old woman presents to the clinic with a history of red dry skin on her hands. She washes her hands up to 70 times per day because she always feels dirty. Washing her hands relieves her anxiety, but as she touches anything, she wants to wash again. Which of the following disorders is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old man with a history of drug abuse presents to the clinic weekly with a variety of health complaints, which vary each time, and are greatly exaggerated. When he is unaware of being observed he appears asymptomatic. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 19-year-old woman presents to the clinic for a routine physical check-up. On examination, she has fine hair all over the body. She feels fat and subsequently diets. BMI 16.8 kg/m2. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 48-year-old woman calls the clinic expressing fear of leaving the house and has been inside for 2 months. She becomes anxious at the thought of walking out the front door and is now unable to enter the room containing the front door. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 27-year-old man presents to the clinic with a history of sweating, palpitations, nausea, and a 'panicky feeling'" when required to deliver a speech at work. He has no other difficulties at work. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"

A 56-year-old woman presents to the clinic with a history of constant counting of floor tiles, cars, items on a dinner plate, and words in a conversation. She is upset by the counting but becomes anxious and agitated when she tries to stop herself from counting. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 61-year-old man presents with a history of constantly thinking about aliens landing in his backyard, especially when away from home. Despite knowing that aliens do not exist, he feels overwhelmed with the idea that they have landed. He fears that he is 'going insane.'" Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"

A 38-year-old man presents to the clinic with a history of hearing voices coming from the toaster and refrigerator, warning that all the food is poisoned. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
A 27-year-old man presents to the clinic for a routine check-up. On examination, he kept looking to the right frequently. When questioned, he said, 'My mother is there
option1: but no one can see her except me.'" He claims that she comes and goes
option2: even though the family indicates she died when the patient was a child. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?"

question_text: A 17-year-old man with a history of intravenous drug abuse presents to the clinic for clinical evaluation. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in investigation?

A 17-year-old high school athlete presents to the clinic for an annual physical check-up. On examination, he has gained 20 Kg in the past 4 months and has very well-developed musculature of the upper body, acne over the face and back and foul breath. Heart rate 94 /min. Temperature 37.6° C. Blood pressure 138/89 mmHg. Respiratory rate 14 /min. Which of the following should the patient be screened for?

A 52-year-old man presents to the clinic for a routine physical check-up. He has a history of pulling out hair when under stress. On examination, he has diffuse hair thinning along with the absence of eyelashes. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 15-year-old girl presents to the clinic with history of tetany, swollen glands, and eroded dental enamel (see lab result). Electrolytes test Result Normal Value Potassium 2.8 3.5-5.1 mmol/L. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 27-year-old man presents to the clinic with a 1-month history of low mood and lack of sleep. It was associated with weight gain, isolation, and concentration impairment. His wife mentioned that he bought a very expensive car and thinking that he is a wealthy man 3 months ago. He is very talkative and difficult to interrupt. Which of the following medications is the most effective for long-term management?
Which of the following is the diagnostic criteria for Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder in children, according to the International Classification of Diseases-10?

A 23-year-old woman with frequent panic attacks presents to clinic complains that during her last attack 3 days ago she felt that she was disconnected from the world, as if the environment is strange. Which of the following would best describe her feelings?
A 30-year-old man has a personality disorder, which is characterized by being aggressive and violent. Which of the following is most likely associated with violence?
Which of the following is associated with dyslexia?
option4: Defect in visual or hearing acuity

question_text: Which of the following is the most likely clinical presentation of delusional patients?

A 7-year-old boy is brought by his mother because he was unable to sit in school without interrupting the class, wandering around the classroom or poking his neighbors. On examination, he has normal growth parameters. Which of the following is the best management?

A 30-year-old woman presents with a complaint of lower back pain for 1 year. During the last 2 years, she had presented with recurrent abdominal pain, bloating, headache, and migratory joint pain. Her menstrual periods are irregular and heavy. She was terminated twice from the job due to frequent absence from work. She is upset that proper diagnosis or treatment was not given to her. She had multiple laboratory tests done during the last 2 years and all were normal. The physical examination is unremarkable. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 23-year-old man with bronchial asthma presents with a 5-year history of severe anxiety in social gatherings. He often has sweating and palpitation when he leads discussions at work. He prefers to sit alone at home. He notices that his presenting complaint is provoked by salbutamol. His physical examination is unremarkable. Which of the following is the most appropriate therapy?

A 28-year-old woman with bipolar disorder, treated for 6 months, presents for a follow-up visit. On examination, she has 8 kg weight gain, generalized weakness, and hair loss. Which of the following is the most likely cause?
A 56-year-old man with schizophrenia is started on neuroleptic medications to treat his psychotic symptoms. Which of the following side effects is the patient at greatest risk?
Which of the following is the most likely benefit of using antipsychotic therapy in schizophrenic patients?

A 23-year-old man is brought to the psychiatrist clinic because for the first time he started to hear some voices talking to him with unknown sources. On examination, he was apathetic and emotionally withdrawn. Which of the following is the best management?

A 45-year-old man with recurrent episodes of schizophrenia is on maintenance therapy to maintain suppression of symptoms, prevent relapse, improve quality of life, and support engagement in psychosocial therapy. Which of the following is the best outcome following the maintenance therapy?
option3: 70% will have a satisfactory quality of life

question_text: Which of the following selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors is the most toxic in overdose?

A 23-year-old woman is newly diagnosed with paranoid schizophrenia based on the presence of both negative and positive symptoms of schizophrenia. Which of the following is the most effective medication?
Which of the following is the lowest fatal dose of tricyclic antidepressant?

A 40-year-old drug addict man presents to the Emergency Department with 1-hour history of unconsciousness. On examination, he has pinpoint pupils and a respiratory rate of 8/minute. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 53-year-old woman with history of depression presents to the Emergency Department with history of nausea, vomiting, epigastric pain, tinnitus, and shortness of breath 6 hours after consuming 50 tablets of aspirin (see lab result). Test Result Normal Value Plasma salicylate concentration 54 15-30 mg/dL Which of the following is the most appropriate initial management?

A 34-year-old healthy man presents to the clinic with a 2-year history of worsening sad mood, poor appetite, insomnia, fatigue, low self-esteem, and feelings of hopelessness. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 9-year-old girl is brought to the clinic by her mother with a bald patch on her scalp. Her mother noted that she plucks her hair while studying and under stress. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 20-year-old man presents to the clinic with poor personal hygiene, echolalia, echopraxia, muttering to self, sitting in an odd posture for hours, and insomnia for the past 2 months. On examination, his orientation and memory are normal. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 45-year-old woman presents to the Primary Health Care Clinic with symptoms of anxiety due to difficulties in her relationship with her co-workers. Her examination was normal and the doctor has decided that there is no need for any investigation. Which of the following would be the most effective approach for counselling?
option1: Instructing how to deal with others

question_text: A 55-year-old smoker presents to the Primary Health Care Clinic for the result of the biopsy of a lesion in his right lung. When informed that he has cancer in his right lung, he asked with anxiety, 'Is it a mistake?'" Which of the following is the most appropriate description for the patient''s reaction?"

A 30-year-old man presents to the clinic with a 10-month history of restlessness, difficulty concentrating, and disturbance in his sleep pattern. He is diagnosed with generalized anxiety disorder. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

An 18-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with a 1-week history of euphoria, increased blood pressure, tachycardia, nausea, weight loss, psychomotor agitation, chills, sweating, and visual hallucinations. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 3-year-old child is brought to the clinic for his yearly physical examination. His history revealed various behavioral issues, including poor interaction with peers and family, delayed language development, repetitive movements and difficulty of accepting change. The child stiffens physically when touched, does not respond to the examiner and does not make eye contact. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 23-year-old woman is admitted to the Psychiatric Unit after causing superficial lacerations to her wrists. The reason is that she felt that her therapist has abandoned her. She has a depressed mood that varies by the hour. On admission, she claimed that she has heard a voice speaking to her, although she currently denies it. She caused a disagreement between the psychiatric resident and the nursing staff, who have conflicting views of her behavior in the unit. Which of the following personality disorders is the most likely diagnosis?

A 40-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with irritability, suspiciousness, overactivity and poor hygiene. Emergency Department doctors regarded his behavior as abnormal and called for a psychiatrist. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 58-year-old man presents to the clinic with a history of disturbed recent memory as a cardinal feature of his disturbed state of mind along with behavioral changes. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old man presents to the clinic complaining of reduced energy, lack of enjoyment, reduced sleep, reduced appetite, suicidal ideas and lack of self-confidence. Examination and lab results are normal. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old was brought to the Emergency Department complaining that his neighbors are trying to harm him and are planning to kill him. On questioning, he was very sure about this and said that he is still hearing their voices talking with each other, though none of them were present. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 26-year-old woman presents to the Psychiatric Outpatient Clinic with a history of psychomotor excitation and irritability. She has grandiose delusions and auditory hallucinations. On a mental state examination, she exhibited a lack of insight and flight of ideas. Which of the following disorders is the most likely diagnosis?

A 32-year-old man presents to the clinic with a 2-year history of getting orgasm too readily during the sexual act. He complains that during sexual intercourse he is having palpitations and worrying thoughts in his mind all the time. On examination, he has normal findings. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 16-year-old girl presents for a follow-up visit complaining that she is unable to eat because of an intense desire to reduce her weight. Sometimes she develops an impulse to eat excessively and she then follows the impulse. She then intermittently self-induces vomiting. On examination, she looked weak. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 20-year-old student presents to the Psychiatric Outpatient Clinic complaining that she feels embarrassed, dizzy, and tremulous whenever she tries to answer verbally in the classroom. Which of the following is the best treatment?

A 21-year-old man with depression presents for a follow-up visit. During the consultation, the psychiatrist asked, 'Do you think that you are mentally ill?'" Which of the following findings is the psychiatrist wants to assess?"

A 23-year-old college student was brought to the Psychiatric Outpatient Clinic with a 3-month history of persecutory delusions, hearing voices commenting on his actions, and disorganized behavior without disturbance of mood, then returning to normal with no medication. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 34-year-old man presents to the clinic for routine visit. During the consultation, he speaks loudly and rapidly without completely expressing one thought before beginning another. Speech patterns are rhyming and the patient is easily distracted by cues in the immediate surroundings. Which of the following is the most likely description for these findings?

A 22-year-old man presents to the clinic with a history of approaching strangers and asking them the same inappropriate question without stopping when instructed to do so. The patient seemed unaware of the fact that this was an inappropriate behavior. Which of the following is the most likely description for this behavior?

A patient received a severe blow on the inferolateral side of the left knee joint while playing football. Radiographic examination confirmed a fracture of the head and neck of the fibula. Which of the following nerves is most vulnerable for damaged?
A 19-year-old fell on an outstretched hand while playing football. Examination confirmed tenderness over the anatomic snuff box. Which of the following is the most suspected fracture?

A 45-year-old man had transurethral catheterization before going for a long surgical procedure. Postoperatively he developed haematuria and a urethral injury is suspected. Which of the following is most likely injured part of urethra?

A patient presented after a blunt injury over the left leg. Examination confirmed a sensory loss on adjacent sides of the great and second toes, and impaired dorsiflexion of the foot. Which of the following nerves is most likely injured?

A 60-year-old man presents to the Emergency Department with epigastric pain radiating to the back. He is a smoker and has long-standing diabetes and hypertension. Abdominal examination reveals a pulsatile supra-umbilical mass. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department complaining of lower abdominal pain for 3 days. He gave a history of being hit by a piece of wood in the same site of pain 4 days back. Examination revealed lethargic patient in pain, which was aggravated by moving his leg. there was grayish turbid semipurulent secretions from a small break in the skin of right lower quadrant (see lab result). Test Result Normal Values WBC 33.1 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in the management?

A 37-year-old man was involved in a fight and sustained a stab wound to the neck. On presentation, patient was conscious, alert. Examination showed a 2 cm wound anterior to the left ear and close to the angle of the mandible with blood ooze. which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 19-year-old woman brought to the Emergency Department after being stabbed to the right upper quadrant. On arrival, she was conscious and oriented. Examination showed only a penetrating wound to the anterior abdominal wall at anterior axillary line just below the right costal margin. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 25-year-old man brought to the Emergency Department after he was involved in motor vehicle crash, which was driving at a speed of 130 km/hour. Patient was wearing seat belt. On arrival to the Emergency Department, he was conscious, and alert (see report). Chest X-ray: Normal. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 33-year-old man was involved in a motor vehicle crash. On arrival to the Emergency Department, he was conscious and alert. Examination showed an open wound to the anterior surface of the neck with devitalized tissue. Blood pressure 100/60 mmHg Heart rate 104 /min Respiratory rate 28 /min Oxygen saturation 87% Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 56-year-old woman was exposed to chemical burns to her trunk. On arrival to the Emergency Department, she was conscious and alert. Examination showed a 6 cm x 11 cm wound at her back with burned clothes and powder of chemicals on her clothes and skin. Which of the following is most appropriate next step after removing her clothes?

A 23-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department after he fell down from 3 m height on his right leg. Examination revealed a deformity of right leg with an open wound in the mid-leg and devitalized tissue and dirt in the wound. The leg was put in a splint. Blood pressure 112/70 mmHg Heart rate 99 /min Respiratory rate 22 /min Oxygen saturation 95 % Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 17-year-old man had a crush injury to his left leg after a heavy object fell on it, 5 hours prior to presentation. He was complaining of severe pain in his leg. Examination revealed a conscious and alert patient. His left leg was cold with delayed capillary refilling. Blood pressure 116/67 mmHg Heart rate 95 /min Respiratory rate 23 /min Oxygen saturation 95 % Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 37-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department with vomiting blood after attacks of excessive vomiting (see lab results). Blood pressure 112/63 mmHg Heart rate 89 /min Respiratory rate 21 /min Oxygen saturation 96 % Test Result Normal Values Hb 135 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) WBC 7.8 4.5-10.5 x 109/L. Which of the following is the most appropriate management plan?

A 63-year-old woman presented to the Surgical Clinic complaining of upper abdominal pain and shortness of breath and occasional attacks of palpitation after meals, for several years. These symptoms relieved by vomiting. Which of the following is the most appropriate method of investigations?

A 40-year-old woman presented with heartburn at the epigastric and retrosternal areas, and symptoms of regurgitation. The patient has tried conservative measures, including proton pumps inhibitors with no improvement in symptoms (see report). Endoscopy: Erythema of the esophagus consistent with reflux esophagitis. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 73-year-old woman underwent percutaneous trans-hepatic cholangiography for treatment of obstructive jaundice. 24 hours after the procedure, she started to have upper gastrointestinal bleeding (see lab results). Blood pressure 105/62 mmHg Heart rate 89 /min Respiratory rate 22 /min Oxygen saturation 94 % Test Result Normal Values Hb 103 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) WBC 7.8 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in the management plan?

A 24-year-old man recovers from an attack of severe pancreatitis. He has mild epigastric discomfort, sensation of bloating, and loss of appetite. Examination reveals an epigastric fullness. The swelling increases in size over a 3-week period of observation (see report). Ultrasound: Confirmed to be a pseudocyst. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 32-year-old diabetic woman has taken contraceptive pills for 12 years develops right upper quadrant pain (see report). CT scan: A 5-cm hypodense lesion in the right lobe of the liver. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
A 68-year-old man has been advised to undergo surgical treatment for a left reducible inguinal hernia. He is otherwise healthy. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 27-year-old man presented to the Surgical Clinic complaining of right groin pain for few weeks, which is aggravated by exercising. Examination confirmed normal external genitalia and groin region. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 42-year-old woman underwent an unevenful laparoscopic cholecystectomy. 2 weeks later, she presents to the Emergency Department with vague abdominal pain (see lab results and report). Blood pressure 117/59 mmHg, Heart rate 83 /min Respiratory rate 19 /min Temperature 37.4 °C Oxygen saturation 95 % Test Result Normal Values Hb 103 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) WBC 7.8 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Total bilirubin 3.7 3.5-16.5 µmol/L CT scan: Large collection in the sub-hepatic area. Which of the following is most appropriate next step?

A 57-year-old man presents with 2 days of abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting. Examination revealed a distended, tympanic and mildly tender abdomen without rebound or guarding (see lab results and reports). Test Result Normal Values Hb 153 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) WBC 12.6 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Plain Abdominal X-ray: Multiple air-fluid levels. CT scan: Multiple dilated loops of small bowel with a transition point in the distal small bowel, with some adjacent mesenteric fat stranding. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?

A 35-year-old woman 1-day post total thyroidectomy was observed by the nurses to develop a spasm of her right hand during blood pressure measurement from the right arm. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 30-year-old man presented to Surgical Clinic with right inguinal hernia. He was complaining of cough for the last 6 months. and did not use any treatment for it. He insisted to have the surgery and threatened the surgeon that he will raise a complain if did not book him for surgery. Which of the following is the most appropriate action?

During a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, the surgeon encountered a difficult anatomy and there was a CBD injury, which was repaired and patient did very well. Which of the following is the most appropriate post-operative action?
option4: Call a colleague to tell them

question_text: A 32-year-old man was operated for a self-induced rectal injury. After the surgery, the family was asking about the cause of his illness. Which of the following is the most appropriate response?

A 5-year-old girl brought by her family with rectal bleeding. During examination, it was observed that there is an abnormal relation between the parents. Examination confirmed multiple bruises over the child body. which of the following is the most appropriate action?
option4: Talk to the family about your concern

question_text: A 60-year-old woman was admitted for elective laparoscopic cholecystectomy. During the procedure, the surgeon found a big mass in the stomach Which of the following is the most appropriate action?

A 28-year-old man is brought to Emergency Room with head injury after motor vehicle accident, he is making incomprehensive sounds, opens his eyes to painful stimuli and flexes his limbs to painful stimuli, and his pupils react sluggishly to light. What is his Glasgow Coma Score?

A 33-year-old woman diagnosed with papillary thyroid cancer and planned for total thyroidectomy, she has mitral valve prolapse with mild regurgitation, no other co-morbidity. Which of the following is most appropriate option to prevent infection?

A 55-year-old woman is admitted with obstructed ventral hernia for emergency exploration; patient is obese, diabetic and was discharged 7 days ago after surgical correction of retinal detachment. Which of following is most appropriate DVT prophylaxis?

A 65-year-old man diagnosed with obstructing lower esophageal cancer and planned for surgery, patient is cachectic with more than 20% weight loss over 3 months. The treating team decided to start on preoperative TPN. Which of the following is the most expected electrolyte abnormality?

A 33-year-old woman otherwise healthy, underwent laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy. complicated by pulmonary embolism, she was started on heparin infusion, and after 5 days (see lab results). Test Result Normal Values Platelets count 59 150-400 × 109/L APTT 53 30-40 sec INR 1.2 0.8-1.2 Prothrombin time 14 10-13 sec Which of the following is the most appropriate management?
option2: Increase IV heparin to reach PTT 60-70
option4: Continue on same dose and repeat labs after 6 hours

question_text: A 17-year-old man presented to Emergency Room with right lower quadrant pain and anorexia. Examination revealed tenderness and rebound tenderness at Mc Burney''s point (see image, lab result and report). Heart rate 92 /min, Temperature 38.2 °C Test Result Normal Values WBC 21.3 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Abdominal X-ray: Round 1.5 cm Radio opaque area at right iliac fossa. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step?

A 44-year-old woman is admitted electively for laparoscopic cholecystectomy for chronic cholelithiasis. She is obese, diabetic and sickle cell trait. Which of the following is most likely type of her gallstones?

A 65-year-old man presented to Emergency Room with history of vague upper abdominal pain and loss of appetite and weight. Examination revealed he was jaundiced; abdomen is soft and lax with palpable non-tender gallbladder. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 75-year-old man admitted to Coronary Care Unit with acute MI, complicated with bacterial pneumonia, patient on tazocin for 12 days. Examination revealed abdomen severe tenderness in the right upper quadrant (see report). Blood pressure 100/60 mmHg Heart rate 96 /min Respiratory rate 22 /min Temperature 38.3 °C Ultrasound abdomen: Distended gallbladder with thick wall and pericholecystic fluids, there is no gallstones. which of following is the most appropriate management?

A 35-year-old woman presented to Emergency Room with vague upper abdominal pain, She underwent elective cholecystectomy for cholelithiasis 7 days ago. Examination revealed she was jaundiced with mild right upper quadrant tenderness (see lab results and report). Temperature 37.1 °C Test Result Normal Values WBC 9.3 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Alkaline phosphatase 169 39-117 IU/L Alanine aminotransferase 35 5-40 IU/L Amylase 102 24-151 IU/L Direct bilirubin 10 1.5-6.5 mol/L Total bilirubin 17 3.5-16.5 mol/L Ultrasound: Minimal fluids in gall-bladder fossa, CBD measure 9 mm Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 70-year-old women p7+1 with chronic constipation presented to Emergency Room with abdominal distension and vomiting. Examination revealed abdomen is distended, tympanic, with round tender non-reducible swelling in the right groin below and lateral to pubic tubercle (see report). X-ray abdomen: Distended small bowel with multiple fluid levels. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 61-year-old man presented to Outpatient Clinic with general fatigue and mild on and off stool mixed with streaks of blood for 4 months. Examination revealed he was pale; abdomen is soft and lax, per rectal examination revealed 2nd degree piles (see lab result). Test Result Normal Values Hb 8.3 130-170 g/L (Male) 120-160 g/L (Female) Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 52-year-old woman presented to Emergency Room with abdominal distension and vomiting, she has history of ventral hernia repair with mesh 12 years back. Examination revealed distended abdomen, no tenderness, and no signs of hernia recurrence (see reports). X-ray abdomen: Dilated bowels with air/fluid levels. CT scan: Contrast cut off at terminal ilium with soft tissue mass and target sign. Which of the following is likely diagnosis?

A 12-year-old boy is brought to the Emergency Room 30 min after accidentally ingesting bleach; vital signs are stable with maintained airway. He was kept NPO, started on IV fluids and broad-spectrum antibiotics. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg Heart rate 76 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6 °C Oxygen saturation 95 % Which of the following is most appropriate next step?
option3: Water ingestion to dilute the bleach
option4: Insert NGT and drain gastric content

question_text: A 31-year-old man was involved in road traffic accident; examination revealed he is fully oriented GCS15/15 before reaching the hospital paramedic reported interval unresponsiveness. The patient suddenly lost consciousness and dilatation of left pupil was noted (see report). X-ray of the skull: Left temporal fracture. Which is the following most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old man presented with history of bleeding per rectum. Anoscopy examination revealed swelling at 3,7 o''clock position. A treatment with injection of sclerosing agents is planned. Which of the following is most indicated for sclerosing therapy?

A 5-year-old boy presents to Emergency Room because the parents noticed that there is only one testis in the scrotum. Examination reveals that one testis is in the scrotum while the other one is felt in the inguinal canal. Which the following is the most appropriate treatment?
A 6-year-old boy presents with a fracture of femur (mid shaft) with 30% of anterior angulation. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?
option2: Closed reduction and hip spica cast
correct_option: Closed reduction and hip spica cast

question_text: A 45-year-old man diagnosed as short bowel syndrome on total parenteral nutrition developed weakness, vertigo and convulsions. Which of the following is the most like diagnosis?

A 30-year-old woman 16-week pregnant presents with nausea, vomiting and right sided abdominal pain. The obstetrician excluded any pregnancy related cause for pain. Temperature 37.8 °C Which of the following is the most likely cause?

A 45-year-old man farmer presented with history of right side abdominal pain, vomiting and fever. Examination confirmed tenderness in right upper quadrant (see report). Temperature 37.6 °C CT abdomen: Calcified cyst 5X6 cm. Hydatid cyst (echinococcal) was confirmed. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 35-year-old man presents with history of dyspnea during sleep and swallowing discomfort for 4 months. He is a known case of asthma (see report). Chest X-ray: Midline mass compressing trachea. Which of the following is the most like diagnosis?
A 20-year-old woman presents with history of dysphagia that was more with liquid than solid. Which of the following is the most appropriate first investigation?

A 55-year-old woman complained of difficulty in breathing 6-hour post total thyroidectomy. Examination confirmed the presence of large neck swelling. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
option4: Open the wound sutures in the ward
correct_option: Open the wound sutures in the ward

question_text: A 20-year-old man presents to Emergency Department with renal colic. Which of the following is most diagnostic imaging?

A 4-year-old boy presented with history of pain above his right knee. Examination revealed there is swelling at distal femur no limitation of knee movement (see report). X-ray: 3x3 sclerotic bone lesion at distal femur. Which of the following is most likely diagnosis?

A 23-year-old military man presented to the clinic with history of bilateral groin swelling. Examination revealed there was bilateral inguinal hernia. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 50-year-old woman presented with history of right upper quadrant pain for the last 5 months associated with jaundice for 4 days. Examination revealed unwell patient, tender abdomen but no rebound. The patient started on I.V antibiotic (see report). Blood pressure 99/70 mmHg, Heart rate 100 /min, Temperature 38 °C Ultrasound: Gall bladder stones, dilated common bile duct with stone. Which is the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 64-year-old man is involved in a high-speed car crash. He is resuscitated initially in a small hospital with limited resources he has head injury. It was decided to transfer this patient to trauma center, which is 40 km away (see report). Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, Heart rate 100 /min Respiratory rate 18 /min Temperature 36.6 °C X-ray chest: Widened mediastinum with fractures left ribs 2-5 but no pneumothorax. Which is the following the most appropriate next step?
option4: Call the receiving hospital and speak to the surgeon on call
correct_option: Call the receiving hospital and speak to the surgeon on call

question_text: A 30-year-old man presented to the Surgical Clinic while taking medical history he want his perceptions of why he felt ill and the doctor wants more detail about his symptoms and medical problems. Which is the following the most appropriate response?
option1: Allow him to complete his thought
option2: Inform him to change his appointment with other doctor
option3: Tell him other patient are waiting outside and clinic is busy now
option4: Politely interrupt him and continue with close-ended question
correct_option: Allow him to complete his thought

question_text: A 22-year-old woman presented to Surgery Clinic wearing revealing clothes, she come up very close to the doctor asking personal question in sedative tone with a language that is different from attending nurse language. Which is the following is the best response by the doctor?
option4: Call for other nurse talk same language
correct_option: Call for other nurse talk same language

question_text: A 70-year-old man presented with history of skin lesion over the right cheek (see report). Skin biopsy: Malignant melanoma. Which is the following is best way to make the patient comply with medical advice?
option2: He believes you are professional and right
option4: Taking time to provide information and gain his trust
correct_option: Taking time to provide information and gain his trust

question_text: A 23-year-old woman presented to the Surgical Clinic with a 1 X 2 cm skin lesion in the left forearm since childhood. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?

A 43-year-old man presented to the Surgical Clinic complaining of a dark colored pigmented skin lesion in the upper thigh for few months. Examination revealed a 3 X 4 cm dark colored skin lesion at the anterior aspect of right thigh with irregular borders and it was elevated above the skin. Examination confirmed a localized tender swelling in the right lower abdominal quadrant with negative cough impulse; it persisted even with contraction of abdominal muscles. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old patient presented with a 6-month history of a dull aching abdominal swelling associated with weight loss. Examination confirmed a 20x20 cm firm, non-tender, intra-abdominal mass (see report). Abdominal CT scan: Lobulated soft tissue mass arising from retro-peritoneum, there was multiple hypodense liver lesions. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old presented with intermittent perianal pain associated with purulent discharge. Examination confirmed a low-lying perianal fistula with an external opening posteriorly close to the anal verge. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

Heart rate 84 /min Temperature 37.2 °C Test Result Normal Value WBC 8 4.5-10.5 x 109/L Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 14-year-old patient presented with a painless neck swelling. Examination confirmed a midline neck swelling below the hyoid bone that moves up and down with swallowing. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

Examination confirmed a patent airway, equal air entry on both sides of the chest, and distended neck veins. Blood pressure 88/45 mmHg Heart rate 110 /min Respiratory rate 30 /min Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 25-year-old was involved in a head-on motor vehicle accident. On examination, he was unresponsive, had distended neck veins, absent breath sound, and hyper-resonant percussion on the right side. His GCS score was 8. Which of the following is the most urgent initial management?

A 25-year-old man was brought to the Emergency Department after being involved in a motor vehicle accident. He opened his eyes spontaneously and responded appropriately to verbal commands. A young man presented with a chronic infection of both axillae. Examination confirmed the presence of multiple discharging sinuses. Which of the following is the most likely source?

A 58-year-old woman presented with a right thigh mass that measured 5x6 cm (see report). Core biopsy: High-grade sarcoma. Which of the following is most appropriate staging image?
Which of the following is the most appropriate biopsy for a limb sarcoma?

A 44-year-old man presented with a 6-month history of vague central abdominal pain. CT scan of abdomen: Presence of 20x10 cm retroperitoneal mass. No enlarged mesenteric or paraaortic lymph nodes noted. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

A 42-year-old woman presented with a right breast mass that has been present for 3 months and is not associated with other symptoms. A 40-year-old woman presented for her first screening mammography. She has no past medical history, and no family history of cancer (see reports). question_text: A 24-year-old man presented with neck pain for 4 months. Physical examination of the neck was normal (see lab results and report). A 38-year-old multiparous woman presented with a 3-year history of abdominal bulging. correct_option: Looking at the confluence of the taenia coli
